summary.zh: 公元前1600年前后，欧亚非诸多地区已形成王国、城邦与部族联盟并存的政治格局；中国进入早商时期，西亚以古巴比伦、赫梯与米坦尼等强国并立，埃及处于第二中间期末至新王国前夕，爱琴海的米诺斯与迈锡尼诸中心兴盛，南亚处于后哈拉帕与早期吠陀过渡阶段，美洲则以奥尔梅克前文化、安第斯早期复杂社会与区域酋邦为主。
summary.en: Around 1600 BCE, Afro-Eurasia was marked by a mosaic of kingdoms, city-states, and tribal confederacies; early Shang ruled in China, Western Asia saw powers such as Babylon, the Hittites, and Mitanni, Egypt stood at the end of the Second Intermediate Period on the eve of the New Kingdom, Minoan and Mycenaean centers flourished in the Aegean, South Asia was in the post-Harappan and early Vedic transition, and the Americas were characterized by pre-Olmec cultures, early Andean complex societies, and regional chiefdoms.

name.zh: 朝鲜北部青铜部族联盟
朝鲜半岛早期青铜文化

description.zh: 朝鲜半岛北部与辽东相关文化圈内存在分散的青铜时代部落政体。
description.en: Dispersed Bronze Age tribal polities existed in northern Korea and the wider Liaodong-linked cultural sphere.

ruler.en: Successor kings of Tang of Shang

governmentForm.zh: 以王室宗族为核心、通过盟属方国与直属邑共同统治的早期青铜时代王国
governmentForm.en: An early Bronze Age kingdom centered on the royal lineage, ruling through allied regional polities and directly controlled settlements

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈明显等级结构，由商王、王族、贵族与武士、祭司与卜人、工匠、平民农户以及附属部族与奴隶构成。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, consisting of the Shang king, royal kin, nobles and warriors, priests and diviners, artisans, common farming households, and subordinate tribal peoples and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 王族宗室、亲附贵族、军事首领与主持祭祀占卜的上层集团
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the royal clan, allied nobles, military leaders, and the upper elite who supervised ritual and divination

succession.zh: 王位主要在王族内部继承，兼具父子相承与兄终弟及等早期宗法特征，由宗族与贵族共认确认合法性。
succession.en: The throne was inherited within the royal house, combining father-to-son succession with fraternal transfer typical of early dynastic kinship systems, with legitimacy confirmed by clan and noble consensus.

structure.zh: 中央权力围绕商王展开，核心职能包括祭祀、军事征伐、田猎、贡赋征收和对附属方国的控制。地方由王畿直属邑、宗族封地及臣属方国共同组成。
structure.en: Central authority revolved around the Shang king, whose core functions included ritual, warfare, royal hunting, tribute collection, and control over subordinate regional polities. The realm consisted of directly governed royal settlements, lineage estates, and dependent allied states.

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、龟甲卜问、灾异解释与王命正当化
function.en: Conducted ancestor rites, turtle-shell divination, interpretation of omens, and legitimization of royal commands

function.zh: 组织王室武装、征调附属方国兵力、管理战俘与边地征伐
function.en: Organized royal forces, levied troops from subordinate polities, and managed captives and frontier campaigns

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、青铜原料、贝与手工业品，并储存分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, bronze raw materials, shells, and craft goods, and stored and redistributed them

function.zh: 监督青铜铸造、骨角器、陶器、玉器与王室车辆器具生产
function.en: Supervised bronze casting, bone and horn working, pottery, jade carving, and production of royal vehicles and equipment

name.en: Royal Domain Land and Labor Office

function.zh: 管理王畿耕地、劳役调配、沟洫营建与大型礼制工程
function.en: Managed royal lands, labor obligations, irrigation and ditch works, and large ritual construction projects

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以王畿为核心，周边分布宗族据点、军事驻地与臣服方国。地方首领通常由王族、贵族或归附酋长担任，向王室纳贡、供兵并参与祭祀秩序。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on the royal domain, surrounded by lineage strongholds, military posts, and subordinate regional states. Local leaders were typically royal kinsmen, nobles, or submitted chiefs who rendered tribute, supplied troops, and participated in the Shang ritual order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律尚未形成成文法典，主要依王命、宗族习惯、祭祀禁忌与军事纪律裁断。刑罚可能包括处死、奴役、没收与祭祀性惩罚。
legalSystem.en: Law had not yet developed into a formal written code; judgments relied mainly on royal command, clan custom, ritual taboos, and military discipline. Punishments likely included execution, enslavement, confiscation, and ritualized penalties.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础以贡纳、劳役和战利品为主，而非统一货币税。直属地区上缴谷物、牲畜、手工业品与劳力，方国则按关系远近纳贡并承担军事义务。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base rested on tribute, labor service, and war booty rather than uniform monetary taxation. Directly ruled areas delivered grain, livestock, craft goods, and labor, while regional states rendered tribute and military obligations according to their relationship to the court.

religion.zh: 商人奉行以祖先崇拜、自然神与上帝信仰相结合的王室宗教。王通过祭祀与占卜沟通祖先与神灵，确保农业、战争和王权的吉凶。
religion.en: Shang religion combined ancestor worship, reverence for natural powers, and belief in the high deity Di. The king communicated with ancestors and spirits through sacrifice and divination to secure favorable outcomes in agriculture, warfare, and kingship.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世意义上的系统哲学，核心观念是祖先护佑、天象与神意可通过占卜获知，以及王权与祭祀责任密不可分。
philosophy.en: There was not yet a systematic philosophy in the later sense; key ideas centered on ancestral protection, the knowability of divine will through divination, and the inseparability of kingship from ritual responsibility.

writingSystem.zh: 处于早期汉字形成阶段的原始商文字，主要用于符号记事、族徽、陶文与后期趋向成熟的卜辞传统之前身。
writingSystem.en: An early stage in the development of Chinese writing, expressed through proto-Shang signs used for recording, clan emblems, pottery marks, and forms ancestral to the more mature oracle-bone tradition of later Shang.

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括高度发展的青铜礼器铸造、夯土城址、宫殿与宗庙建筑、礼制化墓葬、玉器工艺以及以王权祭祀为中心的政治文化整合。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included advanced bronze ritual vessel casting, rammed-earth walled sites, palatial and temple construction, formalized elite burials, jade craftsmanship, and the political-cultural integration of society around royal ritual.

languageFamily.zh: 早期汉藏语系中的上古汉语前阶段
languageFamily.en: A pre-Old Chinese stage within the early Sino-Tibetan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以粟、黍、小麦等农业生产为基础，辅以畜牧、渔猎、手工业和对周边地区的贡纳控制。王室对高价值青铜、玉器和礼器生产有较强支配力，整体处于早期国家中相当繁荣的水平。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, especially millet, broomcorn millet, and wheat, supplemented by herding, fishing and hunting, craft production, and control over tribute from surrounding regions. The royal court exercised strong influence over high-value bronze, jade, and ritual production, making the kingdom relatively prosperous by early state standards.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍农业、畜牧业、青铜冶铸、制陶、骨角器加工、玉器制作、建筑与运输
mainIndustries.en: Millet agriculture, animal husbandry, bronze metallurgy and casting, pottery making, bone and horn working, jade crafting, construction, and transport

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、牲畜、青铜器、铜锡铅原料、玉石、海贝、陶器、骨器、皮革与织物
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, bronze goods, copper-tin-lead materials, jade, marine shells, pottery, bone tools, leather, and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为土地使用权、粮食储备、家畜、工具与家庭劳动力；贵族财富则集中于青铜礼器、战车、牲畜、玉器和附庸人口。
householdWealth.en: Common household wealth was primarily held in land-use access, grain stores, livestock, tools, and family labor; elite wealth was concentrated in bronze ritual vessels, chariots, herds, jade, and dependent populations.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业为基础、由王室再分配和地方贡赋支撑的宫廷—宗族经济，同时存在区域交换网络与礼品性贸易。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian economy supported by royal redistribution and local tribute, functioning as a court-lineage system alongside regional exchange networks and gift-oriented trade.

source.en: Royal Domain Agriculture and Granary Collection
Labor Service Valuation and Workshop Output

category.zh: 作坊、青铜铸造与工官体系
category.en: Workshops, Bronze Casting, and Craft Administration
Rewards to Nobles and Pacification of Dependents

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非单一金属储备，而是由王室仓廪中的粮食、牲畜、青铜原料、贝、玉器和礼器库存共同构成，主要用于灾年调剂、祭祀和军事动员。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a single metallic reserve but a composite of grain stores, livestock, bronze raw materials, shells, jade, and ritual inventory held in royal storage, used mainly for bad years, ritual obligations, and military mobilization.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重视储粮、集中高价值工艺品生产、通过战争与纳贡扩充资源，并以再分配维系王权与贵族联盟。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized grain storage, concentration of high-value craft production, expansion of resources through warfare and tribute, and maintenance of royal authority through redistribution to nobles and allies.

description.zh: 以持戈、矛、弓的步兵为主，是征伐与守卫城邑的骨干力量。
description.en: Infantry armed with dagger-axes, spears, and bows formed the backbone of campaigns and urban defense.

description.zh: 早期战车力量仍较有限，主要由王室与上层贵族掌握，用于指挥、冲击与威慑。
description.en: Early chariot forces were still limited and mainly controlled by the royal house and high nobility for command, shock, and prestige.

description.zh: 由附属方国和臣服部族提供，战时应召，熟悉边地作战与掠袭。
description.en: Supplied by subordinate polities and submitted tribes, these levies were called up in wartime and were effective in frontier fighting and raiding.

commanderInChief.zh: 商王
commanderInChief.en: The Shang King

technology.zh: 军队使用青铜兵器、复合弓前身、皮甲或简易护具、夯土城防及早期战车技术；作战方式兼具会战、围攻、突袭与俘获人口。
technology.en: The military used bronze weapons, early bow forms, leather armor or simple protection, rammed-earth fortifications, and early chariot technology; warfare combined field action, siege, raiding, and population capture.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自黄河中游周边尚未整合的方国、东方与南方部族群体，以及王权扩张过程中可能反复叛服的附庸势力。
threats.en: The chief threats came from not-yet-integrated regional powers around the middle Yellow River, eastern and southern tribal groups, and subordinate allies whose loyalty could shift during Shang expansion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于黄河中游及其支流流域的农业聚落、城邑和附属方国，整体呈农耕核心区与外围部族区并存格局。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in agricultural villages, walled towns, and subordinate regional centers in the middle Yellow River basin and its tributaries, forming a pattern of core farming zones and peripheral tribal regions.

name.en: Royal Domain Town Cluster of the Zhongmu Region

socialClasses.zh: 上层为商王与王族，中层为贵族、武士、祭司和高级工匠，下层为农户、普通工匠与附庸部众，最底层为战俘和奴隶。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the king and royal clan; the middle layers included nobles, warriors, priests, and high artisans; below them were peasants, ordinary craftsmen, and dependent peoples; the lowest stratum consisted of war captives and slaves.

allies.zh: 商的“盟友”主要是纳贡并参与军事行动的方国、宗族封地与归附部族，而非近代意义上的平等国家联盟。
allies.en: Shang 'allies' were mainly tributary regional states, lineage domains, and submitted tribes that joined military action, rather than equal sovereign allies in the modern sense.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是黄河中下游及南东方向拒绝臣服的方国与部族集团，也包括间歇性反叛的边缘附庸。
enemies.en: Its enemies were chiefly regional polities and tribal groups in the middle and lower Yellow River zones and toward the south and east that resisted submission, including periodically rebellious frontier dependents.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以征伐、威慑、纳贡整合和婚姻或宗族关系笼络并行，目标是扩大王室祭祀秩序与资源控制范围。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined conquest, intimidation, tributary incorporation, and the use of marriage or kinship ties to bind others, with the aim of expanding the royal ritual order and resource control.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括分铸法青铜器生产、夯土城墙、大型仓储与宫殿建筑、牛马牵引运输、精细玉器加工以及早期文字与制度化占卜实践。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included piece-mold bronze production, rammed-earth walls, large storage and palace construction, ox- and horse-drawn transport, fine jade working, and early writing together with institutionalized divination practices.

strengths.zh: 优势在于王权与宗教合一带来的强大动员能力、发达的青铜铸造与礼制文化、较高的农业生产力，以及通过贡纳和征伐整合周边资源的能力。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in the strong mobilization created by the union of kingship and religion, advanced bronze casting and ritual culture, relatively high agricultural productivity, and the ability to integrate surrounding resources through tribute and conquest.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于统治网络仍高度依赖宗族关系与军事威慑，地方控制不均衡，继承机制可能引发内部竞争，且对外扩张需要持续消耗人力与物资。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were that the ruling network still depended heavily on kinship ties and military intimidation, local control was uneven, succession could provoke internal rivalry, and external expansion required continuous expenditure of manpower and resources.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后，商处于上升阶段，若能维持王族 cohesion、控制关键资源产地并稳固方国体系，未来有望发展为东亚最强大的青铜时代王朝之一。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, Shang was in a rising phase; if it maintained royal cohesion, controlled key resource zones, and stabilized its network of regional states, it was well positioned to become one of the most powerful Bronze Age dynasties in East Asia.

description.zh: 黄河中游的早期青铜王朝，正扩张并整合周边方国。
description.en: An early Bronze Age dynasty in the middle Yellow River expanding and integrating surrounding polities.

governmentForm.zh: 由区域性部落联盟与村落酋邦构成的分散酋长制网络，缺乏统一国家机器
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of regional tribal alliances and village chiefdoms without a unified state apparatus

socialStructure.zh: 社会以定居村落为基础，由亲族群体、渔猎采集家庭、手工业者、祭祀人物与地方首领构成，整体分层有限但部分富裕聚落已出现地位差异
socialStructure.en: Society was based on settled villages composed of kin groups, hunter-fisher-gatherer households, craft specialists, ritual figures, and local leaders; overall stratification remained limited, though some prosperous communities showed emerging status differences

rulingClass.zh: 各地有威望的酋长家族、长老与祭祀领袖
rulingClass.en: Prestigious local chief lineages, elders, and ritual leaders

succession.zh: 多为家族内部继承并结合长老认可，部分地区可能按资历、声望与祭祀权威推举
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading families with elder approval; in some areas leadership may also have depended on seniority, prestige, and ritual authority

structure.zh: 绳文列岛并无统一中央政府，治理主要在聚落与区域层面运作。大型聚落通过首领、长老会议与祭祀主持者协调资源分配、婚姻联盟、祭仪、冲突调解与季节性劳作。不同地区之间依赖交换网络与礼仪联系，而非常设官僚体系。
structure.en: The Jomon archipelago had no unified central government; governance operated mainly at the village and regional level. Large settlements coordinated resource distribution, marriage alliances, ritual activity, dispute settlement, and seasonal labor through chiefs, councils of elders, and ritual specialists. Links between regions depended on exchange networks and ceremonial ties rather than a permanent bureaucracy.

function.zh: 由长老与首领主持，处理内部纠纷、婚姻安排、猎渔采集时序与村落协作
function.en: Led by elders and chiefs, handling internal disputes, marriage arrangements, hunting-fishing-gathering schedules, and village cooperation

function.zh: 主持祖灵、自然灵与季节性祭祀，管理祭场、土偶、墓葬礼仪与联盟性庆典
function.en: Oversaw ancestral and nature-spirit rituals, managed ritual spaces, figurines, funerary rites, and alliance ceremonies

function.zh: 管理栗、坚果、鱼干、贝类与其他储备物资，在歉收与冬季进行再分配
function.en: Managed stores of chestnuts, nuts, dried fish, shellfish, and other reserves, redistributing them in poor seasons and winter

function.zh: 组织黑曜石、玉石、沥青、漆器原料、海产与工艺品的区域交换与往来
function.en: Organized regional exchange of obsidian, jade, bitumen, lacquer materials, marine products, and crafted goods

function.zh: 负责聚落巡守、边界警戒、报信与冲突时的临时动员
function.en: Handled village watch, boundary vigilance, message relay, and temporary mobilization during conflicts

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落为核心，若干聚落可能围绕大型中心聚落形成区域网络。管理依靠家族首领、长老与祭祀者，没有成文地方官僚等级。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was village-centered, with some groups of settlements likely organized around larger central communities. Management relied on lineage heads, elders, and ritual specialists rather than a formal ranked local bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 无法典化法律，规范主要来自习惯法、亲族义务、仪礼禁忌与共同体协商。纠纷通常通过赔偿、调解、婚姻重组或社群排斥处理。
legalSystem.en: There was no codified law; norms derived chiefly from customary practice, kin obligations, ritual taboos, and communal negotiation. Disputes were usually settled through compensation, mediation, marriage rearrangement, or social exclusion.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制。首领对贡纳的控制主要表现为礼物交换、劳动义务、祭祀供献及共享储藏中的再分配权。
taxationSystem.en: No unified tax system existed. Elite control over tribute took the form of gift exchange, labor obligations, ritual offerings, and redistributive authority over shared stores.

religion.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜与自然祭祀为核心，重视山川、海洋、动物、树木与生育力量。土偶、石棒、环状墓地与祭祀空间反映出与生命、繁衍、治病及祖先相关的宗教观念。
religion.en: Religion centered on animism, ancestral reverence, and the ritual veneration of natural forces, with mountains, seas, animals, trees, and fertility holding major significance. Clay figurines, stone rods, circular cemeteries, and ceremonial spaces reflect beliefs tied to life, reproduction, healing, and ancestors.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学，世界观强调人与自然环境的循环互惠、群体共享、祖先延续与季节节律中的平衡。
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophy had formed, but the worldview emphasized reciprocal cycles between people and nature, communal sharing, ancestral continuity, and balance within seasonal rhythms.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，信息依靠口述传统、仪式记忆、工艺符号与聚落空间安排传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing system; information was transmitted through oral tradition, ritual memory, craft symbolism, and settlement spatial organization.

culturalAchievements.zh: 绳文文化以绳纹陶器、漆器技术、精致骨角器、磨制石器、贝冢聚落、定居性较强的渔猎采集生活方式以及广泛的黑曜石与玉石交换网络著称。中晚期绳文还出现大型聚落规划、木构设施和复杂墓葬仪式。
culturalAchievements.en: Jomon culture was distinguished by cord-marked pottery, lacquer technology, refined bone and antler tools, polished stone implements, shell-midden settlements, a notably sedentary hunter-fisher-gatherer lifeway, and extensive obsidian and jade exchange networks. Middle and Late Jomon also saw large planned settlements, wooden constructions, and increasingly elaborate funerary ritual.

languageFamily.zh: 未知；绳文诸群体可能使用多种已失传的列岛本地语言
languageFamily.en: Unknown; Jomon groups likely spoke multiple now-lost indigenous languages of the archipelago

unit.en: kg grain-equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以渔猎采集为主，并辅以栗树管理、坚果加工、小规模园圃栽培、漆树利用与手工业生产。由于没有货币市场与国家税收体系，经济总量仅能以食物和手工业产出当量粗略估算。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based primarily on fishing, hunting, and gathering, supplemented by chestnut management, nut processing, small-scale horticulture, lacquer use, and craft production. Because there was no monetary market or state tax regime, total output can only be estimated roughly in food and craft equivalents.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、贝类采集、鹿与野猪狩猎、坚果与栗子加工、陶器制作、骨角器加工、石器制造、漆器与编织品制作
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, shellfish gathering, deer and boar hunting, nut and chestnut processing, pottery making, bone and antler working, stone tool production, and lacquerware and woven-goods production

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、翡翠与玉石、沥青、盐渍与干制海产、贝壳、漆、优质陶器、骨角器、兽皮
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, jadeite and other jades, bitumen, salted and dried marine products, shells, lacquer, fine pottery, bone and antler tools, and hides

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为住房质量、储藏坑中的坚果与鱼干、渔猎工具、饰物、优质陶器、漆器和交换得来的稀有石材。部分大型聚落首领家庭拥有更显著的剩余储备与礼仪物品。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in housing quality, stored nuts and dried fish, hunting and fishing gear, ornaments, fine pottery, lacquerware, and exchanged rare stones. In some large settlements, chiefly households held more visible surpluses and prestige goods.

unit.en: kg grain-equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、亲族共享、季节性采集与区域交换为基础的再分配经济；首领通过宴飨、礼物与储藏管理积累声望，而非通过市场税收控制财富。
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy based on household production, kin sharing, seasonal procurement, and regional exchange; chiefs accumulated prestige through feasting, gift-giving, and storage management rather than through market taxation.

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库。所谓“财政储备”主要是大型聚落掌控的食物储藏、珍贵石材、仪礼器物与可再分配的手工业品。
treasuryDescription.en: No unified state treasury existed. What can be called a fiscal reserve consisted mainly of food stores, valuable stone materials, ritual objects, and redistributable crafted goods controlled by major settlements.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践以确保越冬、生计稳定、举行联盟性祭礼和维持首领威望为目标，强调储藏与再分配而非积累货币财富。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to secure winter survival, subsistence stability, alliance rituals, and chiefly prestige, emphasizing storage and redistribution rather than the accumulation of monetary wealth.

description.zh: 由大型聚落中常年承担巡守、瞭望和报信任务的成年男子组成，并非职业军队
description.en: Composed of adult men in larger settlements who regularly handled watch, lookout, and message duties; not a professional army

description.zh: 在资源竞争、报复冲突或防御外来威胁时，由各村落临时集结的战斗人员，装备弓箭、石斧、木矛与棍棒
description.en: Fighters temporarily assembled from villages during resource disputes, revenge conflicts, or defense against outside threats, armed with bows, stone axes, wooden spears, and clubs

description.zh: 沿海与岛屿地区利用独木舟和近岸航行能力执行警戒、联络和小规模袭扰
description.en: In coastal and island zones, canoe-using groups carried out watch, communication, and small-scale raiding

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；战时由地方酋长或资深战首领指挥
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; in wartime leadership fell to local chiefs or senior war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以弓箭、磨制与打制石器、骨角矛头、木盾和简易防护为主，未普遍采用青铜武器。防御依靠地形、壕沟、栅栏和聚落协同。
technology.en: Military technology relied chiefly on bows and arrows, polished and chipped stone weapons, bone and antler spear points, wooden shields, and simple protection, without widespread use of bronze arms. Defense depended on terrain, ditches, palisades, and settlement cooperation.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自列岛内部的资源竞争、局部复仇冲突与区域性对抗，而非大型外来征服。朝鲜半岛与中国大陆的文化影响此时更多表现为间接接触，而非直接军事压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from internal competition over resources, local revenge conflicts, and regional confrontations rather than large-scale foreign conquest. Influences from Korea and the Chinese mainland at this time were more indirect cultural contacts than direct military pressure.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于本州、北海道、四国和九州部分地区，以海岸、河口、湖岸和平原边缘的定居或半定居聚落为主。东北与东日本人口和聚落密度通常高于部分西南地区。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across Honshu, Hokkaido, Shikoku, and parts of Kyushu, concentrated in sedentary or semi-sedentary communities along coasts, estuaries, lakeshores, and lowland margins. Northeastern and eastern Japan generally had higher settlement density than some southwestern areas.

name.zh: 三内丸山大型聚落传统区
北陆玉石交换中心聚落
北海道南部海岸聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为普通家庭、熟练渔猎采集者、工艺专门者、祭祀人员、长老与酋长家族。虽无严格阶级制度，但墓葬、居址与随葬品显示部分地区已有地位分化。
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into ordinary households, skilled hunters-fishers-gatherers, craft specialists, ritual personnel, elders, and chiefly families. Although there was no rigid class system, burials, residences, and grave goods show status differentiation in some regions.

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友；外交主要表现为列岛内部区域联盟、婚姻联系与交换伙伴关系，部分北部和西部群体可能与朝鲜半岛沿海社群保持间接往来
allies.en: There were no unified foreign allies; diplomacy took the form of internal archipelagic alliances, marriage ties, and exchange partnerships, while some northern and western groups may have had indirect contacts with coastal communities of the Korean Peninsula

enemies.zh: 无统一外敌；敌对多局限于相邻聚落或区域集团之间围绕渔场、猎场、石材来源与声望的冲突
enemies.en: There was no single external enemy; hostility was mostly limited to neighboring settlements or regional groups competing over fishing grounds, hunting territories, stone sources, and prestige

foreignPolicy.zh: 总体上重视维持交换路线、婚姻网络和仪礼联系，以减少暴力冲突并保障稀有资源流通。对外关系以接触和吸收为主，而非扩张征服。
foreignPolicy.en: Overall policy emphasized maintaining exchange routes, marriage networks, and ceremonial ties in order to reduce violence and secure the movement of rare resources. External relations were characterized more by contact and selective adoption than by expansion or conquest.

era.zh: 绳文晚期，东亚青铜时代周缘
era.en: Late Jomon, on the periphery of the East Asian Bronze Age

keyInnovations.zh: 绳纹陶与深钵技术、漆器加工、磨制石器与精细骨角器、栗树与坚果资源管理、贝冢与储藏设施、独木舟航行、远距离黑曜石与玉石交换网络
keyInnovations.en: Cord-marked pottery and deep-vessel technology, lacquer processing, polished stone tools and fine bone-antler implements, chestnut and nut resource management, shell middens and storage facilities, dugout canoe travel, and long-distance obsidian and jade exchange networks

strengths.zh: 绳文社会具有很强的环境适应力，能够在海洋、森林与河湖资源之间进行多样化生计组合；聚落定居程度高，手工业传统深厚，区域交换网络稳固，社会整体在1600 BCE前后保持稳定。
strengths.en: Jomon society had strong environmental adaptability, combining marine, forest, river, and lake resources into a diversified subsistence base; settlement was relatively sedentary, craft traditions were deep, regional exchange networks were resilient, and overall society remained stable around 1600 BCE.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、文字、正式官僚与大规模农业基础，使其难以集中劳力、形成持久军力或快速应对广域气候与外来技术冲击。区域差异明显，政治整合能力有限。
weaknesses.en: The absence of unified rule, writing, a formal bureaucracy, and a large agricultural base limited the ability to concentrate labor, sustain military power, or respond rapidly to broad climatic or external technological shocks. Regional variation was strong and political integration remained limited.

outlook.zh: 短期内绳文列岛仍将维持区域酋邦与交换网络并存的稳定格局；但随着朝鲜半岛与中国大陆农业、金属器与新型社会组织逐步向列岛传播，未来数世纪其部分地区将面临深刻转型压力。
outlook.en: In the short term, the archipelago was likely to preserve a stable pattern of regional chiefdoms coexisting with exchange networks; however, as agriculture, metallurgy, and new forms of social organization spread gradually from Korea and the Chinese mainland, parts of the islands would face profound transformation in the coming centuries.

description.zh: 日本列岛仍以绳文聚落网络和区域酋邦为主，尚未形成国家。
description.en: The Japanese archipelago remained dominated by Jomon settlement networks and regional chiefdoms rather than states.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权、祭祀中心与地方聚落首领共同运作的早期青铜时代区域王国
governmentForm.en: An early Bronze Age regional kingdom combining royal authority, cult centers, and local settlement chiefs

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈明显等级化，上层为王室、祭司贵族与掌握青铜、玉石和礼器生产的精英，下层为聚落首领、手工业者、农民、渔猎者与劳役人群。
socialStructure.en: Society was clearly stratified, with the royal house, priestly nobles, and elites controlling bronze, jade, and ritual production at the top, followed by settlement chiefs, artisans, farmers, fishers-hunters, and laboring populations.

rulingClass.zh: 王室与祭司贵族集团
rulingClass.en: The royal house and priestly aristocratic elite

succession.zh: 大体为王族世袭，可能以父系继承为主，但需获得宗教权威与核心贵族支持。
succession.en: Succession was broadly hereditary within the royal line, probably mainly patrilineal, but required support from religious authorities and core nobles.

structure.zh: 中央权力集中于王及其宗教—政治核心，三星堆大型城址与祭祀区既是行政中枢，也是礼仪与财富再分配中心；外围由若干聚落与地方首领服从王权网络。
structure.en: Central authority was concentrated in the king and his politico-religious core. The large Sanxingdui walled center and ritual precinct served both as the administrative hub and as a center for ceremony and redistribution, while surrounding settlements and local chiefs were tied into the royal network.

function.zh: 管理王室居所、仓储、贡品接收、宴飨与宫廷劳役调配。
function.en: Managed royal residences, storage, receipt of tribute, feasting, and palace labor organization.

function.zh: 主持大型祭祀、祖先与神灵崇拜、祭器使用与祭祀坑埋藏仪式。
function.en: Oversaw major rituals, ancestor and deity worship, ritual vessel use, and the ceremonial burial of offerings in sacrificial pits.

function.zh: 征集原料，组织范铸、冶炼、工匠劳作，并监督大型人像、面具、神树与礼器生产。
function.en: Collected raw materials, organized mold-casting and smelting, coordinated artisans, and supervised production of large figures, masks, sacred trees, and ritual bronzes.

name.en: Jade, Stone, and Precious Materials Office

function.zh: 管理玉器、绿松石、象牙等珍贵材料的征收、加工与分配。
function.en: Managed the acquisition, processing, and distribution of jade, turquoise, ivory, and other valued materials.

function.zh: 统计耕地、征收谷物、储备粮食，并调配农业劳役与水利维护。
function.en: Recorded fields, collected grain, maintained food reserves, and organized agricultural labor and water management.

function.zh: 维系外围聚落、山地资源区与地方首领关系，催征贡赋与役使。
function.en: Maintained ties with outer settlements, upland resource zones, and local chiefs, and enforced tribute and labor obligations.

function.zh: 负责王城警卫、城墙与壕沟防御、武器储备以及战时动员。
function.en: Handled palace guards, defenses of walls and moats, weapon reserves, and wartime mobilization.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政并非后世官僚制，而是通过中心城址、附属聚落、宗族网络和地方首领共同实施。王权依靠贡赋、婚盟、祭祀号令与手工业控制来整合四川盆地内的关键区域。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was not a later-style bureaucracy but operated through the central center, subordinate settlements, kin networks, and local chiefs. Royal authority integrated key parts of the Sichuan Basin through tribute, marriage alliances, ritual command, and control of prestige craft production.

legalSystem.zh: 尚无成文法典证据，秩序主要依靠王命、宗族惯例、祭祀禁忌与首领裁决维持；对违逆贡赋、冒犯神圣器物或扰乱劳役者，可能施以惩罚、剥夺地位或强制劳作。
legalSystem.en: There is no evidence for a formal written law code. Order was maintained through royal commands, kin custom, ritual taboos, and the judgments of chiefs; those who defied tribute obligations, violated sacred objects, or disrupted labor duties were likely punished, demoted, or compelled into labor.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物和劳役为主，包括谷物、家畜、渔猎土产、铜矿与其他珍材，以及季节性筑城、耕作、运输和祭祀服务。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind and labor, including grain, livestock, fishing and hunting products, copper ore and other valued materials, plus seasonal service for construction, farming, transport, and ritual activities.

religion.zh: 宗教以祖先崇拜、自然神灵崇拜和王权神圣化为核心，祭祀活动规模巨大，青铜面具、立人像、神树和祭祀坑显示其独特而高度仪式化的信仰体系。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, reverence for natural and supernatural powers, and the sacralization of kingship. Large-scale rituals and the use of bronze masks, standing figures, sacred trees, and sacrificial pits reveal a distinctive and highly ceremonial belief system.

philosophy.zh: 尚未见成体系哲学传统，思想观念主要体现为宇宙—祖先—王权相连的祭祀世界观，重视天象、土地丰饶、祖灵庇佑与神圣秩序。
philosophy.en: There is no evidence of a formal philosophical tradition; ideas were expressed mainly through a ritual worldview linking cosmos, ancestors, and kingship, with emphasis on celestial signs, fertility of the land, ancestral protection, and sacred order.

writingSystem.zh: 目前无确证成熟文字系统。可能存在记号、刻划符号或工匠标记，但国家运作主要依靠口传、仪式程序与实物管理。
writingSystem.en: No confirmed mature writing system is known. Symbols, incisions, or workshop marks may have existed, but state operations relied primarily on oral transmission, ritual procedure, and control of physical goods.

culturalAchievements.zh: 三星堆最杰出的文化成就在于超大型青铜人像与面具、复杂铸造技术、独特宗教艺术风格、玉石加工和大型礼仪性城市建设，显示古蜀文明在中国西南地区形成了高度独立而辉煌的青铜文化中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Sanxingdui’s greatest cultural achievements lay in its colossal bronze figures and masks, advanced casting techniques, distinctive religious art style, jade working, and large ritual-urban construction, demonstrating that Ancient Shu developed an independent and brilliant Bronze Age cultural center in southwest China.

languageFamily.zh: 语言未有定论，可能属于古代四川盆地某已消失的汉藏语系相关支系，亦可能包含多族群成分。
languageFamily.en: The language remains uncertain; it may have belonged to an extinct branch related to the Sino-Tibetan sphere in the ancient Sichuan Basin, possibly with multi-ethnic elements.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业剩余、王室再分配和高价值礼器生产为核心。四川盆地土地肥沃、河流密布，能够支撑较高人口密度与手工业专业化；经济实力更多体现于动员劳力与珍贵物资的能力，而非货币化市场规模。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on agricultural surplus, royal redistribution, and high-value ritual craft production. The fertile Sichuan Basin and dense river systems supported relatively high population density and craft specialization; economic strength was expressed more in the ability to mobilize labor and precious materials than in monetized market exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟、稻等农业生产，渔猎采集，青铜冶铸，玉石加工，陶器制作，木作与建筑，象牙与珍材礼器加工
mainIndustries.en: Millet and rice agriculture, fishing and hunting, bronze metallurgy, jade working, pottery production, woodworking and construction, and ivory and prestige-goods crafting

tradeGoods.zh: 青铜器、玉器、象牙制品、漆木器、陶器、盐、兽皮、羽饰、铜料与地方土产；外来交换品可能包括铜矿来源、海贝及其他稀有珍材。
tradeGoods.en: Bronzes, jades, ivory goods, lacquered wood items, pottery, salt, hides, feather ornaments, copper materials, and regional products; incoming exchange goods may have included copper sources, shells, and other rare prestige materials.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要表现为粮食储备、房屋、农具、家畜和宗族关系；贵族财富则集中于青铜礼器、玉器、象牙、服饰与可支配劳力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of grain stores, houses, tools, livestock, and kin ties, while elite wealth was concentrated in ritual bronzes, jades, ivory, garments, and command over labor.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业基础上的宫廷—祭祀再分配经济为主，辅以地方交换网络和有限的远距珍材贸易；手工业核心受王权和精英控制。
economicSystem.en: A palace-ritual redistributive economy based on agriculture, supplemented by regional exchange networks and limited long-distance trade in prestige materials; core craft production was controlled by the crown and elites.

source.zh: 手工业与礼器控制收益

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是金银现金，而是谷物储备、青铜原料、成品礼器、玉石、象牙及其他可供祭祀、馈赠与再分配的物资。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not mainly cash in gold or silver, but stores of grain, bronze raw materials, finished ritual goods, jade, ivory, and other assets suitable for ceremony, gifting, and redistribution.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在于维持祭祀正统、确保粮食储备、支持大型工坊和维系地方首领忠诚。出现丰年时积蓄仓储，灾年则依靠再分配稳定核心地区。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining ritual legitimacy, securing food reserves, supporting major workshops, and preserving the loyalty of local chiefs. In good harvest years stores were accumulated; in poor years redistribution helped stabilize the core region.

description.zh: 负责宫廷、祭祀区、城门和仓储设施警戒，是最精锐且最常备的武装力量。
description.en: Responsible for guarding the court, ritual precincts, city gates, and granaries; this was the most elite and permanent armed force.

description.zh: 以地方征召农民和附属聚落战士为主，装备木矛、戈、石骨箭镞与皮革或木制防具。
description.en: Drawn mainly from levied farmers and warriors of subordinate settlements, armed with wooden spears, ge-style pole weapons, stone or bone arrowheads, and leather or wooden protection.

description.zh: 利用蜀地山林环境，擅长远射、伏击和山地追击，平时亦承担狩猎与巡逻任务。
description.en: Adapted to the wooded and upland environment of Shu, they specialized in ranged fire, ambush, and pursuit in rough terrain, while also serving in hunting and patrol duties.

description.zh: 负责战时补充兵员、搬运粮草、修筑工事和维持后勤线。
description.en: Provided wartime reinforcements, carried supplies, built fieldworks, and maintained logistics.

commanderInChief.zh: 蜀王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The King of Shu as supreme commander

technology.zh: 军事技术处于早期青铜时代水平，拥有青铜兵器与工具，但规模化战争组织不如中原商文化成熟；更擅长城防、地方控制和复杂地形中的小规模作战。
technology.en: Military technology was at an early Bronze Age level, with bronze weapons and tools available, but large-scale war organization was less developed than in the Central Plains Shang sphere; Shu was better suited for defense, regional control, and fighting in complex terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自四川盆地边缘山地族群、地方聚落离心倾向，以及与长江上游和关中方向文化网络竞争时的资源压力；暂未见与商王朝发生持续大规模战争的明确证据。
threats.en: The main threats came from upland groups on the edges of the Sichuan Basin, centrifugal tendencies among local settlements, and resource pressures tied to competition with networks toward the upper Yangtze and Guanzhong; there is no clear evidence of sustained large-scale warfare with the Shang at this time.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于成都平原及其周边河谷和聚落群，三星堆为最大政治—礼仪中心，外围分布众多农业、手工业与资源采集型社区。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Chengdu Plain and surrounding river valleys and settlement clusters. Sanxingdui was the largest political-ritual center, with many agricultural, craft, and resource-extracting communities in the surrounding zone.

name.zh: 成都平原次级中心聚落

socialClasses.zh: 王室与祭司贵族、地方首领、工匠、农民、渔猎者、劳役人口与可能存在的依附者构成主要社会层次。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata consisted of the royal house and priestly nobles, local chiefs, artisans, farmers, fishers-hunters, laborers, and possible dependent populations.

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的国家盟友；更常见的是对四川盆地内外围聚落、资源区首领和相关文化群体建立朝贡、婚盟与仪式性从属关系。
allies.en: There were no fixed allies in the later interstate sense; more typical were tributary, marital, and ritual subordinate ties with settlement leaders and resource-zone chiefs in and around the Sichuan Basin.

enemies.zh: 无明确长期宿敌；潜在敌对对象包括不服属的盆地边缘部族、争夺资源通道的邻近集团，以及偶发冲突中的地方首领。
enemies.en: There was no clearly defined long-term archenemy; potential hostile forces included unsubdued groups on the basin margins, neighboring groups competing for resource routes, and local chiefs involved in intermittent conflict.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在控制盆地内部与周边资源网络，通过礼物交换、婚盟、宗教威望和工艺品流通扩大影响，同时谨慎维持与更远方文化圈的间接联系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling internal and nearby resource networks of the basin, extending influence through gift exchange, marriage alliances, religious prestige, and circulation of crafted goods, while cautiously maintaining indirect links with more distant cultural spheres.

keyInnovations.zh: 大型分铸与范铸青铜技术、复杂礼器制作、玉石精加工、夯土城墙与壕沟营建、区域性仓储与手工业组织、适应成都平原环境的农业生产体系。
keyInnovations.en: Large-scale sectional and mold-cast bronze technology, complex prestige-object production, fine jade working, rammed-earth walls and moat construction, regional storage and workshop organization, and agricultural systems adapted to the Chengdu Plain environment.

strengths.zh: 古蜀拥有肥沃盆地、较高人口承载力、独特而强大的宗教象征体系、发达青铜工艺和能整合地方资源的中心王权，因此在中国西南地区极具凝聚力与文化影响力。
strengths.en: Ancient Shu possessed a fertile basin, relatively strong demographic capacity, a distinctive and powerful religious-symbolic system, advanced bronze craftsmanship, and a central kingship able to integrate regional resources, giving it strong cohesion and cultural influence in southwest China.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合仍依赖个人王权、祭祀威望和地方首领合作，缺乏成熟文字与制度化官僚体系；一旦发生洪水、资源中断、内部权力重组或祭祀中心转移，国家稳定性可能受损。
weaknesses.en: Political integration still depended on personal kingship, ritual prestige, and cooperation from local chiefs, without a mature writing system or institutionalized bureaucracy; floods, resource disruption, internal power shifts, or relocation of cult centers could therefore undermine stability.

outlook.zh: 至公元前1600年前后，三星堆—古蜀文明处于繁荣高峰，短期内仍可能继续扩展其礼仪与工艺影响力；但中长期前景取决于环境变化、区域权力重组及祭祀中心能否持续维持其权威。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, the Sanxingdui–Ancient Shu Civilization stood near a flourishing peak and was likely to continue expanding its ritual and artistic influence in the short term; over the longer term, its future depended on environmental change, regional political realignment, and the continued authority of its ceremonial center.

description.zh: 四川盆地的区域性青铜王国，以独特礼器与宗教中心著称。
description.en: A regional Bronze Age kingdom in the Sichuan Basin known for distinctive ritual bronzes and cult centers.

name.zh: 华南新石器—青铜过渡诸文化

governmentForm.zh: 由众多村落联盟、部落共同体与沿海首领网络组成的分散酋邦体制
governmentForm.en: A decentralized chieftain system composed of village alliances, tribal communities, and coastal chiefly networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、村社与部落联盟为基础，存在酋长家族、祭仪权威、自由农民、渔民、手工业者与从属群体的分层，但整体仍偏向地方性与亲族性组织
socialStructure.en: Society was based on clans, village communities, and tribal alliances, with stratification among chiefly families, ritual authorities, free farmers, fishers, craft specialists, and dependent groups, though organization remained strongly local and kin-based

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、部落长老与主持祭祀的宗教权威
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly lineages, tribal elders, and ritual authorities who oversaw ceremonies

succession.zh: 多以家族世袭为主，但需获得长老、宗族与盟友部落认可；部分地区酋长地位可因婚姻、武功或祭祀声望而转移
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within leading lineages, but required recognition from elders, kin groups, and allied tribes; in some areas chiefly status could shift through marriage, military success, or ritual prestige

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构，治理依靠村落首领、部落议事会、长老会议与区域性盟约。较大的聚落可支配周边小村，并在战争、祭祀、治水与贸易时临时集结劳力
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; governance relied on village headmen, tribal councils, councils of elders, and regional alliances. Larger settlements could influence nearby hamlets and temporarily mobilize labor for warfare, ritual, water management, and trade

function.zh: 主持祖灵、自然神与地方神灵祭祀，协调季节性农事、葬礼与联盟仪式
function.en: Oversaw rites to ancestors, nature powers, and local deities, and coordinated seasonal agriculture, funerals, and alliance ceremonies

function.zh: 组织稻作、山地垦殖、沟渠维护、堤埂修筑与储粮管理
function.en: Organized rice farming, upland clearing, ditch maintenance, embankment building, and grain storage

name.zh: 手工业与青铜作坊事务

function.zh: 管理陶器、石器、木器、纺织、玉石加工以及少量青铜铸造与原料分配
function.en: Managed pottery, stone tools, woodworking, textiles, jade and stone working, and limited bronze casting and material allocation

function.zh: 维护河口、渡口、海湾交换点，协调盐、贝、玉石、铜料与陶器流通
function.en: Maintained estuary, ferry, and bay exchange points and coordinated the circulation of salt, shell valuables, jade, copper materials, and pottery

function.zh: 召集战士、修筑环壕与木栅、组织巡逻并处理部落冲突
function.en: Summoned warriors, maintained ditches and palisades, organized patrols, and handled intertribal conflict

function.zh: 记录并分配谷物、鱼盐、布匹、劳役与节庆贡品
function.en: Recorded and allocated grain, fish and salt, cloth, labor services, and ceremonial tribute

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村寨为核心。若干血缘相近或通婚密切的聚落构成部落；若干部落因贸易、婚盟或防务形成松散联盟。沿海地区与珠江、韩江、西江、闽江等流域的中心聚落地位较高，但并无常设郡县制度
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on villages and fortified hamlets. Settlements linked by kinship or marriage formed tribes; several tribes could create loose alliances for trade, marriage, or defense. Central settlements in coastal zones and the Pearl, Han, Xi, and Min river systems held higher status, but there was no permanent territorial county system

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典，主要依赖习惯法、长老裁决、盟誓、赔偿与宗教禁忌。对盗窃、婚姻纠纷、土地边界、伤害与渔猎资源争夺，多以赔礼、实物赔偿、宴饮和解或驱逐处理
legalSystem.en: There was no written law code; order rested on customary law, judgments by elders, oaths, compensation, and ritual taboos. Theft, marriage disputes, land boundaries, injury, and conflicts over fishing or hunting resources were usually resolved by apology, payment in goods, reconciliation feasts, or expulsion

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制。酋长主要通过贡纳、礼物交换、节庆献礼、战利品分配与季节性劳役获取资源；较大聚落可能定期征收谷物、鱼干、盐、布、木材与贝饰
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system. Chiefs obtained resources mainly through tribute, gift exchange, festival offerings, distributions of war booty, and seasonal labor obligations; larger settlements could regularly collect grain, dried fish, salt, cloth, timber, and shell ornaments

religion.zh: 信仰以祖先崇拜、自然崇拜与地方神灵崇拜为核心，常见山川、蛇龙、鸟、太阳、水域与丰产仪式。葬俗、玉石与陶礼器体现对祖灵与地方权威的重视
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, nature worship, and local cults, with common reverence for mountains, rivers, serpent-dragon and bird symbols, the sun, waters, and fertility rites. Burial customs and the use of jade, stone, and ceramic ritual objects reflected concern with ancestors and local authority

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学，世界观主要体现为对祖先、土地、季节循环、婚姻盟约与人与自然协调关系的实践性观念
philosophy.en: No formal philosophy had emerged; the worldview was expressed instead through practical ideas concerning ancestors, land, seasonal cycles, marriage alliances, and harmony between humans and the natural world

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，信息传递依靠口述传统、结绳记事、器物符号、陶器刻划记号与仪式记忆
writingSystem.en: There was no mature writing system; information was transmitted through oral tradition, knotted cords, object symbols, incised pottery marks, and ritual memory

culturalAchievements.zh: 在稻作扩展、干栏或高地聚落营建、印纹硬陶与彩陶制作、玉石与贝饰加工、独木舟与近海航行、木构建筑以及区域性礼仪网络方面成就显著，体现出华南从新石器时代向青铜时代过渡的多样性
culturalAchievements.en: These cultures achieved notable development in the spread of rice agriculture, stilted or upland settlement construction, stamped hard pottery and painted ceramics, jade and shell ornament production, dugout canoe use and near-coastal navigation, timber architecture, and regional ritual networks, reflecting the diversity of South China's transition from the Neolithic to the Bronze Age

languageFamily.zh: 语言高度多样，可能包含早期南亚语、南岛语、侗台语前身及其他已消失的地方语言群
languageFamily.en: Languages were highly diverse and may have included early Austroasiatic, pre-Austronesian, pre-Kra-Dai, and other now-lost local language groups

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农业为主，辅以渔猎、采集、盐业、手工业与地区交换。人均产出低于成熟青铜国家，但沿海与河谷社区拥有较强的区域流通能力
gdpDescription.en: The economy was primarily based on subsistence agriculture, supplemented by fishing, hunting, gathering, salt production, craft work, and regional exchange. Output per person was lower than that of mature Bronze Age states, but coastal and riverine communities had substantial regional circulation capacity

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、粟黍杂粮、渔业、贝类采集、盐业、陶器制造、木器、纺织、玉石与石器加工、少量青铜冶铸
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, millet and mixed grains, fishing, shellfish gathering, salt production, pottery making, woodworking, textile production, jade and stone working, and limited bronze metallurgy

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、鱼干、贝壳、贝饰、玉石、石料、陶器、木材、漆料、羽毛、兽皮、竹木器、铜矿与少量青铜器
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, dried fish, shells, shell ornaments, jade, stone materials, pottery, timber, lacquer materials, feathers, hides, bamboo and wooden goods, copper ore, and small numbers of bronze objects

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、稻谷储备、渔具舟船、家畜、陶器、玉石或贝饰以及婚姻联盟关系上；精英墓葬与普通家庭之间已有明显差距
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through access to farmland, rice stores, fishing gear and boats, livestock, pottery, jade or shell ornaments, and marriage alliances; a visible gap already existed between elite burials and ordinary households

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给生产为基础，结合礼物交换、贡纳再分配、婚盟交换与有限区域贸易的部落再分配经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal redistributive economy based on village self-sufficiency, combined with gift exchange, tribute redistribution, marriage exchange, and limited regional trade

source.zh: 礼物、婚盟献纳与节庆贡品
source.en: Gifts, marriage-alliance offerings, and festival tribute

source.zh: 贸易中转与手工业征收

category.en: Craft specialists, workshops, and exchange networks

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，财富分散储存在各区域中心聚落的粮仓、盐仓、酋长家屋、祭祀场所与工匠作坊中。储备多为谷物、盐、贝、玉石、布匹与青铜原料，而非铸币金属
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single state treasury; wealth was stored across regional central settlements in granaries, salt stores, chiefly residences, ritual sites, and craft workshops. Reserves consisted mainly of grain, salt, shells, jade, cloth, and bronze materials rather than coined metal

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持储粮、平衡节庆再分配、在歉收时进行赈济，并通过宴饮和礼物巩固首领权威与跨部落联盟
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining grain reserves, balancing ceremonial redistribution, offering relief in poor harvest years, and reinforcing chiefly authority and intertribal alliances through feasting and gift distribution

description.zh: 由各村寨成年男子组成，使用木矛、石斧、骨器、弓箭与少量青铜兵器，适合伏击、寨战与山地作战
description.en: Drawn from adult males of village communities, armed with wooden spears, stone axes, bone tools, bows, and a small number of bronze weapons; suited to ambushes, settlement fighting, and upland warfare

description.zh: 较常备化的核心武装，负责护卫首领、执行突袭、保卫仓储与主持联盟战争
description.en: A more permanent armed core responsible for guarding chiefs, conducting raids, defending storage sites, and leading alliance warfare

description.zh: 擅长林地、河岸与湿地环境中的远程骚扰，通常与步战队伍混合部署
description.en: Skilled in ranged harassment in forests, riverbanks, and wetlands, usually deployed alongside infantry forces

name.en: Boat crews and coastal patrol forces

description.zh: 以独木舟和小型木船执行河口巡逻、运输、近岸袭扰与渔盐点防卫
description.en: Used dugout canoes and small wooden craft for estuary patrols, transport, near-shore raiding, and defense of fishing and salt sites

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；战时由最强势联盟酋长或资深战首领指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; in wartime the strongest alliance chief or a senior war leader took command

technology.zh: 军事技术仍以晚期新石器传统为主，采用木栅、环壕、盾牌、弓箭、石兵器和少量青铜刃器；机动依赖步行与舟船，尚无战车体系
technology.en: Military technology remained largely rooted in Late Neolithic traditions, using palisades, ditches, shields, bows, stone weapons, and limited bronze blades; mobility depended on foot movement and boats, with no chariot system

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部落间资源争夺、沿海与河谷控制权竞争、对铜盐与渔场的冲突，以及北方更复杂政体势力向南接触所带来的压力
threats.en: The main threats came from intertribal competition for resources, rivalry over coastal and river-valley control, conflict over copper, salt, and fisheries, and pressure created by increasing contact with more complex polities to the north

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布极为分散，主要集中于珠江流域、闽粤沿海、岭南盆地、河口三角洲、低山丘陵与适合稻作的谷地。聚落规模多为小型村寨，少数区域中心可达数千人
populationDescription.en: The population was highly dispersed, concentrated mainly in the Pearl River basin, the Fujian-Guangdong coast, Lingnan basins, estuarine deltas, low hills, and rice-growing valleys. Most settlements were small villages or fortified hamlets, though a few regional centers reached several thousand inhabitants

name.zh: 珠江三角洲中心聚落群

name.en: Upper Xi River Alliance Fortified Center

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为酋长与首领家族、祭司与巫觋、武装亲随、工匠、自由农民与渔民、附属家庭及俘获者。阶层差异已出现，但尚未形成严格的国家等级制
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into chiefs and leading families, priests and shamans, armed retainers, craftspeople, free farmers and fishers, dependent households, and captives. Social differences had emerged, but a rigid state hierarchy had not yet formed

allies.zh: 内部以邻近部落婚盟和贸易盟约为主要合作形式；对外与西南方向的百越先民群体及东南亚北缘文化圈可能存在松散交换联系
allies.en: Internally, cooperation took the form of marriage alliances and trade compacts among neighboring tribes; externally there were likely loose exchange contacts with ancestral Yue groups to the southwest and cultural spheres along the northern edge of mainland Southeast Asia

enemies.zh: 无长期统一外敌，冲突对象主要是相邻部落联盟；在北缘与长江以南其他复杂化文化接触时，也可能出现竞争与掠夺
enemies.en: There was no single long-term external enemy; conflicts were mainly with neighboring tribal alliances. Competition and raiding could also arise along the northern margins where these communities met more complex cultures south of the Yangtze

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外策略以维持交换通道、缔结婚盟、避免大规模持久战争为主；在关键资源点如盐场、铜源、渡口与渔场则倾向采取强硬防御或袭扰
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized keeping exchange routes open, building marriage alliances, and avoiding prolonged large-scale wars; at key resource sites such as salt fields, copper sources, ferries, and fisheries, however, communities tended toward firm defense or raiding

era.zh: 青铜时代早期的地方过渡阶段
era.en: A local transitional phase of the Early Bronze Age

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟湿地稻作、山地刀耕火种结合定居农业、印纹硬陶、高地或干栏木构建筑、独木舟航行、贝玉饰制作、有限青铜冶铸与区域性聚落防御工事
keyInnovations.en: Developed wet-rice cultivation, upland slash-and-burn combined with settled farming, stamped hard pottery, elevated or stilted timber architecture, dugout canoe navigation, shell and jade ornament production, limited bronze metallurgy, and regional settlement fortifications

strengths.zh: 生态适应能力强，稻作与渔盐经济互补，海湾与河网交通便利，文化形态多样，地方联盟在资源调配与生计恢复方面具有韧性
strengths.en: These cultures had strong ecological adaptability, complementary rice-farming and fishing-salt economies, convenient transport through bays and river networks, diverse cultural forms, and resilient local alliances for resource allocation and recovery

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、无统一文字与官僚体系、军事组织有限、跨区域动员能力弱，对北方先进青铜技术和更复杂政体的竞争处于不利地位
weaknesses.en: They were politically fragmented, lacked unified writing and bureaucracy, had limited military organization, and possessed weak cross-regional mobilization, leaving them at a disadvantage compared with northern societies using more advanced bronze technology and more complex political structures

outlook.zh: 总体前景稳定但分化显著。未来数世纪内，部分华南与岭南文化可能继续保持地方酋邦形态，部分则会在与中原、长江流域及东南亚北缘交流中吸收青铜技术、扩大等级分化，并逐步走向更复杂的区域政体
outlook.en: The overall outlook was stable but marked by divergence. Over the coming centuries, some South China and Lingnan cultures would likely remain local chieftaincies, while others would absorb bronze technology through contact with the Central Plains, the Yangtze region, and the northern margins of Southeast Asia, deepen social stratification, and move toward more complex regional polities

description.zh: 华南与岭南分布着众多农业与海岸部族共同体，尚未形成统一国家。
description.en: Southern and Lingnan China contained numerous agrarian and coastal tribal communities not yet unified into states.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落—村社联盟，以地方首领、宗族长与长老议事为核心，没有统一王朝国家
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal-village confederative order centered on local chiefs, clan heads, and councils of elders, without a unified dynastic state

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村落农民、牧民、手工业者、商旅群体与地方首领家族构成，较旧城市时代更地方化且分层较浅，但精英家庭仍控制土地、水源、储粮与交换网络
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of village farmers, pastoralists, craft workers, itinerant traders, and local chiefly families. It was more localized and somewhat less stratified than the earlier urban age, though elite households still controlled land, water, stored grain, and exchange networks

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长、宗族首领、祭祀长老与掌握仓储及灌溉的家族精英
rulingClass.en: Local chiefs, clan leaders, ritual elders, and household elites who controlled storage and irrigation

succession.zh: 多为宗族内部的世袭与长老认可相结合，部分聚落首领可能由强势家族推举产生
succession.en: Succession generally combined hereditary transmission within leading clans with approval by councils of elders; in some settlements leaders were likely selected from dominant families

structure.zh: 不存在覆盖整个文明的中央官僚体系。治理主要在区域与聚落层面进行，由酋长、长老会、仓储管理者、灌溉监督者和祭祀人物分担公共事务
structure.en: There was no civilization-wide centralized bureaucracy. Governance operated mainly at regional and settlement levels, with chiefs, elder councils, storehouse managers, irrigation supervisors, and ritual figures sharing public functions

function.zh: 裁决纠纷、协调宗族关系、确认首领权威与处理婚姻、土地和赔偿事务
function.en: Adjudicated disputes, coordinated clan relations, confirmed chiefly authority, and handled marriage, land, and compensation matters

function.zh: 保管谷物、组织贡纳与劳役征集、在歉收时调配储备
function.en: Managed grain stores, organized tribute and labor collection, and redistributed reserves during poor harvests

function.zh: 维护井渠、堤岸、蓄水设施和农田边界，协调播种与收获时序
function.en: Maintained wells, channels, embankments, water-control works, and field boundaries, and coordinated planting and harvest schedules

function.zh: 监督陶器、铜器、珠饰等作坊生产，管理度量习惯与区域交换联系
function.en: Oversaw workshops producing pottery, copper tools, and beads, and managed customary measures and regional exchange links

function.zh: 组织聚落守卫、护送商旅、召集战时民兵并监视边缘地带
function.en: Organized settlement guards, escorted caravans, mustered wartime militias, and watched frontier zones

localAdmin.zh: 行政单位以村落、集镇和若干区域中心为主。哈拉帕等旧城址仍可能保有象征性与区域性功能，但实际权力分散于周边社区与乡村网络
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on villages, market towns, and a few regional centers. Former urban sites such as Harappa likely retained symbolic and regional importance, but real power was dispersed among surrounding communities and rural networks

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依赖习惯法、宗族调解、赔偿原则与长老裁断，而非成文法典。盗窃、牲畜纠纷、水源争夺和婚姻财产是常见案件
legalSystem.en: Law relied chiefly on customary practice, clan mediation, compensation, and elder judgment rather than written legal codes. Theft, livestock disputes, water access conflicts, and marriage property issues were common cases

taxationSystem.zh: 没有统一税制，更多是以谷物、牲畜、手工业品和季节性劳役形式向地方首领或公共仓储缴纳贡纳
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; instead, contributions were rendered to local chiefs or communal storehouses in the form of grain, livestock, craft goods, and seasonal labor service

religion.zh: 宗教呈现延续与地方化并存的状态，包含生育崇拜、祖先与地方神灵崇拜、动物象征、火与净化仪式，以及与水源、树木和土地相关的神圣观念
religion.en: Religion showed both continuity and localization, including fertility cults, ancestor and local deity worship, animal symbolism, fire and purification rites, and sacred concepts associated with water, trees, and the land

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系统哲学传统，世界观主要体现在祖先传统、农时经验、自然循环观念与社区和谐的实践伦理之中
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical tradition is evident; worldview was expressed through ancestral custom, agricultural knowledge, ideas of natural cycles, and a practical ethic of communal harmony

writingSystem.zh: 印度河印记符号传统大体衰落，已不再像成熟城市时期那样广泛用于行政。部分符号可能在印记、陶器刻划或身份标识中零散延续，但整体上书写功能显著减弱
writingSystem.en: The Indus sign tradition had largely declined and was no longer widely used for administration as in the mature urban period. Some signs may have survived sporadically on seals, pottery marks, or identity symbols, but writing functions had greatly diminished overall

culturalAchievements.zh: 尽管大城市衰退，仍延续高水平陶器制作、珠饰工艺、棉纺织传统、井与排水技术经验、地方性建筑技巧以及跨区域风格混合
culturalAchievements.en: Despite urban decline, the civilization preserved strong pottery production, bead craftsmanship, cotton textile traditions, knowledge of wells and drainage, local building techniques, and a blending of regional styles

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不确定，可能包含多种地方语言；学界常推测部分居民使用与原始达罗毗荼语相关的语言，也可能并存其他底层语言群
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain and likely involved multiple local languages; scholars often hypothesize that some populations spoke languages related to Proto-Dravidian, alongside other substrate language groups

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业与牧业为基础，较成熟哈拉帕时代明显收缩并地方化。总产出仍相当可观，但更多分散在众多村落与区域交换网络中，而非集中于大型城市与国家仓储
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture and pastoralism and had clearly contracted and localized compared with the Mature Harappan age. Aggregate output remained substantial, but it was dispersed across many villages and regional exchange networks rather than concentrated in major cities and state storehouses

mainIndustries.zh: 小麦与大麦种植、豆类与芝麻栽培、牛羊牧养、棉纺织、陶器烧制、铜器加工、石珠与贝饰制作、区域运输贸易
mainIndustries.en: Wheat and barley cultivation, pulses and sesame farming, cattle and sheep herding, cotton textiles, pottery firing, copper working, stone bead and shell ornament production, and regional transport trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、棉布、陶器、铜器、半宝石珠、贝壳饰品、木材、牲畜、乳制品与少量来自西方和高地的金属原料
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cotton cloth, pottery, copper tools, semi-precious stone beads, shell ornaments, timber, livestock, dairy products, and limited metal raw materials from western and upland regions

name.zh: 以物易物与重量银并用

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富主要体现为粮食储备、牲畜、农具、陶器和家屋，而非金属货币。富裕家庭则拥有更多牛群、水井使用权、珠饰与铜器
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in grain stores, livestock, tools, pottery, and dwellings rather than coinage. Wealthier households possessed larger cattle herds, better access to wells, and more ornaments and copper goods

unit.en: annual per capita income in grams of silver equivalent

unit.zh: 年度对外交换折算银克
unit.en: annual foreign exchange in grams of silver equivalent

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给为主、区域交换为辅的农业—牧业经济，配合首领贡纳、家庭手工业与有限长距离贸易
economicSystem.en: A village-based agro-pastoral economy oriented toward subsistence, supplemented by regional exchange, chiefly tribute, household craft production, and limited long-distance trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”并非中央金库，而是分散于各区域中心和酋长仓储中的谷物、牲畜、铜器、珠饰及少量称量金银
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a central vault but a dispersed stock of grain, livestock, copper goods, ornaments, and small quantities of weighed precious metal held in regional centers and chiefly storehouses

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在维持粮食安全、水利修缮和地方守卫，收入多取实物与劳役，政策倾向保守与应急而非扩张
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on food security, maintenance of waterworks, and local defense; revenues were collected mostly in kind and labor, and policy was conservative and survival-oriented rather than expansionist

description.zh: 分散驻守于区域中心、仓储和交通节点的常备守卫，装备长矛、弓箭、木盾与少量铜制武器
description.en: Dispersed guards stationed at regional centers, storehouses, and transit points, equipped with spears, bows, wooden shields, and a limited number of copper weapons

description.zh: 由村社农牧民在危机时征召而成，主要负责防御、护卫牲群和应对邻近冲突
description.en: Farmers and pastoralists mustered in times of danger, mainly for defense, herd protection, and nearby conflicts

description.zh: 效忠地方强族与酋长的小规模精锐，承担护卫、追击与威慑任务，机动性相对较高
description.en: Small elite bands loyal to dominant families and chiefs, used for protection, pursuit, and intimidation, with relatively higher mobility

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地酋长分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; command was exercised separately by local chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术属青铜时代较低至中等水平，以步兵、弓矛和简易防御工事为主，缺乏成熟战车体系与强国家后勤
technology.en: Military technology stood at a low to moderate Bronze Age level, centered on infantry, bows and spears, and simple fortifications, without a developed chariot arm or strong state logistics

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自区域间资源竞争、牧群掠夺、河道与水源变动引发的迁徙压力，以及与早期吠陀印欧雅利安群体接触中的局部冲突
threats.en: Major threats came from interregional competition for resources, livestock raiding, migration pressures caused by river and water-source shifts, and localized conflict arising from contact with early Vedic Indo-Aryan groups

populationDescription.zh: 人口较成熟哈拉帕时代分散得多，绝大多数生活在村庄、小型聚落和河谷农牧地带，旧城市遗址仅保留有限人口与区域功能
populationDescription.en: The population was far more dispersed than in the Mature Harappan period, with the great majority living in villages, small settlements, and riverine agro-pastoral zones, while former urban sites retained only limited populations and regional functions

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为地方首领与强宗族、祭祀与长老、自由农民与牧民、手工业者与商旅、依附劳作者等层次，但界限不如同时代王国社会那样僵硬
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into local chiefs and strong clans, ritual specialists and elders, free farmers and pastoralists, craft workers and traders, and dependent laborers, though boundaries were less rigid than in contemporary kingdoms

allies.zh: 无统一的正式盟友体系；各地区聚落可能与德干早期农牧文化、高地社群及部分海湾贸易中介保持松散交换关系
allies.en: There was no unified formal alliance system; individual regions may have maintained loose exchange relations with early Deccan agro-pastoral cultures, upland communities, and some Gulf trade intermediaries

enemies.zh: 无单一国敌，但与邻近游动牧群、资源竞争者以及部分进入西北次大陆的早期吠陀群体存在摩擦
enemies.en: There was no single national enemy, but friction existed with neighboring mobile pastoral groups, resource competitors, and some early Vedic groups entering the northwestern subcontinent

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以地方生存和交换维系为主，重在保障水源、放牧路线、婚姻联盟与区域贸易，而非远征或领土扩张
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily about local survival and maintaining exchange, emphasizing water access, grazing routes, marriage alliances, and regional trade rather than conquest or territorial expansion

keyInnovations.zh: 延续井水利用、排水经验、标准化陶器传统、棉纺织、铜器铸造与石珠加工，但创新速度已放缓，更多表现为地方适应和旧技术残存
keyInnovations.en: Continuities included well use, drainage knowledge, standardized pottery traditions, cotton textiles, copper casting, and stone bead working, but innovation had slowed and was expressed more through local adaptation and the survival of older techniques

strengths.zh: 拥有深厚的农业传统、村社韧性、广泛分布的人口基础、成熟手工业记忆以及对多样生态环境的适应能力
strengths.en: Its strengths were deep agricultural traditions, resilient village communities, a broad population base, enduring craft knowledge, and the ability to adapt to varied ecological settings

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一中央政权与大型城市组织能力，远距离贸易衰退，文字行政功能减弱，河道变化与生态压力加剧，容易在外来人口流动与内部碎片化中继续衰落
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included the absence of a unified central state and large-scale urban organization, declining long-distance trade, diminished administrative writing, intensifying riverine and ecological stress, and vulnerability to further decline amid migration and internal fragmentation

outlook.zh: 到公元前1600年前后，该文明已不再是统一的城市文明，而是向多区域乡村文化群转化。其物质与社会传统将继续影响西北与西印度次大陆，并在与早期吠陀文化及地方传统的交汇中逐步融入新的历史格局
outlook.en: By around 1600 BCE, this was no longer a unified urban civilization but a transition into multiple regional rural cultural spheres. Its material and social traditions would continue to shape northwestern and western South Asia and would gradually merge into new historical formations through interaction with early Vedic culture and local traditions

description.zh: 印度河流域的大城市时代已过，分散的地方政体与农业聚落继续存在。
description.en: The great urban age of the Indus had passed, but dispersed local polities and agrarian settlements persisted.

name.zh: 德干铜石并用文化诸酋邦

capital.zh: 因地区而异，无统一都城
capital.en: Varied by region, with no single capital

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落酋邦联盟与村社首领制
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal chiefdoms and village headman rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、氏族和村落共同体为核心，上层为酋长家族与战士随从，中层为自由农牧民与手工业者，下层为依附者、债役者及战争俘获人口。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kin groups, clans, and village communities, with chiefdom families and warrior retainers at the top, free agro-pastoralists and artisans in the middle, and dependents, debt bondsmen, and war captives at the lower end.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、氏族长老与武装随从
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly lineages, clan elders, and armed retainers

succession.zh: 多为父系世袭并经氏族长老认可，部分地区也可能在近亲支系中择贤继承
succession.en: Usually patrilineal hereditary succession ratified by clan elders, with some regions selecting capable heirs from related lineages

structure.zh: 无统一国家官僚机构，各地区由酋长主持祭祀、分配土地与牧场、组织防御和调解争端；村社由长老会议与家长共同治理。
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; regional chiefs oversaw ritual, allocation of land and pasture, defense, and dispute mediation, while village communities were governed by councils of elders and household heads.

function.zh: 管理酋长住所、储粮、贡品、宾客接待与部落内部协调
function.en: Managed the chief's residence, grain stores, tribute goods, hospitality, and internal tribal coordination

function.zh: 就继承、土地使用、婚姻联盟、纠纷与公共事务提出集体决议
function.en: Issued collective decisions on succession, land use, marriage alliances, disputes, and communal affairs

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、季节仪式、雨祈、牲畜献祭及农业时序安排
function.en: Conducted ancestor rites, seasonal ceremonies, rain rituals, animal offerings, and agricultural timing

function.zh: 收集部分谷物、家畜、乳制品、皮革和手工品，并在饥荒或宴饮时再分配
function.en: Collected portions of grain, livestock, dairy products, hides, and craft goods for redistribution in famine relief or feasting

function.zh: 动员青壮年战士、守卫聚落、护卫牲群并参与袭掠与报复行动
function.en: Mobilized able-bodied warriors, guarded settlements, protected herds, and carried out raiding and retaliatory actions

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村社和氏族为单位，谷地、台地与牧场区域常由若干聚落围绕一个较强酋长形成松散支配网络。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages and clans; valley, upland, and pasture zones often formed loose networks of settlements around a stronger chief.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，由长老、家族首领和酋长仲裁；赔偿、牲畜补偿、婚姻交换和血亲调解是主要处理方式。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, adjudicated by elders, lineage heads, and chiefs; compensation, livestock payments, marriage exchange, and kin mediation were the principal mechanisms.

taxationSystem.zh: 无标准化税制，主要通过贡纳、劳役、节庆献礼、牲畜供给和战时动员来支持酋长与公共事务。
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized tax system; support for chiefs and communal functions came mainly through tribute, labor obligations, festival offerings, livestock supply, and wartime mobilization.

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然神灵、山丘与河流圣地信仰、生育与畜群保护仪式为核心，常见火祭、牲畜祭和季节性公共宴仪。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, sacred hills and rivers, and fertility and herd-protection rites, with fire rituals, animal offerings, and seasonal communal feasts.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学传统，价值观重在亲族义务、长幼秩序、互惠交换、勇武声望与对土地雨水循环的敬畏。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition; values emphasized kin obligation, age hierarchy, reciprocal exchange, warrior prestige, and reverence for land and seasonal rainfall.

writingSystem.zh: 无本地文字系统，知识主要依赖口述传统、歌谣、仪式记忆和手工技艺传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no local writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, song, ritual memory, and craft apprenticeship.

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出适应德干高原环境的混合农牧经济、磨石与陶器传统、半定居聚落布局、储粮设施、珠饰制作和区域性祭祀宴饮文化。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed a mixed agro-pastoral economy adapted to the Deccan plateau, traditions of stone tools and pottery, semi-sedentary settlement layouts, grain storage facilities, bead-making, and regional ritual-feasting culture.

languageFamily.zh: 可能以早期达罗毗荼语系方言为主，并伴有其他地方性语言群
languageFamily.en: Likely dominated by early Dravidian dialects, alongside other local language groups

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模不高但稳定，主要建立在小米、豆类、部分谷物种植与牛、羊、山羊饲养基础上，市场交换有限，财富多以牲畜、粮食和劳力体现。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was modest but stable, based chiefly on millet, pulses, some cereal cultivation, and the herding of cattle, sheep, and goats; market exchange was limited, and wealth was expressed mostly in livestock, grain, and labor.

mainIndustries.zh: 农耕、畜牧、制陶、磨石工具制作、骨角器、编织、皮革加工、珠饰与小规模铜器使用
mainIndustries.en: Farming, pastoralism, pottery, ground stone tool production, bone and antler work, weaving, leatherworking, bead-making, and limited copper use

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、乳制品、谷物、豆类、陶器、石珠、半宝石原料、木材与少量铜器或铜料
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, hides, dairy products, grain, pulses, pottery, stone beads, semi-precious stone materials, timber, and small amounts of copper goods or copper raw material

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要由牛群、羊群、储粮、陶器、金石珠饰和劳动力构成；酋长家族在牲畜数量、宴饮能力和婚姻交换上明显占优。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth consisted mainly of cattle, sheep and goats, stored grain, pottery, ornaments of stone and beads, and available labor; chiefly families held clear advantages in herd size, feasting capacity, and marriage exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、村社协作、互惠交换和酋长再分配为核心的农牧混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed agro-pastoral economy centered on household production, village cooperation, reciprocal exchange, and chiefly redistribution

source.en: Labor dues and public works obligations

source.zh: 交换中转与酋长再分配收益
source.en: Exchange intermediation and chiefly redistribution gains

category.zh: 储粮、畜群维护与灾荒调剂
category.en: Storage, herd maintenance, and famine relief

category.zh: 婚盟赠礼与部落间协调

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金属储备，而是分散于各酋长控制下的储粮坑、牲群、贡物、珠饰和少量铜器、贵重石料。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not a centralized metal reserve but a dispersed stock of grain pits, herds, tribute goods, ornaments, and small quantities of copper objects and valued stones under chiefly control.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作以维持酋长威望和再分配能力为重，优先保障祭祀、宴饮、亲族联盟和基本防御，而非积累大量金属货币。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized maintaining chiefly prestige and redistributive capacity, prioritizing ritual, feasting, kin alliances, and basic defense rather than accumulating large metallic reserves.

description.zh: 由酋长直接供养的核心武装，负责护卫、报复性袭击、牛群防卫和重要集会安全。
description.en: The core force maintained directly by chiefs, responsible for protection, retaliatory raids, herd defense, and security at major gatherings.

description.zh: 由各聚落成年男子临时动员，使用木矛、石制或铜制刃器、弓箭和棍棒，主要执行防御和边界巡护。
description.en: Temporarily mobilized adult men from settlements, using wooden spears, stone- or copper-bladed weapons, bows, and clubs, mainly for defense and border watch.

name.zh: 牧群护卫与机动袭掠队
name.en: Herd Guards and Mobile Raiding Bands

description.zh: 依托熟悉地形的青年战士，保护迁牧路线并在资源竞争时实施快速袭扰。
description.en: Young fighters familiar with the terrain who protected transhumant routes and conducted rapid raids during resource competition.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地酋长分别统率，无统一最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: Regional chiefs commanded separately, with no unified supreme commander

technology.zh: 军事技术以木矛、弓箭、石锤、棍棒、皮盾及少量铜刃器为主，依赖熟悉地形、突袭和防守聚落外围工事。
technology.en: Military technology relied on wooden spears, bows, stone maces, clubs, hide shields, and a limited number of copper blades, with tactics depending on terrain knowledge, raiding, and the defense of simple settlement works.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落争夺牧场、水源和牲畜，此外还有局部旱灾引发的人口迁动与冲突；尚未面临来自外部帝国的持续征服压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring tribes competing for pasture, water, and livestock, as well as movement and conflict caused by localized drought; there was not yet sustained conquest pressure from external empires.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于德干高原各河谷、台地和半干旱草灌地区，以中小型村落和季节性牧营为主，整体稳定增长缓慢。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across river valleys, uplands, and semi-arid grass-shrub zones of the Deccan plateau, living mainly in small and medium villages and seasonal pastoral camps, with overall slow but stable growth.

name.zh: 伊纳姆冈类型区域中心
戴马巴德类型聚落中心

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族与长老、战士随从、自由农牧民、工匠与交换中介者、依附者及少量俘获人口构成主要社会层次。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata consisted of chiefly families and elders, warrior retainers, free agro-pastoralists, artisans and exchange intermediaries, dependents, and a small number of captives.

allies.zh: 无统一对外联盟；通常通过邻近氏族、婚姻关系和季节性互助网络维持局部友好
allies.en: There were no unified external alliances; local friendship was usually maintained through neighboring clans, marriage ties, and seasonal mutual-aid networks

enemies.zh: 无固定国家级宿敌，但与邻近酋邦、迁入群体和争夺资源的部落经常发生冲突；北方晚期后哈拉帕与早期吠陀群体的接触可能既有交换也有摩擦。
enemies.en: There were no fixed state-level enemies, but conflict was frequent with neighboring chiefdoms, incoming groups, and tribes competing over resources; contact with late post-urban Indus and early Vedic groups to the north likely involved both exchange and friction.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以婚盟、互市、牲畜交换、路径共享和有限武装报复为主，目标是维持地方平衡而非扩张性征服。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations centered on marriage alliances, local exchange, livestock dealings, route sharing, and limited armed retaliation, aiming to preserve local balance rather than pursue expansionist conquest.

keyInnovations.zh: 混合农牧生产、储粮坑与仓储设施、较成熟的手制与轮制陶器、磨制石器传统、珠饰工艺、有限铜器使用以及适应季风节律的季节性生产安排。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mixed agro-pastoral production, storage pits and granary facilities, increasingly developed handmade and wheel-made pottery, ground stone traditions, bead craftsmanship, limited copper use, and seasonal production schedules adapted to monsoon rhythms.

strengths.zh: 其优势在于对德干环境的适应力强，经济结构兼具农业与畜牧弹性，地方共同体凝聚力高，外来冲击较弱时能维持长期稳定。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in strong adaptation to the Deccan environment, the flexibility of a mixed farming and herding economy, high cohesion within local communities, and the ability to sustain long-term stability when external pressures remained low.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是政治分散、缺乏统一行政和文字记录，难以形成大规模工程、远程军事协同和稳定的跨区域征收体系，也较易受旱灾影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were political fragmentation, the absence of unified administration and writing, difficulty in organizing large-scale works, long-range military coordination, and stable supra-regional extraction systems, as well as vulnerability to drought.

outlook.zh: 短期内这些文化群仍将以地方酋邦形态持续存在，并与北方及沿海区域发生更多交换；长期看，金属使用扩大、聚落分化和跨区域接触增强，可能推动更复杂的社会分层与区域文化整合。
outlook.en: In the near term these cultural groups would likely persist as local chiefdoms while engaging in growing exchange with northern and coastal regions; over the longer run, wider metal use, settlement differentiation, and intensified interregional contact could encourage more complex stratification and regional cultural integration.

description.zh: 德干高原分布着多个农牧结合的区域首领社会，尚未形成统一王朝。
description.en: The Deccan hosted multiple agro-pastoral chiefdoms without any unified dynasty.

name.zh: 东南亚早期青铜聚落网络
name.en: Early Southeast Asian Bronze Settlement Networks
Ban Chiang and Related Cultural Sphere

governmentForm.zh: 区域性村落联盟与酋邦网络，由多个自治聚落通过婚姻、祭祀与交换联系在一起
governmentForm.en: A regional network of village alliances and chiefdoms, with multiple autonomous communities linked through marriage, ritual, and exchange

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、村落与区域交换网络为基础，存在较明显但非绝对的地位差异；手工业者、农民、渔猎者与祭仪主持者共同构成社会核心
socialStructure.en: Society was based on kinship, villages, and regional exchange networks, with visible but not absolute status distinctions; craft specialists, farmers, fishers-hunters, and ritual leaders formed the social core

rulingClass.zh: 掌握土地分配、祭祀权威与长距离交换渠道的地方首领家族
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly lineages controlling land allocation, ritual authority, and access to long-distance exchange routes

succession.zh: 多为家族世袭并辅以长老认可，部分聚落可能根据婚姻联盟与个人威望进行继承调整
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading families with elder approval, though succession in some communities could be adjusted through marriage alliances and personal prestige

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家官僚体制；治理以村落议事、酋长协调和区域性礼仪中心为核心，较大聚落可对周边小聚落形成松散领导
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; governance centered on village councils, chiefly coordination, and regional ritual centers, while larger settlements could exercise loose leadership over nearby smaller communities

function.zh: 组织稻作、蓄水、田地分配与季节性劳作协调
function.en: Organized rice cultivation, water storage, field allocation, and seasonal labor coordination

function.zh: 管理铜、锡、石器、陶器、贝饰与区域贸易往来，维护工匠与运输网络
function.en: Managed flows of copper, tin, stone tools, pottery, shell ornaments, and regional trade, maintaining artisan and transport networks

function.zh: 主持祖先崇拜、葬俗、社群庆典与跨聚落礼仪关系
function.en: Oversaw ancestor worship, funerary customs, communal festivals, and inter-settlement ritual relations

function.zh: 组织村落守卫、狩猎武装与聚落间纠纷裁决
function.en: Organized village guards, hunting war-bands, and arbitration of disputes between settlements

function.zh: 征集稻米、鱼干、陶器与金属制品等贡礼，用于再分配、祭祀和接待外来交换者
function.en: Collected tribute in rice, dried fish, pottery, and metal goods for redistribution, ritual use, and hosting outside traders

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理以村落为单位，由酋长、长老、亲族领袖和祭仪主持者共同管理；较大河谷与高地边缘聚落可能形成若干区域中心
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the village level through chiefs, elders, kin leaders, and ritual specialists; larger riverine and upland-edge settlements could serve as regional centers

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，强调亲族调解、赔偿、婚姻安排与公共仪式宣誓；严重冲突可能导致逐出、赔礼或报复性械斗
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based mainly on customary law, emphasizing kin mediation, compensation, marriage arrangements, and oath-taking in public ritual; severe conflicts could lead to expulsion, compensatory offerings, or retaliatory fighting

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，主要以季节性贡礼、公共劳役、宴饮供给和交换品上缴形式维持首领权威与区域再分配
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; chiefly authority and regional redistribution were sustained mainly through seasonal tribute, communal labor, feast provisions, and contributions of exchange goods

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然灵信仰与地方祭祀为主，河流、稻田、山地与墓地被视为具有神圣力量；随葬品反映灵魂延续与身份展示观念
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, animistic beliefs, and local ritual cults, with rivers, rice fields, hills, and burial grounds regarded as spiritually potent; grave goods reflect ideas of continued existence and status display

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学传统，世界观着重祖先、土地、季节循环、社群和谐与交换互惠
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical tradition had emerged; the worldview emphasized ancestors, land, seasonal cycles, communal harmony, and reciprocity in exchange

writingSystem.zh: 无已知文字系统，信息通过口述传统、记忆谱系、器物样式和礼仪实践传承
writingSystem.en: There was no known writing system; information was transmitted through oral tradition, remembered genealogies, artifact styles, and ritual practice

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展了具有区域特色的彩陶与绳纹、刻划装饰传统；较早掌握铜基与青铜器制造；稻作社会与墓葬礼仪高度结合，展现出稳定而广泛的文化互动圈
culturalAchievements.en: The sphere developed regionally distinctive painted pottery and cord-marked or incised decorative traditions; it adopted early copper-base and bronze manufacture; rice-farming society was closely integrated with funerary ritual, showing a stable and wide cultural interaction zone

languageFamily.zh: 语言状况不明，可能包含南亚语系与早期南岛语、侗台相关或其他已消失的地方语言成分
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain, possibly including Austroasiatic elements alongside early Austronesian, Kra-Dai-related, or other now-lost local languages

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作为核心，辅以渔猎、采集、家畜饲养、陶器生产和金属加工。整体生产力属于早期青铜时代的中等区域水平，剩余主要集中在较大聚落与酋长家庭手中。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on rice farming, supplemented by fishing, hunting, gathering, animal keeping, pottery production, and metalworking. Overall productivity was moderate for an early Bronze Age regional sphere, with surplus concentrated mainly in larger settlements and chiefly households.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、渔业、狩猎采集、陶器烧造、铜器与早期青铜器制作、石器打制与磨制、纺织和贝饰加工
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, fishing, hunting-gathering, pottery firing, copper and early bronze production, chipped and polished stone tool manufacture, textile work, and shell ornament processing

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、鱼干、盐、陶器、石斧、贝饰、铜锭、铜器、少量青铜器、林木产品、树脂与羽毛等珍异品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, dried fish, salt, pottery, stone axes, shell ornaments, copper ingots, copper tools, limited bronze goods, forest products, resins, and feathered exotica

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为稻田使用权、储粮、陶器、金属器、家畜、渔猎工具与装饰品；首领家庭在墓葬与宴饮能力上明显更富
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through use-rights over rice fields, stored grain, pottery, metal tools, livestock, fishing and hunting gear, and ornaments; chiefly families were clearly wealthier in funerary display and feasting capacity

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、村落共享、酋长再分配和区域交换并行的混合经济体系
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining household production, village sharing, chiefly redistribution, and regional exchange

category.en: Support for chiefly households and administration

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金库，而是分散在区域中心的储粮、铜料、贝饰与礼仪财富；可在歉收、婚盟、祭祀与冲突时调动
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not a centralized vault but a dispersed stock of grain, copper materials, shell valuables, and ritual wealth held in regional centers; it could be mobilized during poor harvests, marriage alliances, ceremonies, and conflict

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政逻辑以稳定粮食储备、维持首领威望、资助宴饮与交换、并在灾荒时实施再分配为主
