succession.en: Succession was primarily patrilineal and usually passed to a royal son, though powerful nobles and court alliances could also support fraternal succession or accession by collateral branches.

structure.zh: 米坦尼王国实行宫廷主导的分层统治。国王统辖核心地区，并通过附庸王、公侯、总督与驻军控制上美索不达米亚和北叙利亚。行政并非高度统一的官僚帝国，而是王室直辖区、附庸城邦和边疆军政据点并存的复合体系。
structure.en: The Mitanni kingdom operated a court-led layered system of rule. The king governed the core territories directly and controlled Upper Mesopotamia and northern Syria through vassal kings, princes, governors, and garrisons. Administration was not a fully uniform bureaucracy, but a composite system of royal domains, vassal cities, and frontier military-administrative centers.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室地产、贡品储藏、外交接待与王命传达。
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, tribute storage, diplomatic reception, and transmission of royal orders.

function.zh: 统筹战车编练、马匹征集、贵族军役、边境驻防与战时动员。
function.en: Oversaw chariot organization and training, horse levies, noble military service, frontier garrisons, and wartime mobilization.

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金属、纺织品与附庸贡品，并管理中央仓储。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, textiles, and vassal tribute, and managed central storage facilities.

function.zh: 负责外交文书、契约、土地记录、附庸誓约与王室档案。
function.en: Handled diplomatic records, contracts, land records, vassal oaths, and royal archives.

function.zh: 组织国家祭祀、维护神庙地产、协调祭司团体与占卜仪式。
function.en: Organized state cult rituals, maintained temple estates, and coordinated priestly bodies and divinatory ceremonies.

function.zh: 监督地方统治者、附庸王、边地城镇与道路通信网络。
function.en: Supervised local rulers, vassal kings, frontier towns, and road communications.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依靠旧有城邦传统与王室任命结合运作。核心地区由总督、城长和书记官管理；外围地区保留附庸王与本地贵族，只需纳贡、供兵并宣誓效忠。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined older city-state traditions with royal appointments. Core lands were run by governors, city rulers, and scribes; peripheral areas retained vassal kings and local nobles so long as they paid tribute, supplied troops, and swore loyalty.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以习惯法、王命、契约文书和城邦传统为基础，受胡里安、阿卡德和叙利亚法文化共同影响。土地、婚姻、债务、奴役与军役义务多以泥板契约记录。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on customary law, royal decree, written contracts, and city traditions, shaped jointly by Hurrian, Akkadian, and Syrian legal culture. Landholding, marriage, debt, servitude, and military obligations were often recorded on clay tablets.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政来源以实物征收为主，包括谷物、羊毛、牲畜、金属和劳役；附庸则缴纳贡物、马匹、战车兵与奢侈品。税负在不同地区差异较大，王室更重视贡赋与军役而非统一定额货币税。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was collected mainly in kind, including grain, wool, livestock, metals, and labor service; vassals paid tribute in goods, horses, chariot fighters, and luxury items. Burdens varied widely by region, and the crown valued tribute and military service more than standardized money taxation.

religion.zh: 宗教为多神信仰，以胡里安神祇为核心，如风暴神特舒布、女神赫巴特和沙乌什卡，同时吸收叙利亚、美索不达米亚传统；王权通过祭祀、誓约和占卜获得神圣合法性。上层军事文化中还保留少量与印欧雅利安神名相关的传统因素。
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, centered on Hurrian deities such as the storm god Teshub, the goddess Hebat, and Shaushka, while also absorbing Syrian and Mesopotamian traditions; kingship drew sacred legitimacy from ritual, oath, and divination. Elite military culture also preserved limited elements associated with Indo-Aryan divine names.

philosophy.zh: 尚无后世意义上的系统哲学，思想核心在于王权秩序、神意、誓约忠诚、家族荣誉与军事贵族伦理。
philosophy.en: There was no systematic philosophy in the later sense; the main intellectual values were royal order, divine will, oath-bound loyalty, family honor, and the ethics of a military aristocracy.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用楔形文字书写阿卡德语作为外交与行政文字，胡里安语也见于宗教和地方环境中；印章与泥板文书是国家治理的重要工具。
writingSystem.en: Cuneiform was used chiefly to write Akkadian for diplomacy and administration, while Hurrian also appeared in religious and local contexts; seals and clay tablets were essential instruments of governance.

culturalAchievements.zh: 米坦尼以马匹饲养、战车作战传统、胡里安宗教音乐与跨区域宫廷文化著称。其影响扩及北叙利亚，并在后世赫梯与近东文书中留下关于马术训练和外交礼制的重要遗产。
culturalAchievements.en: Mitanni was noted for horse breeding, chariot warfare traditions, Hurrian religious music, and a transregional court culture. Its influence extended across northern Syria and left an important legacy in later Hittite and Near Eastern records concerning horse training and diplomatic ceremonial.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言为胡里安语；外交行政多用东闪米特语支的阿卡德语书面语。
languageFamily.en: The principal language was Hurrian; written diplomacy and administration often used Akkadian of the East Semitic branch.

gdpDescription.zh: 米坦尼经济属于晚期青铜时代上美索不达米亚的高水平区域经济，以灌溉农业、旱作农业、牧马业、羊毛纺织、金属加工与跨区域贡赋贸易为支柱。其富庶更多体现为王室与贵族控制的实物财富、牲畜、土地和附庸贡品，而非统一货币市场。
gdpDescription.en: The Mitanni economy was a high-level regional Bronze Age economy of Upper Mesopotamia, based on irrigation and dry farming, horse and livestock breeding, wool textiles, metalworking, and long-distance tributary trade. Its wealth was expressed more in royal and aristocratic control of goods, herds, land, and vassal tribute than in a unified monetary market.

mainIndustries.zh: 大麦与小麦种植、绵羊与山羊畜牧、马匹饲养、战车与木工制造、青铜加工、毛纺织、皮革、陶器、神庙与宫廷手工业
mainIndustries.en: Barley and wheat cultivation, sheep and goat pastoralism, horse breeding, chariot and woodworking production, bronze working, wool textiles, leather, pottery, and temple and palace handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口或转运马匹、战车、羊毛织物、皮革、谷物余粮、奴隶、木材与金属制品；输入锡、优质铜、黄金、白银、香料、名贵石材、黎凡特奢侈品与埃及制成品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports or re-exports included horses, chariots, wool textiles, leather, grain surpluses, slaves, timber, and metal goods; imports included tin, quality copper, gold, silver, aromatics, fine stone, Levantine luxury goods, and Egyptian manufactures.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要以粮食储备、牲畜、农具、毛织物和家屋体现；贵族财富则集中于土地、马群、战车、附庸关系、金属器和奴仆。城乡差距与贵族和平民差距显著。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in grain stores, livestock, tools, wool textiles, and dwellings; noble wealth was concentrated in land, horse herds, chariots, dependent retainers, metal goods, and slaves. Gaps between town and countryside and between nobles and commoners were substantial.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为宫廷-神庙-庄园主导的再分配经济，辅以城镇交换、商旅贸易与附庸贡赋网络。实物征收和重量银结算并存，但日常基层经济仍以粮食、劳役与互惠交换为主。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a palace-temple-estate redistribution system supplemented by urban exchange, merchant trade, and a vassal tribute network. Collection in kind existed alongside settlement by weighed silver, but everyday local exchange still relied chiefly on grain, labor, and reciprocity.

source.zh: 农业实物税与王室庄园收入
source.en: Agricultural taxes in kind and royal estate income

source.zh: 牲畜、马匹与军役折算收入
source.en: Livestock, horses, and commuted military obligations

source.zh: 贸易关卡、贡道与市场收费
source.en: Trade tolls, caravan-route dues, and market charges

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以仓储谷物、羊毛、金属锭、重量银、牲畜记录和附庸贡品形式保存，而非统一铸币金库。王室能够在战争或歉收时动用储备，但流动性受运输与地方忠诚度制约。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was held chiefly as stored grain, wool, metal ingots, weighed silver, livestock accounts, and vassal tribute rather than as a coin-based hoard. The crown could mobilize reserves in war or famine, but liquidity was constrained by transport and local loyalty.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持军贵族忠诚、确保附庸纳贡、稳定粮仓储备和保障战车马政。丰年偏向积储，战时则通过非常贡赋、掠获和追加劳役扩大财政能力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining noble military loyalty, securing vassal tribute, stabilizing granary reserves, and supporting the chariot-horse establishment. In good years the crown emphasized accumulation; in wartime it expanded capacity through extraordinary levies, plunder, and additional labor obligations.

description.zh: 米坦尼最具威望的核心兵种，由玛丽安努贵族及其随从组成，擅长机动作战、远程射击与战场冲击，是王权与贵族身份的象征。
description.en: Mitanni's most prestigious core arm, composed of maryannu nobles and their retainers, skilled in maneuver warfare, missile combat, and battlefield shock, and serving as a symbol of kingship and aristocratic status.

description.zh: 由王室直属部队、城邦征召兵与附庸供兵组成，装备矛、弓、斧、短剑与盾牌，承担守城、护运和主战线支援任务。
description.en: Drawn from royal troops, city levies, and vassal contingents, infantry carried spears, bows, axes, short swords, and shields, and performed garrison, convoy, and line-support duties.

description.zh: 部署于要塞、渡口和交通节点，负责监视赫梯、亚述及叙利亚诸邦动向，并保护贡道与补给线。
description.en: Stationed at forts, crossings, and transport nodes, these troops monitored Hittite, Assyrian, and Syrian movements and protected tribute routes and supply lines.

description.zh: 由附庸城邦和边地部落提供，包括轻装步兵、弓手和少量地方战车，主要用于侦察、掠袭与侧翼支援。
description.en: Supplied by vassal cities and frontier tribes, these included light infantry, archers, and a few local chariots, mainly used for reconnaissance, raiding, and flank support.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王帕尔沙塔塔尔一世（约）
commanderInChief.en: King Parshatatar I (approx.)

technology.zh: 军事技术处于近东前列，尤以轻型双轮战车、复合弓传统、马匹训练与贵族战车战术闻名。青铜兵器、鳞甲或皮甲、头盔、盾牌与攻城基础器械皆已具备，但尚未形成后世大规模铁制装备。
technology.en: Military technology was among the leading systems of the Near East, especially in light two-wheeled chariots, composite-bow traditions, horse training, and aristocratic chariot tactics. Bronze weapons, scale or leather armor, helmets, shields, and basic siege equipment were all present, though large-scale iron armament had not yet emerged.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自安纳托利亚的赫梯扩张、东南方向亚述势力恢复、叙利亚附庸的不稳定，以及巴比伦与札格罗斯周边势力变化。保持对北叙利亚和哈布尔流域的控制是国家安全关键。
threats.en: The chief threats came from Hittite expansion in Anatolia, the recovery of Assyrian power to the southeast, instability among Syrian vassals, and shifts among Babylonian and Zagros polities. Maintaining control over northern Syria and the Khabur basin was central to state security.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约八十万，主体分布于哈布尔河流域、上幼发拉底地区与北叙利亚腹地。人口构成以胡里安人为主，混有阿摩利人、阿卡德语居民、叙利亚城邦居民及少量印欧战车贵族成分。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 800,000, concentrated in the Khabur basin, upper Euphrates districts, and the northern Syrian interior. The population was predominantly Hurrian, mixed with Amorites, Akkadian-speaking inhabitants, Syrian urban populations, and a small Indo-European-associated chariot elite element.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与高贵族居于顶层；其下为战车贵族、地方统治家族、祭司和书吏；再下为城镇工匠、商人与自由农牧民；底层包括依附佃农、债役者、战俘奴隶与家内奴仆。不同地区的城邦传统使阶层边界并不完全一致。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family and high nobility; below them were the chariot aristocracy, local ruling houses, priests, and scribes; beneath them were artisans, merchants, and free farmers and herders; the lowest strata included dependent tenants, debt bondsmen, war captives, and household slaves. Social boundaries were not entirely uniform across regions because of differing city traditions.

allies.zh: 1600年前后米坦尼尚处扩张上升阶段，稳定的长期同盟体系尚未完全定型；其现实伙伴主要是北叙利亚若干附庸城邦、胡里安关联政权及出于共同利益反制赫梯或亚述的地方势力。
allies.en: Around 1600 BCE Mitanni was still in an expansionary and consolidating phase, so a stable long-term alliance system was not yet fully formed; its practical partners were mainly vassal states in northern Syria, related Hurrian polities, and local powers willing to counter Hittite or Assyrian influence.

enemies.zh: 主要对手为赫梯王国、亚述诸城与争夺叙利亚控制权的区域强权；与巴比伦并非持续全面战争关系，但在外交与边境问题上存在竞争和牵制。
enemies.en: Its principal rivals were the Hittite kingdom, Assyrian centers, and regional powers competing for Syria; relations with Babylon were not one of constant total war, but they involved diplomatic rivalry and strategic balancing.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制北叙利亚交通走廊、收纳附庸、保障贡赋流动和维持大国均势为主。米坦尼常通过王室婚姻、宗主誓约、军事威慑和扶植地方统治者来扩大影响。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling the transport corridors of northern Syria, incorporating vassals, securing tribute flows, and maintaining a balance among great powers. Mitanni often expanded influence through royal marriage, suzerain oaths, military pressure, and support for local rulers.

keyInnovations.zh: 轻型战车体系、马匹训练与育种、复合弓应用、青铜兵器和工具、楔形文字行政、印章管理、灌溉与仓储组织、跨区域道路通信
keyInnovations.en: Light chariot systems, horse training and breeding, composite-bow use, bronze weapons and tools, cuneiform administration, seal management, irrigation and storage organization, and long-distance road communication

strengths.zh: 米坦尼的优势在于强大的战车贵族军事传统、对上美索不达米亚与北叙利亚要道的控制、胡里安文化凝聚力以及能把附庸网络转化为兵力和贡赋的政治能力。
strengths.en: Mitanni's strengths lay in its powerful chariot-aristocratic military tradition, control of key routes in Upper Mesopotamia and northern Syria, Hurrian cultural cohesion, and political ability to turn a vassal network into troops and tribute.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于行政整合度有限、对地方忠诚和附庸稳定依赖较深、贵族势力强而中央集权不足，并且在多线外交压力下容易因继承危机或外部强权夹击而失衡。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited administrative integration, deep dependence on local loyalty and vassal stability, a powerful nobility that constrained centralization, and vulnerability to succession crises or pressure from multiple great powers.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年左右，米坦尼处于兴盛上升期，具备成为近东北部主导强权的条件。若能稳固王位继承、整合叙利亚附庸并遏制赫梯与亚述复兴，其国势可继续扩张；但长期看，复合型统治结构也埋下分裂和被外敌逐步蚕食的风险。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE Mitanni was in a thriving upward phase and had the potential to become the dominant power of the northern Near East. If it could stabilize succession, integrate its Syrian vassals, and check Hittite and Assyrian recovery, its power could continue to expand; in the longer run, however, its composite structure also carried the risk of fragmentation and gradual erosion by external rivals.

description.zh: 上美索不达米亚的胡里安强国，以战车贵族和对叙利亚北部的影响著称。
description.en: A powerful Hurrian state in Upper Mesopotamia noted for its chariot aristocracy and influence in northern Syria.

name.zh: 原始腓尼基海洋文明（比布鲁斯城邦）

ruler.zh: 里布-哈达德式地方海岸统治者（姓名未详）
ruler.en: Local coastal ruler in the style of Rib-Hadda (personal name unknown)

governmentForm.zh: 以王室为核心、依托港口商贸与神庙网络的世袭城邦君主制
governmentForm.en: Hereditary city-state monarchy centered on the royal household and supported by port trade and temple networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族家族、神庙祭司、书记与行政人员、商人与船主、工匠、农民、渔民、码头劳工以及数量有限的债役或战俘奴仆构成。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal household and noble families, temple priests, scribes and administrators, merchants and shipowners, artisans, farmers, fishermen, dock laborers, and a limited number of debt servants or war captives.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、港口贵族、大神庙祭司与掌握对埃及贸易的富商家族
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed of the royal family, harbor nobility, senior temple priests, and wealthy merchant families controlling trade with Egypt

succession.zh: 通常为父系世袭长子继承，但在王位争议时需获得王族、神庙与城中显贵支持
succession.en: Succession was normally patrilineal and hereditary through the eldest son, though disputed accessions required support from royal kin, temples, and leading urban notables

structure.zh: 政权规模较小，王宫、神庙与港务体系互相重叠。国王直接统辖外交、贡赋、军备与大型贸易合同，日常事务由书记官、仓储官、港口官与地方村社头人执行。
structure.en: The polity was small in scale, with palace, temple, and port administration closely intertwined. The king directly oversaw diplomacy, tribute, armament, and major trade contracts, while daily affairs were carried out by scribes, storehouse officials, harbor officers, and heads of village communities.

function.zh: 负责文书、账册、往来书信、库存记录及对外条约文本
function.en: Handled documents, accounts, correspondence, inventory records, and texts of foreign agreements

function.zh: 管理停泊、装卸、船只征用、海运税费与外来商人登记
function.en: Managed berthing, loading and unloading, vessel requisitioning, maritime dues, and registration of foreign merchants

function.zh: 主持祭礼、管理庙产、保管奉献物并协助王权合法化
function.en: Supervised rituals, managed temple estates, safeguarded offerings, and supported royal legitimacy

function.zh: 征收木材、橄榄油、酒、银重与手工业品，负责集中储存和再分配
function.en: Collected timber, olive oil, wine, weighed silver, and craft products, then centralized storage and redistribution

function.zh: 维护城墙、武器仓库、卫兵轮值及战时动员
function.en: Maintained walls, weapon stores, guard rotations, and wartime mobilization

localAdmin.zh: 比布鲁斯直接控制城内街区、港区及其附近村落和林地；外围社区多以家族长老和王室委任的征收人管理。
localAdmin.en: Byblos directly controlled its urban quarters, harbor district, and nearby villages and timber zones; outlying communities were largely managed through clan elders and royal tax collectors.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断、神庙誓约和商业契约为基础。涉及债务、婚姻、土地、货运与伤害赔偿的纠纷多由长老、书记和王廷共同处理。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary practice, royal judgment, temple oaths, and commercial contracts. Disputes involving debt, marriage, land, cargo, and compensation for injury were usually handled by elders, scribes, and the royal court together.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物和劳役为主，包括木材配额、农产上缴、港口费、贸易抽成、船舶征发及节庆献纳；贵金属多按重量计征。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on goods and labor service, including timber quotas, agricultural dues, harbor fees, trade shares, ship levies, and festival offerings; precious metals were assessed chiefly by weight.

religion.zh: 宗教属西闪米特多神信仰体系，崇拜巴力、埃勒、巴拉特·格巴勒（比布鲁斯女主）等神灵，并与埃及宗教象征长期交流融合。
religion.en: Religion belonged to the West Semitic polytheistic sphere, venerating deities such as Baal, El, and Baalat Gebal (Lady of Byblos), while maintaining long-standing exchange and partial blending with Egyptian religious symbolism.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学传统，世界观以神意、王权神授、祖先记忆、航海经验和实用商业伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition; worldview centered on divine will, sacral kingship, ancestral memory, seafaring experience, and a practical commercial ethic.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用楔形文字与埃及象形文字影响下的行政书写传统，地方西闪米特语已在口语中发展，早期辅音字母化倾向可能在少数标记实践中出现，但尚未成为标准国家文字。
writingSystem.en: Administration mainly used traditions shaped by cuneiform and Egyptian hieroglyphic influence. The local West Semitic language was developing in speech, and early consonantal alphabetic tendencies may have appeared in limited marking practices, but had not yet become a standardized state script.

culturalAchievements.zh: 以精细木工、象牙与骨器装饰、彩绘与金属器工艺、远距离海运组织能力、神庙建筑以及在埃及—黎凡特贸易中的中介角色著称。
culturalAchievements.en: It was noted for fine woodworking, ivory and bone ornamentation, painted and metal crafts, long-distance maritime organization, temple architecture, and its intermediary role in Egypt-Levant trade.

languageFamily.zh: 西闪米特语族（西北闪米特支前期迦南语连续体）
languageFamily.en: West Semitic language family (early Northwest Semitic/Canaanite continuum)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模虽小，但因港口转运、黎巴嫩雪松木输出、奢侈品加工与对埃及贸易而具有超出人口规模的财富密度。经济以王室—神庙再分配与私人商贸并存为特征。
gdpDescription.en: Although small in total scale, the economy had a wealth density greater than its population would suggest because of port transshipment, export of Lebanese cedar, luxury craft production, and trade with Egypt. It combined palace-temple redistribution with private commerce.

mainIndustries.zh: 木材采运、造船与修船、渔业、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、陶器、金属加工、象牙与木器工艺、港口转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Timber extraction and transport, shipbuilding and repair, fishing, wine and olive oil production, pottery, metalworking, ivory and wood craftsmanship, and port entrepôt trade

tradeGoods.zh: 主要输出雪松木、木板、树脂、葡萄酒、橄榄油、工艺箱柜、珠饰与少量金属制品；输入埃及金器、亚麻、纸草、石材制品、粮食以及来自内陆的铜、锡、银与豪华原料。
tradeGoods.en: Main exports included cedar logs, timber planks, resins, wine, olive oil, crafted chests and furniture, beads, and small metal goods; imports included Egyptian gold goods, linen, papyrus, stone vessels, grain, and inland copper, tin, silver, and luxury raw materials.

name.zh: 称量白银与实物交换体系
name.en: Weighed silver and commodity exchange system

householdWealth.zh: 王室、神庙和商人家族财富集中明显；普通家庭财富主要表现为房屋、陶器、渔具、小块耕地、果树与牲畜。城市家庭比周边村落家庭拥有更多耐用品和进口器物。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was strongly concentrated in the royal household, temples, and merchant families; ordinary households held wealth chiefly in houses, pottery, fishing gear, small plots, orchard trees, and livestock. Urban households possessed more durable goods and imported items than surrounding villages.

economicSystem.zh: 宫殿—神庙再分配经济与家族商贸网络并行，价格和交换多按重量银、标准容量与长期关系协商形成。
economicSystem.en: A palace-temple redistributive economy operated alongside family-based trade networks, with prices and exchanges negotiated through weighed silver, standard measures, and long-term relationships.

source.en: Agricultural dues, wine-oil, and payments in kind

source.zh: 附庸村社劳役折值与司法罚金
source.en: Imputed labor service from dependent villages and judicial fines

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要以仓储物资、称量银、进口贵重品、木材库存和神庙奉献物形式存在，而非统一铸币。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves existed mainly as stored goods, weighed silver, imported valuables, timber stocks, and temple offerings rather than as standardized coinage.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视稳定港口收入、保障对埃及贸易、维持城防和储粮，并通过节庆与馈赠巩固王权与外部联系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing stable harbor revenue, preservation of Egyptian trade, maintenance of defenses and grain reserves, and the use of festivals and gifts to reinforce royal authority and external ties.

description.zh: 以矛、短剑、木盾为主，负责城门、城墙和王宫守卫，战时扩编为主力步兵。
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, daggers, and wooden shields, they guarded the gates, walls, and palace, and formed the core infantry in war.

name.zh: 港口水手与海上武装队
name.en: Harbor Sailors and Maritime Armed Crews

description.zh: 平时从事航运与港务，战时为商船加装武装，执行护航、巡逻和近岸突袭。
description.en: Normally engaged in shipping and harbor work, they armed merchant vessels in wartime for convoy duty, patrol, and short-range coastal raids.

description.zh: 由城民与周边村社男子临时征发，装备较简陋，适于守城、搬运和短期野战支援。
description.en: Temporarily raised from townsmen and nearby village men, they were more lightly equipped and suited for defense, transport work, and short field support.

commanderInChief.zh: 比布鲁斯国王
commanderInChief.en: The King of Byblos

technology.zh: 处于青铜时代晚中期黎凡特军事水平，使用青铜矛头、匕首、复合弓、皮甲或厚织物护具、木盾和轻型海船；尚未形成大规模战车兵。
technology.en: Military technology was typical of the mid-late Bronze Age Levant, using bronze spearheads, daggers, composite bows, leather or heavy textile armor, wooden shields, and light seagoing craft; no major chariot arm had yet formed.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边叙利亚—迦南城邦竞争、内陆山地部族袭扰、海上掠夺以及大国势力变化带来的外交压力，尤其需谨慎平衡与埃及及北方势力的关系。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival Syrian-Canaanite city-states, raids from inland highland groups, maritime predation, and diplomatic pressure created by shifts among larger powers, especially the need to balance relations with Egypt and northern states.

populationDescription.zh: 人口由比布鲁斯城内居民、港口流动人口、近郊农业村落和山区林木采运社区组成，规模在同时代黎凡特沿海城邦中属中等偏上。
populationDescription.en: The population included urban residents of Byblos, a floating harbor population, nearby agricultural villages, and upland timber-working communities, placing it in the medium-to-upper range among contemporary Levantine coastal city-states.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、祭司与大商人；中层为书记、船主、工匠、专业水手与富农；下层为小农、渔民、码头工、伐木工、佃作者及少量奴仆。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of royalty, nobles, priests, and major merchants; the middle stratum of scribes, shipowners, artisans, professional sailors, and prosperous farmers; the lower stratum of smallholders, fishermen, dockworkers, timber laborers, tenant cultivators, and a small number of servants or slaves.

allies.zh: 与埃及保持长期友好贸易关系，并同部分叙罗-迦南沿海城邦维持互市与礼物外交。
allies.en: It maintained a long-standing friendly trade relationship with Egypt and sustained market exchange and gift diplomacy with some Syro-Canaanite coastal city-states.

enemies.zh: 无固定意义上的宿敌，但与竞争港口、部分内陆首领及海上劫掠者存在周期性冲突。
enemies.en: It had no single permanent enemy, but experienced recurring tensions with rival ports, certain inland chiefs, and maritime raiders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以商业优先、向强邻示好、避免卷入大国正面战争、通过进贡、婚盟、礼物与港口服务换取自治空间为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized commerce, accommodation of stronger neighbors, avoidance of direct entanglement in great-power wars, and the securing of autonomy through tribute, marriage ties, gifts, and port services.

keyInnovations.zh: 黎凡特沿海远航经验、雪松木大型梁材加工、复合弓应用、青铜工具与武器、港口仓储管理、跨文化文书与度量衡实践
keyInnovations.en: Levantine coastal seafaring experience, processing of large cedar timbers, use of the composite bow, bronze tools and weapons, harbor warehousing management, and cross-cultural record-keeping and metrological practice

strengths.zh: 地处优良港口，拥有珍贵雪松资源，对埃及贸易根基深厚，海运与中介商业能力突出，政治结构虽小但灵活稳定。
strengths.en: It possessed an excellent harbor, access to valuable cedar resources, deeply rooted trade with Egypt, strong maritime and intermediary commercial capacity, and a small but flexible and stable political structure.

weaknesses.zh: 腹地狭小、粮食自给有限、军力不足以对抗区域大国，经济高度依赖外贸与航路安全，也容易受王位继承和商贸家族竞争影响。
weaknesses.en: Its hinterland was narrow, grain self-sufficiency limited, and military power insufficient against major regional states; the economy was highly dependent on foreign trade and secure sea lanes, and was also vulnerable to succession disputes and competition among merchant families.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后，若埃及联系持续、航运环境稳定且北方大国压力可控，比布鲁斯将继续作为黎凡特重要木材与奢侈品港口繁荣；但一旦区域霸权格局剧变，其自治与财富都可能迅速受损。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, if ties with Egypt endured, shipping conditions remained stable, and pressure from northern powers stayed manageable, Byblos would likely continue to prosper as a major Levantine timber and luxury port; however, any sharp change in regional hegemony could quickly damage both its autonomy and wealth.

description.zh: 黎巴嫩海岸古老港邦，长期参与与埃及的木材和奢侈品贸易。
description.en: An ancient Levantine port polity long engaged in timber and luxury trade with Egypt.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心、由宫廷与贵族家族共同运作的青铜时代城邦君主制
governmentForm.en: A Bronze Age city-state monarchy centered on kingship and operated jointly by the palace and leading noble families.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与宫廷贵族、祭司、书记官与行政人员、武士与战车贵族、商人与手工业者、农民、牧民以及家内奴婢和战俘构成；城乡之间联系紧密，王城统辖周边村落与属地。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal household and court nobility, priests, scribes and administrators, warriors and chariot elites, merchants and artisans, peasants, pastoralists, and household dependents including slaves and captives; the royal city closely directed surrounding villages and dependent lands.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、宫廷贵族、大神庙祭司集团与控制土地和贸易通道的显贵家族
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal family, court nobles, major temple priests, and prominent families controlling land and trade routes.

succession.zh: 通常为王族世袭，以父子继承为主；若王位有争议，宫廷贵族与宗教权威可影响继承结果。
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within the royal line, usually from father to son; when contested, court nobles and religious authorities could influence the outcome.

structure.zh: 夏琐的统治以王宫为行政中枢，王兼具军事领袖、最高裁决者与宗教赞助者身份；下设宫廷管家、仓储与贡赋官、外交使者、军事统领及地方监督者，管理上城、下城及附属村庄。
structure.en: Hazor was governed from the palace as the administrative center, with the king serving as military leader, supreme judge, and religious patron; beneath him were palace stewards, storehouse and tribute officials, diplomats, military commanders, and local overseers administering the upper city, lower city, and dependent villages.

function.zh: 管理宫廷人员、王室地产、粮食分配、接待与日常供给
function.en: Managed palace personnel, royal estates, ration distribution, hospitality, and daily provisioning.

function.zh: 征收谷物、油、酒、牲畜与手工业品，登记入库并向宫廷、神庙与军队发放
function.en: Collected grain, oil, wine, livestock, and craft goods, recorded them in storage, and issued supplies to the palace, temples, and military.

function.zh: 以阿卡德楔形文字和地方语文书处理条约、账目、外交信函与司法记录
function.en: Handled treaties, accounts, diplomatic correspondence, and legal records in Akkadian cuneiform and local administrative usage.

function.zh: 维持与埃及、叙利亚诸城、米坦尼及其他迦南城邦的往来、婚盟和礼物交换
function.en: Maintained relations, marriage ties, and gift exchange with Egypt, Syrian cities, Mitanni, and other Canaanite city-states.

function.zh: 组织王室卫队、城防守军、战车队及临时征召部队，管理武器和马匹
function.en: Organized the royal guard, city garrison, chariot corps, and levied troops, while supervising weapons and horses.

function.zh: 协调祭祀、神庙地产、祭品与节庆，巩固王权与神圣秩序
function.en: Coordinated rituals, temple estates, offerings, and festivals, reinforcing kingship and sacred order.

localAdmin.zh: 城外属地由王任命的监督者、村长和地产管理人负责；基层治理依赖地方显贵、宗族首领与神庙网络。
localAdmin.en: The outer territories were managed by royal overseers, village headmen, and estate stewards; local administration relied heavily on regional notables, clan leaders, and temple networks.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁决、赔偿原则和宗教誓约为基础；财产、婚姻、债务、伤害与贸易纠纷多由长老、官员或王庭审理。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, royal judgment, compensation principles, and sacred oaths; disputes over property, marriage, debt, injury, and trade were judged by elders, officials, or the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要通过实物贡赋与劳役征发实现，包括谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、牲畜、金属制品、木材转运收益以及战时额外征调。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was raised mainly through payments in kind and labor service, including grain, olive oil, wine, livestock, metal goods, timber transit dues, and additional wartime levies.

religion.zh: 典型西闪米特多神信仰，崇拜伊勒、巴力、亚舍拉、阿斯塔特等神祇；王权通过祭祀、誓约和圣所赞助获得合法性。
religion.en: Religion was a typical West Semitic polytheistic system venerating deities such as El, Baal, Asherah, and Astarte; kingship derived legitimacy through ritual, oath-taking, and patronage of sanctuaries.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系统哲学传统，核心观念是神意、王权秩序、家族荣誉、盟约义务与农业季节循环的神圣性。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition; central ideas revolved around divine will, royal order, family honor, covenantal obligation, and the sacred rhythm of agricultural seasons.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与外交中主要使用阿卡德语楔形文字；地方口语属于西北闪米特语支，亦可能存在少量地方性书写实践。
writingSystem.en: Akkadian cuneiform was used chiefly for administration and diplomacy; the local spoken language belonged to the Northwest Semitic branch, and limited local writing practices may also have existed.

culturalAchievements.zh: 夏琐以大型土筑防御体系、宫殿建筑、储藏设施、区域贸易网络和体现叙利亚—迦南风格融合的陶器、金属器与象牙制品而著称。
culturalAchievements.en: Hazor was noted for its massive earthen defenses, palace architecture, storage facilities, regional trade networks, and ceramics, metalwork, and ivory objects showing a blend of Syrian and Canaanite styles.

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族
languageFamily.en: Northwest Semitic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 夏琐是南黎凡特最大的城邦之一，经济以农业剩余、畜牧、手工业和转口贸易为基础。其财富不主要表现为流通货币，而是体现在粮仓、油酒储备、牲畜、青铜器、进口奢侈品和对商路的控制。
gdpDescription.en: Hazor was one of the largest city-states in the southern Levant, with an economy based on agricultural surplus, herding, craft production, and transit trade. Its wealth was expressed less in circulating money than in granaries, oil and wine reserves, livestock, bronze goods, imported luxuries, and control of trade routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 大麦与小麦种植、橄榄油与葡萄酒生产、绵羊和山羊畜牧、青铜加工、陶器制作、纺织、象牙与奢侈品再分配贸易
mainIndustries.en: Barley and wheat cultivation, olive oil and wine production, sheep and goat herding, bronze working, pottery manufacture, textiles, ivory goods, and redistribution of luxury trade items.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口或转运谷物、油、酒、羊毛、皮革、陶器与地方手工业品；输入铜、锡、贵金属、雪松木、象牙、彩陶、优质器皿与埃及、叙利亚、美索不达米亚奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: It exported or transshipped grain, oil, wine, wool, leather, pottery, and local craft goods; it imported copper, tin, precious metals, cedar, ivory, painted wares, fine vessels, and luxury goods from Egypt, Syria, and Mesopotamia.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要以土地使用权、谷物储藏、牲畜、陶器、织物和家内劳动力体现；城市精英则拥有大宅、金属器、进口器物和商路利益。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of access to land, grain stores, livestock, pottery, textiles, and household labor; urban elites possessed large residences, metal goods, imported objects, and interests in trade routes.

economicSystem.zh: 以王宫—神庙再分配、乡村贡赋和商人长途交换并存的混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining palace-temple redistribution, rural tribute, and long-distance merchant exchange.

source.zh: 附属村镇贡物与战利品
source.en: Tribute from dependent settlements and booty

category.en: Storage, water systems, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要由金银称量金属、储粮、油酒储备、青铜武器、牲畜和可供分配的进口奢侈品构成，而非大量铸币。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted chiefly of weighed gold and silver, stored grain, oil and wine reserves, bronze weapons, livestock, and imported luxury goods available for redistribution rather than coined money.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重保障王宫、神庙和军队供给，通过稳定征收实物贡赋、维护仓储与控制商路来增强城邦韧性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized provision for the palace, temples, and military, strengthening the city-state through stable collection in kind, maintenance of storage systems, and control of trade routes.

description.zh: 驻守城门、城墙和要塞的核心部队，装备矛、盾、短剑和弓。
description.en: Core troops stationed at gates, walls, and fortified points, equipped with spears, shields, short swords, and bows.

description.zh: 由王室与贵族维持的小型精锐力量，用于突击、追击和显示威望。
description.en: A small elite force maintained by the king and nobility, used for shock action, pursuit, and prestige.

description.zh: 在危机或战时从附属村庄与城市居民中征调，承担守备、运输和辅助作战。
description.en: Raised from dependent villages and towns in times of crisis or war, serving in defense, transport, and support roles.

commanderInChief.zh: 夏琐王
commanderInChief.en: King of Hazor

technology.zh: 军备处于晚期中青铜时代水平，使用青铜矛头、匕首、复合弓、简式战车、城门塔楼和夯土—砖石复合防御工事。
technology.en: Military technology was typical of the later Middle Bronze Age, using bronze spearheads, daggers, composite bows, simple chariots, gate towers, and combined earthen and masonry fortifications.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近迦南城邦竞争、北方叙利亚与米坦尼势力变化、埃及对南黎凡特的周期性干预，以及商路争夺引发的袭扰。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival Canaanite city-states, shifting power in northern Syria and Mitanni, periodic Egyptian intervention in the southern Levant, and raiding connected to competition over trade routes.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约四万，包括夏琐主城、下城居民、周边村庄农民、牧民、商旅人口及少量外来工匠和使节，是同时期南黎凡特规模最大的聚居中心之一。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 40,000, including residents of upper and lower Hazor, surrounding village farmers, pastoralists, merchants in transit, and a small number of foreign artisans and envoys, making it one of the largest population centers in the southern Levant of its time.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、祭司与富商；中层为书记、军官、工匠和较富裕农牧民；下层为普通农民、劳工、仆役与战俘。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum included the royal family, nobles, priests, and wealthy merchants; the middle stratum included scribes, officers, artisans, and better-off farmers and herders; the lower stratum consisted of ordinary peasants, laborers, servants, and captives.

allies.zh: 与部分叙利亚城邦、其他迦南城邦及在特定时期的埃及或米坦尼保持务实往来与临时结盟。
allies.en: Hazor maintained pragmatic relations and temporary alliances with some Syrian cities, other Canaanite city-states, and at times Egypt or Mitanni.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是争夺区域霸权与商路控制权的邻近城邦，以及在黎凡特扩张影响力的更大强国。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were neighboring city-states contesting regional primacy and trade routes, as well as larger powers extending influence into the Levant.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以平衡强邻、维系贸易通道、通过礼物、婚盟、使节和承认宗主关系来确保自治与繁荣为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on balancing stronger neighbors, preserving trade corridors, and securing autonomy and prosperity through gifts, marriages, envoys, and occasional acknowledgment of overlordship.

era.zh: 青铜时代中晚期过渡阶段
era.en: Middle to Late Bronze Age transition

keyInnovations.zh: 大型土木防御工程、宫殿仓储管理、青铜兵器与工具、复合弓、战车运用、区域性楔形文字行政与长距离商路组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included large-scale earthwork fortifications, palace storage administration, bronze weapons and tools, composite bows, chariot use, regional cuneiform administration, and organization of long-distance trade routes.

strengths.zh: 夏琐的优势在于人口与规模领先、地处南北商路节点、农业与畜牧基础稳固、城防坚强，并能吸收叙利亚、埃及和美索不达米亚文化与贸易资源。
strengths.en: Hazor’s strengths lay in its large population and scale, strategic position on north-south trade routes, solid agricultural and pastoral base, strong fortifications, and ability to draw on Syrian, Egyptian, and Mesopotamian cultural and trade resources.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政治结构依赖王室与地方显贵合作，容易受继承危机、外部大国干预、贸易中断和战车贵族有限兵力制约。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a political structure dependent on cooperation between the crown and local magnates, vulnerability to succession crises, intervention by great powers, disruption of trade, and the limited scale of its elite military forces.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后，夏琐正处于繁荣上升期，若能继续维持商路控制并在埃及、叙利亚与北方强权之间灵活周旋，仍可保持南黎凡特首要城邦地位；但地区权力格局一旦剧变，其独立性将面临严峻考验。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, Hazor was in a phase of growing prosperity; if it could retain control of trade routes and maneuver flexibly among Egypt, Syria, and northern powers, it could preserve its position as a leading southern Levantine city-state, though any major regional power shift would place its independence under severe strain.

description.zh: 南黎凡特最大的迦南城市之一，在区域贸易与政治中地位突出。
description.en: One of the largest Canaanite cities in the southern Levant, prominent in regional trade and politics.

capital.zh: 示剑、耶路撒冷及其他城邦中心
capital.en: Shechem, Jerusalem, and other city-state centers

governmentForm.zh: 分散的城邦君主制，受区域霸权与外交宗主关系影响
governmentForm.en: Fragmented city-state monarchy shaped by regional hegemony and diplomatic overlordship

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、祭司、书记官、商人、工匠、农民、佃户、城内劳役者与奴隶构成，城市与乡村联系紧密。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of royal households and nobles, priests, scribes, merchants, artisans, farmers, tenant cultivators, urban laborers, and slaves, with close ties between cities and countryside.

rulingClass.zh: 各城邦王室、武装贵族家族、宫廷官员与大神庙祭司集团
rulingClass.en: Royal families of the city-states, armed noble lineages, palace officials, and major temple priesthoods

succession.zh: 通常为王室世袭，但在王位争夺、外部干预或贵族支持变化时常出现兄弟继承、旁支继承或篡夺。
succession.en: Succession was usually dynastic within royal families, but rival brothers, collateral branches, or usurpers could prevail when elite support shifted or outside powers intervened.

structure.zh: 迦南并无统一中央政权，而是由多个设有宫殿、神庙和城防的城邦组成。每个城邦由国王统治，下设宫廷书记官、税吏、军官和宗教官员，并控制周边村庄、耕地与道路关卡。
structure.en: Canaan lacked a single central state and was instead divided among numerous fortified city-states with palaces, temples, and defenses. Each city-state was ruled by a king and staffed by palace scribes, tax collectors, military officers, and religious officials, controlling surrounding villages, farmland, and road checkpoints.

function.zh: 管理王室地产、仓储、劳役调配、贡赋记录与宫廷日常事务
function.en: Managed royal estates, storage, labor assignments, tribute records, and the daily affairs of the court

function.zh: 主持祭祀、节庆、供品征收、神庙土地管理与占卜仪式
function.en: Oversaw ritual worship, festivals, collection of offerings, temple lands, and divinatory ceremonies

function.zh: 征收谷物、油、酒、牲畜和手工业品，并管理城邦仓库
function.en: Collected grain, oil, wine, livestock, and manufactured goods, and managed city-state storehouses

function.zh: 组织守军、战车贵族、动员民兵并维护城墙与城门
function.en: Organized garrisons, chariot elites, militia mobilization, and maintenance of walls and gates

function.zh: 监管商旅、征收过路费、保护商道并管理对外交换
function.en: Regulated merchants, collected tolls, protected trade routes, and supervised external exchange

localAdmin.zh: 城邦直接管辖城内居民与邻近村社，乡村由村长、地主代理人、守备官或王室任命的监督者管理；边缘地区常通过盟约、贡纳或驻军控制。
localAdmin.en: City-states directly governed urban inhabitants and nearby village communities, while rural areas were managed by headmen, estate agents, garrison officers, or royal overseers; peripheral zones were often controlled through treaties, tribute, or stationed troops.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命、赔偿原则和神誓裁决为基础，涉及土地、水源、婚姻、债务、人身伤害与贸易纠纷；审判由国王、长老、官员或神庙权威主持。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary practice, royal decree, compensation principles, and oath-based divine adjudication, covering land, water, marriage, debt, personal injury, and trade disputes; judgment was issued by kings, elders, officials, or temple authorities.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物为主，包括谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、牲畜、纺织品和劳役；商道过境税和附庸贡赋亦十分重要。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind, including grain, olive oil, wine, livestock, textiles, and labor service; tolls on caravan routes and tribute from dependents were also important.

religion.zh: 迦南宗教为多神信仰，崇拜埃尔、巴力、阿舍拉、阿斯塔蒂、阿纳特等神祇。宗教与农时、王权、战争和城邦保护神紧密相连，神庙祭祀、供物与高地圣所极为重要。
religion.en: Canaanite religion was polytheistic, venerating deities such as El, Baal, Asherah, Astarte, and Anat. Religion was closely tied to agriculture, kingship, warfare, and city patron gods, with temple ritual, offerings, and high-place sanctuaries of major importance.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世意义上的系统哲学，世界观主要体现为神意、王权合法性、祖先传统、契约义务与农业周期之间的平衡。
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophy existed in the later sense; the worldview was expressed through divine will, royal legitimacy, ancestral tradition, obligations of covenant, and the balance of agricultural cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 书写主要使用楔形文字进行外交通讯和行政记录，在部分地区也使用地方化的音节或原始字母性标记；书写语言常受阿卡德语外交传统影响，同时反映本地西北闪米特语。
writingSystem.en: Writing primarily used cuneiform for diplomacy and administration, while some areas also employed local syllabic or proto-alphabetic markings; written language was often shaped by the Akkadian diplomatic tradition while reflecting local Northwest Semitic speech.

culturalAchievements.zh: 迦南人在城防建筑、宫殿和神庙布局、青铜器制作、彩绘与抛光陶器、象牙与金属工艺、宗教诗歌传统以及连接埃及、叙利亚与美索不达米亚的中介贸易方面成就突出。
culturalAchievements.en: The Canaanites excelled in fortification architecture, palace and temple planning, bronze production, painted and burnished pottery, ivory and metal craftsmanship, religious poetic traditions, and intermediary trade linking Egypt, Syria, and Mesopotamia.

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族
languageFamily.en: Northwest Semitic

gdpDescription.zh: 迦南经济以集约农业、葡萄与橄榄种植、畜牧、陶器与金属手工业及转口贸易为核心。由于地处埃及与叙利亚—美索不达米亚之间，若干城邦凭借山道、谷地和海陆节点获取显著贸易收益，但整体仍是以农业剩余驱动的青铜时代区域经济。
gdpDescription.en: The Canaanite economy centered on intensive agriculture, viticulture and olive cultivation, herding, pottery and metal crafts, and transit trade. Because Canaan lay between Egypt and Syria-Mesopotamia, several city-states earned notable profits from mountain routes, valleys, and land-sea nodes, though the economy as a whole remained a Bronze Age regional system driven by agricultural surplus.

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食种植、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、牧羊与养山羊、陶器制造、青铜器与武器铸造、木材与石材加工、染织、商旅转运
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, wine and olive oil production, sheep and goat herding, pottery manufacture, bronze tools and weapon casting, timber and stone working, textiles, and caravan brokerage

tradeGoods.zh: 橄榄油、葡萄酒、谷物、陶器、青铜制品、木材、沥青、染织品、乳香类转口货、埃及制品、叙利亚与美索不达米亚奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Olive oil, wine, grain, pottery, bronze goods, timber, bitumen, textiles, incense transit goods, Egyptian products, and Syrian and Mesopotamian luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、谷仓储备、牲畜、橄榄树与葡萄园、金属器具和家族劳动力。城市精英拥有更高比例的金银、进口器物和战车装备。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was chiefly expressed in land-use rights, grain stores, livestock, olive trees and vineyards, metal tools, and family labor. Urban elites held higher shares of silver, prestige imports, and chariot equipment.

economicSystem.zh: 宫殿—神庙再分配与市场交换并存的混合经济，王室控制关键仓储、土地和外交贸易节点，民间村社与商人网络负责大量日常生产与流通。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining palace-temple redistribution with market exchange, in which royal institutions controlled key storage, lands, and diplomatic trade nodes, while village communities and merchant networks handled much everyday production and circulation.

source.zh: 贸易关税与道路过境费

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散存放于王宫仓库、神庙宝库和精英家族库存中，以粮食、油、酒、金银称量金属、武器和牲畜形式存在；其稳定性受收成与战争影响很大。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were distributed across palace granaries, temple treasuries, and elite household stores, held as grain, oil, wine, weighed precious metal, weapons, and livestock; their stability depended heavily on harvests and warfare.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持城墙、守军、祭祀正统和商路安全，在丰年积储、歉年调仓，并通过附庸贡纳和外交馈赠维持区域平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining walls, garrisons, cultic legitimacy, and route security, storing surpluses in good years, releasing supplies in poor ones, and using tribute and diplomatic gifts to preserve regional balance.

description.zh: 驻守城墙、城门和要塞的常备与半常备步兵，装备矛、盾、短剑和投射武器。
description.en: Standing and semi-standing infantry stationed on walls, gates, and strongholds, armed with spears, shields, short swords, and missile weapons.

description.zh: 由农民和附属村社战时征发，适合短期防御、护送和围城支援。
description.en: Raised from farmers and subordinate villages in wartime, suitable for short-term defense, escort duty, and siege support.

description.zh: 由王室与贵族维持的精锐机动作战力量，兼具冲击、指挥和威望象征功能。
description.en: An elite mobile force maintained by royal and noble households, serving shock, command, and prestige functions.

description.zh: 主要用于守城、伏击山地道路和支援步兵阵线。
description.en: Used chiefly for defense of walls, ambushes in hill routes, and support of infantry lines.

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: Each City King as Commander-in-Chief

technology.zh: 采用中青铜时代晚期军事技术，包括复合弓、青铜矛头与剑、皮木盾、简易攻城设施、夯土与石砌城墙，以及轻型双轮战车。
technology.en: Military technology of the later Middle Bronze Age included composite bows, bronze spearheads and swords, leather-and-wood shields, simple siege devices, earthen and stone fortifications, and light two-wheeled chariots.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自城邦间冲突、北方叙利亚与米坦尼势力扩张、内陆游动群体袭扰，以及埃及在南部与沿海地区重新加强干预的可能。
threats.en: Major threats came from warfare among rival city-states, expansion of northern Syrian and Mitanni influence, raids by mobile inland groups, and the possibility of renewed Egyptian intervention in the south and along the coast.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于山地城镇、谷地农庄、沿海道路节点和若干区域中心，城邦规模差异很大。多数居民生活在依附城市的农村网络中。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed among hill-country towns, valley farmsteads, coastal route nodes, and a number of regional centers, with wide variation in city-state size. Most inhabitants lived in rural networks dependent on urban strongholds.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、祭司与战车武士；中层为书记官、商人、地主、工匠首领；下层为小农、佃户、牧民、劳役者与奴隶。不同城邦之间身份流动有限但存在通过军功、商业或宫廷任职而上升的机会。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum comprised royal families, nobles, priests, and chariot warriors; the middle included scribes, merchants, landholders, and master artisans; the lower included small farmers, tenants, pastoralists, laborers, and slaves. Social mobility was limited between city-states but advancement through military service, trade, or court office was possible.

allies.zh: 无持久统一联盟；各城邦根据局势与叙利亚城邦、沿海航海城邦、埃及地方官或邻近迦南诸王结成临时同盟。
allies.en: There was no enduring unified alliance; city-states formed temporary partnerships according to circumstance with Syrian cities, coastal maritime polities, Egyptian authorities, or neighboring Canaanite kings.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是竞争性迦南城邦、控制商路的区域强权，以及试图掠夺边地资源的游动部落或外来军队。
enemies.en: Enemies were chiefly rival Canaanite city-states, regional powers seeking control of trade routes, and mobile tribes or invading armies aiming to seize frontier resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以现实主义为特征，核心目标是保全自治、维持商路、争夺水源与耕地，并在埃及、叙利亚和北方大国之间灵活周旋。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was strongly pragmatic, aimed at preserving autonomy, maintaining trade routes, competing for water and arable land, and maneuvering flexibly between Egypt, Syria, and northern great powers.

keyInnovations.zh: 巨型土石复合城防、坡道与城门体系、青铜铸造、复合弓、战车使用、灌溉与梯田农业、储藏与再分配仓储制度，以及与原始字母发展相关的早期书写实验。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included massive earth-and-stone fortifications, ramp and gate systems, bronze casting, composite bows, chariot use, irrigation and terraced agriculture, storage and redistributive granary systems, and early writing experiments linked to proto-alphabetic development.

strengths.zh: 迦南文明的优势在于地理位置优越、商路密集、城防坚固、农业与园艺多样化，并具备连接埃及与近东各大文化圈的中介能力。
strengths.en: The strengths of Canaanite civilization lay in its strategic location, dense trade routes, strong fortifications, diversified agriculture and horticulture, and its intermediary role linking Egypt with the wider Near East.

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是政治高度分裂、城邦彼此竞争、对外部强权压力缺乏统一应对，以及资源与人口规模不足以支持长期大规模战争。
weaknesses.en: Its chief weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, rivalry among city-states, lack of unified response to outside great powers, and insufficient resources and population for prolonged large-scale war.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后，迦南整体处于相对稳定且富于活力的阶段，但其未来将持续受埃及复兴、北方军事强权变化与地区商路重组所左右。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, Canaan as a whole was in a relatively stable and dynamic phase, but its future would continue to be shaped by Egyptian resurgence, shifts in northern military powers, and reorganization of regional trade routes.

description.zh: 巴勒斯坦与约旦河流域散布着众多有城墙的迦南城邦。
description.en: Palestine and the Jordan Valley were dotted with numerous fortified Canaanite city-states.

name.zh: 西亚安纳托利亚卢维文明（阿尔扎瓦王国）
name.en: Western Anatolian Luwian Civilization (Kingdom of Arzawa)

ruler.zh: 阿尔扎瓦国王（具体王名未可靠见于前1600年文献）
ruler.en: King of Arzawa (personal name not securely attested for c. 1600 BCE)

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心的地区性青铜时代君主制，依赖宫廷、地方贵族与附属城邦网络治理
governmentForm.en: A Bronze Age regional monarchy centered on kingship, governed through the court, local nobles, and a network of subordinate towns and polities

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、军事贵族、祭司、宫廷文书与工匠、自由农牧民、依附民及奴婢构成；沿海与河谷定居点较分层，内陆山区保留较强部族性
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal household, military nobles, priests, palace scribes and artisans, free farmers and herders, dependent laborers, and slaves; stratification was stronger in coastal and riverine settlements, while inland highlands retained stronger tribal features

rulingClass.zh: 阿尔扎瓦王族、宫廷贵族、战车武士首领、主要圣所祭司与地方城邦领主
rulingClass.en: The Arzawan royal family, court nobility, chariot-warrior leaders, priests of major sanctuaries, and local town lords

succession.zh: 以父系世袭为主，通常由王子继位，但需获得王室核心贵族与地方精英承认，危机时可能出现支系争位
succession.en: Succession was primarily patrilineal and usually passed to a son, but required recognition by core royal nobles and provincial elites; collateral claimants could emerge in times of crisis

structure.zh: 中央政权规模有限，以国王宫廷为核心；王室通过宫廷书记、税役监督、军队首领与地方统治者管理农业腹地、港口与山地通道
structure.en: The central state was limited in scale and centered on the royal court; the crown governed agricultural hinterlands, ports, and mountain corridors through palace scribes, tax-and-labor overseers, military chiefs, and local rulers

function.zh: 管理王室起居、粮仓、宫廷工坊、外交接待与王令传达
function.en: Managed the royal household, granaries, palace workshops, diplomatic reception, and transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、纺织品与金属贡赋，并储备王室与军队物资
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, textiles, and metal tribute and maintained supplies for the royal household and army

function.zh: 组织战车兵、步兵、守备与征召，维护武器、马匹与边境警戒
function.en: Organized chariotry, infantry, garrisons, and levies, and maintained weapons, horses, and frontier watch

function.zh: 协调重要祭祀、管理神庙财产、祭品与节庆仪式，强化王权合法性
function.en: Coordinated major rites, managed temple property, offerings, and festivals, and reinforced royal legitimacy

function.zh: 监管海岸贸易点、通道、渡口及对外商旅往来
function.en: Supervised coastal trade points, roads, crossings, and foreign merchant movement

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由附属城镇首领、乡里长老与王室派驻监督共同构成；沿海中心较受王室控制，山区与边缘地带多采取间接统治
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined subordinate town chiefs, village elders, and royal supervisors; coastal centers were more directly controlled, while uplands and peripheral zones were often governed indirectly

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断、赔偿原则与圣所誓约为基础；杀伤、盗窃、土地纠纷、婚姻与贡役义务多通过赔偿、宣誓与仲裁解决
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary practice, royal judgment, compensation principles, and sanctuary oaths; injury, theft, land disputes, marriage matters, and tribute obligations were commonly settled through compensation, oath-taking, and arbitration

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡赋和劳役为主，内容包括谷物、酒油、牲畜、羊毛布匹、金属与运输服务；贸易节点还征收通行与停泊性收费
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on payment in kind and labor service, including grain, wine and oil, livestock, woolen textiles, metal, and transport obligations; trade nodes also charged transit and anchorage dues

religion.zh: 多神信仰，崇拜风暴神、太阳女神、山川泉水神与地方守护神；王权通过祭祀与神圣誓约维系政治秩序
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on storm gods, a solar goddess, deities of mountains, rivers, and springs, and local tutelary gods; kingship upheld political order through sacrifice and sacred oaths

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学，世界观以神意、祖先记忆、王权正当性、契约誓言与自然神圣性为核心
philosophy.en: No formal philosophy had developed; worldview was grounded in divine will, ancestral memory, royal legitimacy, oath-bound agreement, and the sacred character of nature

writingSystem.zh: 在约前1600年，书写使用有限；可能接触并局部采用安纳托利亚与叙利亚体系中的楔形文字作外交或记账用途，卢维圣书体尚未广泛见于正式国家行政
writingSystem.en: Writing was limited around 1600 BCE; cuneiform from Anatolian and Syrian traditions may have been known and used in restricted diplomatic or accounting contexts, while Luwian hieroglyphic usage was not yet broadly visible in formal state administration

culturalAchievements.zh: 西安纳托利亚宫廷与地方中心发展出兼具安纳托利亚和爱琴交流特色的青铜器、纺织、木石建筑、祭祀传统与精英战车文化；其沿海位置促进了与爱琴诸岛和内陆的文化中介作用
culturalAchievements.en: Western Anatolian courts and local centers developed bronze working, textiles, timber-and-stone architecture, ritual traditions, and elite chariot culture shaped by both Anatolian and Aegean interaction; their coastal position made them important cultural intermediaries between the Aegean and inland Anatolia

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系安纳托利亚语族卢维语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Anatolian branch, Luwian subgroup

unit.en: deben of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、牧业和宫廷再分配为基础，辅以沿海贸易、金属加工和交通中介收入；整体属于地区性中等强度青铜时代王国经济，财富集中于宫廷、神庙和贵族家庭
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, herding, and palace redistribution, supplemented by coastal trade, metalworking, and transit income; overall it was a medium-intensity regional Bronze Age kingdom economy, with wealth concentrated in the palace, sanctuaries, and noble households

mainIndustries.zh: 大麦与小麦种植、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、羊毛纺织、牧马养牛、青铜铸造、木材采运、陶器、沿海转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Barley and wheat cultivation, wine and olive oil production, wool textiles, horse and cattle husbandry, bronze casting, timber extraction, pottery, and coastal transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 输出谷物、羊毛布、皮革、木材、陶器、金属制品、酒与油；输入铜、锡、奢侈品、精制器皿、珠饰、象牙及部分爱琴工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included grain, woolen cloth, leather, timber, pottery, metal goods, wine, and oil; imports included copper, tin, luxury goods, fine vessels, beads, ivory, and some Aegean crafted products

name.en: Weighed silver and payments in kind

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、牲畜、谷仓余粮、织机与家庭器具；精英财富则集中于马匹、战车、金属器、珠宝、奴仆和大宗储藏
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in access to fields, livestock, stored grain, looms, and domestic equipment; elite wealth was concentrated in horses, chariots, metal vessels, jewelry, servants, and large storerooms

unit.en: deben of silver equivalent per year
deben of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 宫廷再分配与地方家户生产并存的混合经济，实物征收、劳役动员和有限银重估值共同运作
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining palace redistribution with local household production, operating through collection in kind, labor mobilization, and limited valuation in weighed silver

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以储粮、牲畜、织物、青铜器材和部分称量银共同构成，而非纯粹铸币金库；战时动员能力依赖仓储储备多于贵金属储备
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted chiefly of stored grain, livestock, textiles, bronze stock, and some weighed silver rather than a coin-based hoard; wartime mobilization depended more on granary reserves than on precious metal alone

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持储备、避免压榨核心农区，并以对附属地和贸易通道的控制来补充王室收入
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing reserves, avoiding overburdening core farming districts, and supplementing royal income through control of subordinate territories and trade corridors

description.zh: 由贵族战士与驾御者组成，是王国最精锐的机动力量，用于冲击、指挥和威慑
description.en: Composed of noble warriors and drivers, this was the kingdom's elite mobile arm for shock action, command presence, and intimidation

description.zh: 装备矛、盾、短剑与部分弓箭，负责守城、随军和地方治安
description.en: Equipped with spears, shields, daggers, and some bows, they handled town defense, campaign support, and local security

description.zh: 由乡村与附属地临时征发，战斗力不均，但适合守备、补给与山地通道警戒
description.en: Temporarily raised from villages and subordinate districts, these troops varied in quality but were useful for garrison duty, supply work, and guarding mountain routes

description.zh: 负责海岸瞭望、港口防卫、侦察与快速袭扰
description.en: Responsible for coastal watch, harbor defense, reconnaissance, and rapid raiding

commanderInChief.zh: 阿尔扎瓦国王亲自统军，辅以王族与贵族将领
commanderInChief.en: The King of Arzawa personally commanded, assisted by royal kinsmen and noble captains

technology.zh: 典型中期青铜时代水平，使用青铜矛头、剑、匕首、复合弓、木制盾牌、双轮战车及马匹牵引；攻城技术有限，作战更重机动、突袭与边境战
technology.en: Typical Middle Bronze Age technology using bronze spearheads, swords, daggers, composite bows, wooden shields, two-wheeled chariots, and horse traction; siege methods were limited, and warfare emphasized mobility, raids, and frontier fighting

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自东部崛起的赫梯扩张、邻近卢维或特洛伊系政体的竞争，以及沿海与山地部族性袭扰；若赫梯内部稳定，阿尔扎瓦将面临更大军事压力
threats.en: The main threats came from Hittite expansion to the east, rivalry with neighboring Luwian or Trojan-related polities, and raiding from coastal and upland groups; if the Hittite realm stabilized internally, Arzawa would face greater military pressure

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于凯斯特罗斯河流域、沿海平原、若干山间盆地和交通节点，整体密度中等，兼具定居农业与山区牧养特征
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the Cayster valley, coastal plains, several inland basins, and transport nodes, with moderate density and a mix of settled farming and upland pastoralism

socialClasses.zh: 王族与高等贵族、战车武士与祭司、宫廷工匠与书记、自由农民与牧民、附属民、奴婢和战俘构成主要社会层次
socialClasses.en: The main social strata were the royal family and high nobility, chariot warriors and priests, palace artisans and scribes, free farmers and herders, dependent populations, and slaves or captives

allies.zh: 可能与部分西安纳托利亚城邦、沿海卢维诸政体及部分爱琴海贸易伙伴保持松散联盟或互利关系，但联盟多具临时性
allies.en: Arzawa likely maintained loose alliances or mutually beneficial relations with some western Anatolian towns, coastal Luwian polities, and certain Aegean trade partners, though such alignments were often temporary

enemies.zh: 最主要对手是赫梯文明；在边缘地带也可能与特洛伊周边势力、内陆诸侯及不稳定部族发生冲突
enemies.en: Its principal rival was the Hittite civilization; it could also clash on the margins with powers around Troy, inland lords, and unstable tribal groups

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持西安纳托利亚自主地位、控制通往爱琴海的贸易与交通、并通过联姻、贡礼和有限军事行动制衡赫梯为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving western Anatolian autonomy, controlling routes to the Aegean, and balancing the Hittites through marriage ties, gift exchange, and limited military action

keyInnovations.zh: 双轮战车使用、青铜兵器与工具、复合弓、宫廷仓储管理、区域道路与港口组织、羊毛纺织和油酒生产技术
keyInnovations.en: Use of two-wheeled chariots, bronze weapons and tools, composite bows, palace-based storage management, regional road and port organization, wool textile production, and oil and wine processing

strengths.zh: 地处西安纳托利亚交通与海陆交换节点，拥有较稳固的农业基础、活跃的沿海贸易、可动员的地方贵族武力，以及作为赫梯西方对手的重要战略地位
strengths.en: It occupied key western Anatolian land-sea exchange routes, possessed a solid agricultural base, active coastal trade, mobilizable noble military power, and major strategic importance as a western rival to the Hittites

weaknesses.zh: 国家整合度有限，地方性强，行政与书写体系不如赫梯成熟；军力依赖贵族与征召，长期大战和攻城能力较弱，容易受王位纷争和附属地离心影响
weaknesses.en: State integration was limited and strongly local; administration and writing were less developed than those of the Hittites; military strength depended on nobles and levies, making prolonged war and siege operations difficult, while succession disputes and centrifugal tendencies among subordinate areas posed chronic risks

outlook.zh: 在前1600年前后，阿尔扎瓦处于相对稳定并具区域影响力的阶段；若能维持沿海贸易和地方联盟，可继续作为西安纳托利亚强国存在，但随着赫梯国家重整和扩张，其长期独立前景将承受越来越大压力
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, Arzawa stood in a relatively stable and regionally influential phase; if it maintained coastal trade and local alliances, it could continue as a major western Anatolian power, but its long-term independence would come under increasing pressure as the Hittite state recovered and expanded

description.zh: 安纳托利亚西部的卢维亚王国，是赫梯的重要对手之一。
description.en: A western Anatolian Luwian kingdom and one of the important rivals of the Hittites.

governmentForm.zh: 以大王为核心的世袭君主制，结合宫廷、贵族议事传统与附庸体系
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy centered on the Great King, combined with palace administration, aristocratic consultative traditions, and a vassal system

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与高贵族、战车贵族与军事随从、地方领主与祭司、自由民农牧者、工匠与商人、依附劳役者及奴隶构成；王权依靠宗教合法性、土地控制与军事掠夺维系统治。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and high nobility, chariot aristocrats and military retainers, local lords and priests, free farming and pastoral households, artisans and merchants, and dependent laborers and slaves; royal power rested on religious legitimacy, control of land, and military conquest.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、宫廷贵族、军政首领及掌握神庙与要塞的地方精英
rulingClass.en: The royal family, court nobility, military-administrative chiefs, and local elites controlling temples and fortresses

succession.zh: 原则上由王室男性继承，但在古赫梯时期常受宫廷斗争、贵族支持与军事实力影响，继位并非总是稳定。
succession.en: In principle succession passed through the royal male line, but in the Old Hittite period it was often shaped by court struggle, noble backing, and military strength, making succession far from fully stable.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕哈图沙王宫组织，由大王统领王室、军事、司法与宗教事务；王后在祭祀中地位重要，贵族会议传统仍保留影响；地方通过总督、城堡长官和附庸王管理。
structure.en: The central state was organized around the royal palace at Hattusa, with the Great King overseeing royal, military, judicial, and religious affairs; the queen held major ritual importance, aristocratic council traditions still retained influence, and provinces were managed through governors, fortress commanders, and vassal rulers.

function.zh: 负责泥板文书、条约、敕令、贡赋记录、王室通信与库存登记
function.en: Handled cuneiform tablets, treaties, decrees, tribute records, royal correspondence, and inventory registration

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金属与劳役，管理王室仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and labor dues, and managed royal storage and redistribution

function.zh: 组织战车兵、马匹、青铜武器、要塞补给与行军征发
function.en: Organized chariot forces, horses, bronze weapons, fortress supplies, and campaign mobilization

function.zh: 主持国家祭典、管理神庙地产、祭司系统与王权宗教合法性
function.en: Supervised state rituals, temple lands, priestly systems, and the religious legitimacy of kingship

function.zh: 维护边境据点、道路、驿传、桥渡及地方驻军联络
function.en: Maintained frontier strongholds, roads, relay communications, crossings, and coordination with local garrisons

localAdmin.zh: 哈图沙直接控制核心安纳托利亚地区，地方由城堡长官、区域总督、王族成员或受控附庸管理；叙利亚方向则通过军事驻防和臣属城邦施加影响。
localAdmin.en: Hattusa directly controlled core central Anatolia, while local administration was carried out by fortress commanders, regional governors, royal relatives, or subordinate vassals; in Syria, influence was exercised through military garrisons and dependent city-states.

legalSystem.zh: 赫梯法律以习惯法、王室敕令与赔偿原则为基础，相较部分近东政体在许多罪行上较少使用极端肉刑，更强调罚金、赔偿、身份等级与实际损失。
legalSystem.en: Hittite law rested on customary norms, royal decrees, and compensation principles; compared with some Near Eastern states it often relied less on extreme bodily punishment for many offenses, emphasizing fines, restitution, social rank, and actual damage.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自谷物、牲畜、金属、手工业制品和劳役征发，附庸地区另纳贡赋、战利品和军役；税收多以实物与劳役形式而非统一铸币缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue came mainly from grain, livestock, metals, craft goods, and labor obligations, while vassal regions added tribute, booty, and military service; taxes were paid mostly in kind and labor rather than in standardized coinage.

religion.zh: 赫梯宗教为多神信仰，吸纳哈梯、卢维亚、胡里安及美索不达米亚传统，常被称为“千神之国”；风暴神、太阳女神及地方山川神灵均受崇拜，国王兼具祭祀责任。
religion.en: Hittite religion was polytheistic and absorbed Hattic, Luwian, Hurrian, and Mesopotamian traditions, giving rise to the famous idea of the 'land of a thousand gods'; the Storm God, Sun Goddess, and many local deities of mountains and rivers were worshiped, and the king bore major ritual duties.

philosophy.zh: 尚无独立成体系的哲学传统，思想核心体现为王权神授、誓约神圣、秩序维护、祖先传统与对神意的占卜解释。
philosophy.en: There was no separate formal philosophical tradition; the core worldview centered on divinely sanctioned kingship, the sacred nature of oaths, maintenance of order, ancestral custom, and divinatory interpretation of divine will.

writingSystem.zh: 国家行政主要使用楔形文字书写赫梯语，同时保留本地象形记号传统；外交与文书实践明显受美索不达米亚影响。
writingSystem.en: State administration primarily used cuneiform to write the Hittite language, while local hieroglyphic traditions also persisted; diplomatic and documentary practice was strongly influenced by Mesopotamia.

culturalAchievements.zh: 哈图沙宫殿与城防建设、王室档案传统、法律文书、安纳托利亚与叙利亚文化融合、战车军事文化以及多语文书管理是其主要成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included the palace and fortification building of Hattusa, the royal archival tradition, legal texts, the fusion of Anatolian and Syrian cultures, chariot-based military culture, and multilingual record keeping.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系安纳托利亚语族，以赫梯语为王室行政语言，并与卢维亚语、哈梯语、胡里安语并存
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Anatolian branch, with Hittite as the royal administrative language alongside Luwian, Hattic, and Hurrian

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、王室再分配和战争掠夺为主，辅以安纳托利亚金属资源、木材与叙利亚贸易通道收益。以青铜时代国家标准衡量，赫梯王国属于高动员、高征发能力的强国型经济。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, herding, royal redistribution, and war booty, supplemented by Anatolian metal resources, timber, and access to Syrian trade routes. By Bronze Age state standards, the Hittite kingdom had a strong economy with high mobilization and extraction capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、绵羊与牛马畜牧、青铜冶铸、木材采集、战车制造、陶器、纺织、王室仓储与军需后勤
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, sheep and cattle-horse pastoralism, bronze metallurgy, timber extraction, chariot manufacture, pottery, textiles, royal storage, and military logistics

tradeGoods.zh: 铜、银、铅、木材、羊毛织物、皮革、马匹、谷物、陶器，以及经叙利亚流通的锡、奢侈品与手工业制品
tradeGoods.en: Copper, silver, lead, timber, wool textiles, leather, horses, grain, pottery, and, through Syria, tin, luxury goods, and crafted products

name.zh: 金属称量与实物贡赋体系
name.en: Weighed Metal and Commodity Tribute System

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、牲畜、谷仓储备、工具与家内劳动力；精英财富则表现为地产、战利品、金属储藏、战车与附庸控制权。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly expressed in access to land, livestock, grain stores, tools, and household labor; elite wealth appeared in estates, war booty, metal reserves, chariots, and control over dependents.

economicSystem.zh: 以宫廷—神庙—地方领主再分配体系为核心，结合农村自给生产、劳役征发、附庸贡纳与跨区域交换的青铜时代王国经济
economicSystem.en: A Bronze Age kingdom economy centered on palace-temple-lordly redistribution, combined with village subsistence production, labor obligations, vassal tribute, and long-distance exchange

source.zh: 农业实物税与仓储征收
source.en: Agricultural in-kind taxes and granary levies

source.zh: 矿产、工坊与金属称量收入

category.zh: 仓储、道路与地方要塞维护
category.en: Storage, roads, and provincial fortress maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以粮仓储备、牲畜、青铜器、贵金属称量储藏和战利品构成，不是后世意义上的现金库；在长期远征后，战利品可显著补充王室资源。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of stored grain, livestock, bronze goods, weighed precious metals, and booty, rather than a later-style cash fund; after major campaigns, captured wealth could significantly replenish royal resources.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在保证军粮、战车、王室祭祀与边疆据点运转，通过提高附庸贡纳和征发劳役支撑扩张，但过度远征会加大补给压力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on securing provisions for armies, chariots, royal cult, and frontier strongholds, using increased tribute and labor mobilization to support expansion, though excessive campaigning raised logistical strain.

description.zh: 由王室与贵族主导的精锐突击力量，依赖马匹、战车工匠和训练有素的乘员，是赫梯远征叙利亚时的核心兵种。
description.en: An elite striking arm dominated by the royal house and nobility, dependent on horses, chariot craftsmen, and trained crews; it was the key force in Hittite campaigns into Syria.

description.zh: 构成军队主体，装备矛、盾、斧、短剑和弓箭，用于围城、守备、道路控制与大规模会战支援。
description.en: These formed the main body of the army, equipped with spears, shields, axes, short swords, and bows, and were used for sieges, garrison duty, road control, and support in major battles.

description.zh: 部署于安纳托利亚山地通道与叙利亚方向据点，兼有地方附庸提供的部队，机动性和忠诚度不如王室核心军。
description.en: Stationed along Anatolian mountain routes and Syrian strongholds, including troops supplied by local vassals; they were less mobile and less reliable than the royal core forces.

commanderInChief.zh: 大王穆尔西里一世
commanderInChief.en: Great King Mursili I

technology.zh: 军队采用青铜武器、复合弓、长矛、盾牌、攻城器具与双马战车；赫梯在安纳托利亚高地环境下发展出适合长程陆上作战的组织能力，但此时尚未达到后期帝国时代的极盛水平。
technology.en: The army used bronze weapons, composite bows, spears, shields, siege equipment, and two-horse chariots; in the Anatolian highland environment the Hittites developed strong organization for long overland campaigns, though this was not yet the full peak of the later empire.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自叙利亚城邦联盟、胡里安—米坦尼势力的兴起、巴比伦及北方或西部安纳托利亚边疆不稳；远征巴比伦虽显军威，但也可能造成王国核心地带兵力空虚。
threats.en: Main threats came from Syrian city-state coalitions, the rise of Hurrian-Mitanni power, Babylon, and instability on the northern and western Anatolian frontiers; campaigns as far as Babylon demonstrated strength but also risked leaving the core kingdom exposed.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约五十万，主要分布于中安纳托利亚高原的农业村落、要塞聚落和王室领地；叙利亚方向存在受控或暂时臣属的人口资源，但整合程度有限。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 500,000, concentrated in agricultural villages, fortified settlements, and royal lands across the central Anatolian plateau; to the south in Syria there were controlled or temporarily subordinate populations, but they were only partially integrated.

socialClasses.zh: 顶层为王室与大贵族，其下是战车贵族、军官、祭司和地方长官；中层为自由民农户、牧民、工匠和商旅；底层包括附属劳役者、战俘和奴隶。社会身份会影响法律赔偿标准、服役义务与资源分配。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family and great nobles, followed by chariot aristocrats, officers, priests, and local officials; the middle strata consisted of free peasant households, herders, artisans, and merchants; the lower strata included dependent laborers, war captives, and slaves. Social rank affected legal compensation, service obligations, and access to resources.

allies.zh: 稳定盟友较少，更多依靠臣属城邦、阶段性附庸和婚姻政治；在叙利亚的部分地方统治者会在赫梯军威下暂时归附。
allies.en: There were few stable allies; the kingdom relied more on subordinate city-states, temporary vassals, and dynastic politics, while some Syrian rulers submitted under Hittite military pressure.

enemies.zh: 巴比伦、部分叙利亚城邦、胡里安势力以及潜在的米坦尼竞争者构成主要敌对或竞争对象；西部与北部安纳托利亚也存在经常性的边疆冲突。
enemies.en: Babylon, various Syrian city-states, Hurrian powers, and emerging Mitanni rivals were the main enemies or competitors; western and northern Anatolia also saw recurring frontier conflict.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以军事震慑、征服、纳贡与附庸化为主，通过突袭和远征打破叙利亚—美索不达米亚旧有权力平衡；相比后世赫梯帝国时期，1600年前后更偏向以武力塑造秩序而非成熟条约体系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on military intimidation, conquest, tribute extraction, and vassalization, using raids and campaigns to disrupt the older Syrian-Mesopotamian balance of power; compared with the later Hittite imperial period, around 1600 BCE it relied more on force than on a mature treaty system.

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的青铜兵器制造、战车组织、楔形文字档案行政、山区堡垒网络、长距离军事补给与多语言官僚管理是其关键技术优势。
keyInnovations.en: Its key technological strengths were mature bronze weapon production, organized chariot warfare, cuneiform archival administration, mountain fortress networks, long-distance military logistics, and multilingual bureaucratic management.

strengths.zh: 赫梯王国在1600年前后拥有强劲的军事进攻能力、可动员的贵族战车阶层、坚固的安纳托利亚核心地带、吸纳外来制度的灵活性，以及通过远征叙利亚甚至巴比伦展示出的区域霸权潜力。
strengths.en: Around 1600 BCE the Hittite kingdom possessed strong offensive military capacity, a mobilizable chariot aristocracy, a defensible Anatolian core, flexibility in adopting outside institutions, and clear potential for regional hegemony demonstrated by campaigns into Syria and even Babylon.

weaknesses.zh: 其主要弱点是继承与宫廷政治不稳、远距离征服难以长期控制、地方与附庸忠诚度有限、财政高度依赖征发和战利品，以及对叙利亚与美索不达米亚复杂局势的持续管理能力不足。
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses were unstable succession and court politics, difficulty in holding distant conquests for long, limited loyalty from provinces and vassals, heavy fiscal dependence on extraction and booty, and insufficient capacity to manage the complex Syrian and Mesopotamian political environment on a sustained basis.

outlook.zh: 短期内赫梯仍是西亚最具爆发力的新兴强权之一，能够继续左右叙利亚局势；但若王室内斗加剧或远征超出补给极限，王国可能在扩张高峰后迅速出现收缩与动荡。
outlook.en: In the short term the Hittites were one of the most dynamic rising powers in Western Asia and could continue to shape events in Syria; however, if royal infighting intensified or campaigns outran logistical limits, the kingdom could enter contraction and instability soon after its expansionary peak.

description.zh: 安纳托利亚中部强国，正向叙利亚与巴比伦发动远征。
description.en: A central Anatolian great power undertaking campaigns into Syria and even against Babylon.

name.zh: 奇祖瓦特那的奇里乞亚卢维—胡里安文明

governmentForm.zh: 青铜时代世袭君主制，结合宫廷、地方贵族与庙宇权威的复合王国
governmentForm.en: A Bronze Age hereditary monarchy, a composite kingdom balancing royal court, local nobles, and temple authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、军事与祭司贵族、地方领主、自由农民与牧民、手工业者、商旅及依附劳役人口构成；奇里乞亚平原的农业定居者与山区、边地社群并存。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, military and priestly nobles, local lords, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans, merchants, and dependent laborers; settled agricultural communities of the Cilician plain coexisted with upland and frontier groups.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要为王族、卢维语与胡里安语背景的宫廷贵族、战车武士首领以及主持重要神庙的祭司家族。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted chiefly of the royal family, court nobles of Luwian- and Hurrian-speaking background, chariot-warrior chiefs, and priestly families controlling major temples.

succession.zh: 王位通常由王室父系世袭继承，但需获得宫廷贵族、军事首领与主要宗教中心的承认；在继承不稳时，婚姻联盟与宗主强权影响显著。
succession.en: The throne generally passed through dynastic patrilineal succession, but required recognition from court nobles, military chiefs, and major cult centers; in unstable accessions, marriage alliances and pressure from stronger neighbors were significant.

structure.zh: 国家以库曼尼王廷为核心，王室通过宫廷书记、使者、军政首领和地方城镇领主管理平原与山口地带；政务高度依赖个人忠诚、盟誓关系和神庙网络。
structure.en: The state was centered on the royal court at Kummanni, from which the king governed the plain and mountain passes through palace scribes, messengers, military-administrative chiefs, and local town lords; administration relied heavily on personal loyalty, oath ties, and temple networks.

function.zh: 负责王令记录、外交书信、契约、贡赋清册与印玺保管，使用楔形文字并兼容当地多语环境。
function.en: Handled royal decrees, diplomatic correspondence, contracts, tribute registers, and seal custody, using cuneiform within a multilingual local environment.

function.zh: 管理库曼尼等地神庙祭礼、供物、土地、神职人员与宗教节期，并维护王权的神圣合法性。
function.en: Managed temple rites, offerings, lands, clergy, and religious festivals at Kummanni and other centers, sustaining the sacred legitimacy of kingship.

function.zh: 征发军役、维护战车队、储备武器牲畜并协调边境守备与山口防御。
function.en: Oversaw levies, maintained the chariot corps, stored weapons and draft animals, and coordinated frontier garrisons and pass defense.

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、油、酒、木材与金属实物税，管理仓储并向宫廷、驻军与神庙分配。
function.en: Collected taxes in kind—grain, livestock, oil, wine, timber, and metals—managed storage, and redistributed supplies to palace, garrisons, and temples.

function.zh: 监管山口、渡口和商旅通道，征收过境税并保护通往叙利亚与安纳托利亚内地的交通。
function.en: Supervised mountain passes, crossings, and caravan routes, levied transit dues, and protected movement toward Syria and inland Anatolia.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由附属城镇首领、贵族家族与庙宇代理人共同承担；王廷对核心平原控制较强，对山地和边缘地带更多依赖盟誓、质子与军事巡查。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared by subordinate town rulers, noble families, and temple agents; the court exercised firmer control in the core plain, while upland and peripheral zones were managed more through oaths, hostages, and military inspection.

legalSystem.zh: 法律为习惯法、王室裁断与契约实践的结合，受安纳托利亚与叙利亚传统共同影响；案件多涉及土地、婚姻、债务、牲畜、逃亡劳役与人身伤害，凭证人、誓言与书面契约裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined customary law, royal judgment, and contractual practice, shaped by both Anatolian and Syrian traditions; common cases concerned land, marriage, debt, livestock, runaway laborers, and bodily injury, adjudicated through witnesses, oaths, and written contracts.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括谷物、羊群、牛、油、酒、金属、木材与运输服务；贸易节点征收通行税，战时加派军需与役使。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind and labor service, including grain, flocks, cattle, oil, wine, metals, timber, and transport obligations; tolls were collected at trade nodes, with extra military requisitions in wartime.

religion.zh: 宗教呈强烈混合性，融合卢维—安纳托利亚与胡里安传统；风暴神、太阳女神、山神与地方守护神并受崇拜，祭祀与王权合法性紧密相连，库曼尼是重要圣地。
religion.en: Religion was strongly syncretic, blending Luwian-Anatolian and Hurrian traditions; storm gods, solar goddesses, mountain deities, and local tutelary gods were worshiped, with ritual closely tied to royal legitimacy, and Kummanni serving as a major sacred center.

philosophy.zh: 尚无后世意义上的系统哲学，世界观主要体现为神意、盟誓、祖先传统、王权职责与仪式秩序的观念。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophy in the later sense; worldview was expressed through divine will, oath obligation, ancestral custom, royal duty, and ritual order.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与外交主要使用阿卡德语楔形文字书写，也可能存在本地卢维语口语与多语并用环境；书写集中于宫廷、神庙和外交活动。
writingSystem.en: Administration and diplomacy mainly employed Akkadian cuneiform, while local Luwian speech and a multilingual environment likely persisted; literacy was concentrated in the palace, temples, and diplomatic activity.

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就体现在多族群宗教融合、仪式传统、战车贵族文化、宫廷契约书写、山口贸易组织以及后来在赫梯与叙利亚世界中具有影响力的胡里安祭仪传承。
culturalAchievements.en: Its cultural achievements lay in multiethnic religious synthesis, ritual traditions, chariot aristocratic culture, palace contract writing, organization of pass trade, and Hurrian cult forms that later influenced the Hittite and Syrian worlds.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言环境包括印欧语系安纳托利亚支的卢维语，以及胡里安语；官方书写外交语言多用阿卡德语。
languageFamily.en: The principal linguistic environment included Luwian of the Anatolian branch of Indo-European and Hurrian; Akkadian often served as the written language of official diplomacy.

unit.en: silver shekels equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模对一个九万人口的青铜时代区域王国而言属中等偏上，建立在奇里乞亚平原的粮食、葡萄酒、橄榄油、畜牧与山地木材、金属通道控制之上；财富集中于宫廷、神庙与贵族，而非货币化市场。
gdpDescription.en: For a Bronze Age regional kingdom of ninety thousand people, the economy was moderately strong, based on grain, wine, olive oil, and herding from the Cilician plain, along with control of upland timber and metal routes; wealth was concentrated in palace, temple, and noble hands rather than in a monetized market.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、牧业、战车与武器配套制造、纺织、木材采运、青铜加工、陶器制作以及过境贸易服务。
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, animal husbandry, chariot and weapons support production, textiles, timber extraction, bronze working, pottery manufacture, and transit trade services.

tradeGoods.zh: 输出谷物、酒、油、牲畜、马匹、木材、沥青与部分金属制品；输入锡、优质铜、奢侈织物、珠饰、叙利亚工艺品与外交礼物。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included grain, wine, oil, livestock, horses, timber, bitumen, and some metal goods; imports included tin, quality copper, luxury textiles, beads, Syrian crafted items, and diplomatic gifts.

name.zh: 按银衡量的实物交换体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、谷仓余粮、羊群、牛、农具和家庭成员劳动力；贵族与神庙则掌握大型仓储、马匹、战车、金属器与附属劳役人口。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was measured chiefly in land-use rights, stored grain, flocks, cattle, tools, and family labor; nobles and temples controlled large storehouses, horses, chariots, metal goods, and dependent laborers.

unit.en: silver shekels equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以宫廷—神庙再分配经济为核心，辅以地方贵族庄园生产、村社自给和区域商道交换；白银主要是计价标准而非常用铸币。
economicSystem.en: The economy centered on palace-temple redistribution, supplemented by noble estates, village subsistence, and regional route exchange; silver served mainly as a unit of account rather than coinage.

source.zh: 王室庄园与神庙地产收益
source.en: Royal estate and temple land income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是统一的金银储藏，而是由库曼尼王宫、神庙仓廪、牲畜群、金属库存与外交礼品共同构成的复合储备体系。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a single cache of specie, but a composite reserve of palace stores at Kummanni, temple granaries, livestock herds, metal stocks, and diplomatic valuables.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守稳健，重视积累粮食和牲畜储备以应对歉收、战事与宗主压力；在稳定时期维持小幅盈余，以巩固王权与边境防务。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and stability-oriented, emphasizing grain and livestock reserves against poor harvests, war, and pressure from stronger neighbors; in stable years the court aimed for a modest surplus to sustain royal authority and frontier defense.

description.zh: 由王室与贵族供养的精锐战车兵构成，兼具冲击、机动与指挥象征功能，是奇祖瓦特那军力核心。
description.en: An elite arm maintained by the crown and nobility, providing shock, mobility, and command prestige, and forming the core of Kizzuwatna's military power.

description.zh: 常备步兵驻守都城、仓储设施与主要据点，装备矛、盾、斧与短剑，承担守备与护卫任务。
description.en: Standing infantry guarded the capital, storehouses, and major strongpoints, equipped with spears, shields, axes, and short swords for defense and escort duties.

description.zh: 由村社与附属城镇按需征发，多在战时集结，用于守卫山口、补充主力和维持地区控制。
