capital.en: Mycenae, Tiryns, Pylos, and other palatial centers

governmentForm.zh: 以宫殿为核心的分立君主制王国体系
governmentForm.en: A palace-centered system of separate monarchic kingdoms

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王宫为顶端，瓦纳克斯及其贵族、军事首领和祭司控制土地、工匠、仓储与再分配；其下为地方首领、自由民农户、专业工匠、商人、依附劳工与奴隶。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the palace, where the wanax and associated nobles, military leaders, and religious officials controlled land, craftsmen, storage, and redistribution; beneath them were local chiefs, free peasant households, specialized artisans, merchants, dependent laborers, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 宫殿王族、贵族家族、军事贵胄与高级祭司集团
rulingClass.en: The palatial royal houses, noble families, military elites, and high religious officials

succession.zh: 通常为王族世袭，但各地王国制度并不完全统一，继承可能受宗族、贵族支持与武力控制影响。
succession.en: Succession was generally dynastic within royal houses, though practices were not fully uniform across kingdoms and could be shaped by kinship, noble backing, and military control.

structure.zh: 各王国由宫殿行政中心统治，瓦纳克斯居于最高地位，下设军事领袖、地方官、书记员和仓储管理人员。王权通过档案记录、仓库控制、贡赋征收和宗教仪式实施。
structure.en: Each kingdom was ruled from a palace administrative center, with the wanax at the top, supported by military leaders, local officials, scribes, and storehouse managers. Royal authority was exercised through record keeping, control of storerooms, collection of dues, and religious ceremonies.

function.zh: 以线形文字B前身或早期行政记号记录牲畜、粮食、劳役、土地与贡品，管理王宫账目。
function.en: Recorded livestock, grain, labor obligations, land, and tribute with early administrative notation and developing forms of what would later appear as Linear B, managing palace accounts.

function.zh: 管理谷物、橄榄油、酒、纺织品、铜与青铜器的收纳、配给和对工匠及战士的发放。
function.en: Managed the intake and disbursement of grain, olive oil, wine, textiles, copper, and bronze goods, issuing supplies to craftsmen and warriors.

function.zh: 负责武器制造配发、战车维护、贵族武士登记与地方征召安排。
function.en: Oversaw weapons production and distribution, chariot maintenance, registration of elite warriors, and local levy arrangements.

function.zh: 组织献祭、节庆、神庙供奉与王权合法性仪式，管理祭司与祭品。
function.en: Organized sacrifices, festivals, sanctuary offerings, and rituals legitimizing royal power, while managing priests and offerings.

function.zh: 监督附属村社、地方首领辖区和宫殿领地，征收实物贡赋与劳役。
function.en: Supervised dependent villages, districts under local chiefs, and palace estates, collecting dues in kind and labor obligations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖村社长、地方贵族和宫殿任命的监督者。不同王国下辖若干区域中心与乡村聚落，向宫殿输送粮食、畜产、手工业品和劳役。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village headmen, regional nobles, and palace-appointed overseers. Different kingdoms controlled regional centers and rural settlements that supplied food, livestock products, craft goods, and labor to the palace.

legalSystem.zh: 尚无成文法典保存于此时期，秩序主要依靠王权裁断、习惯法、血缘义务、赔偿与宗教誓言维持。宫殿对土地、劳役和贡赋享有优先裁决权。
legalSystem.en: No written law code survives for this period; order was maintained largely through royal judgment, customary law, kin obligations, compensation, and religious oaths. The palace held primary authority in disputes involving land, labor, and dues.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物和劳役为主，包括谷物、油、酒、羊毛、牲畜、金属和工艺品；部分地区还需承担修筑城防、运输和军事征召义务。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind and labor, including grain, oil, wine, wool, livestock, metals, and crafted goods; some districts also owed fortress building, transport service, and military levies.

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以宫殿祭祀和地方圣所并存；崇拜自然、生育、战争与王权相关神祇，部分后世希腊神名可能已有早期形态。祖先崇拜、墓葬仪式和献祭极为重要。
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, combining palace cult with local sanctuaries; deities linked to nature, fertility, war, and kingship were worshipped, and some later Greek divine names may already have existed in early form. Ancestor veneration, funerary ritual, and sacrifice were highly important.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系统哲学传统，世界观以英雄伦理、王权神圣性、祖先荣耀、占兆与仪式秩序为核心。
philosophy.en: There was not yet a formal philosophical tradition; worldview centered on heroic ethics, the sacral nature of kingship, ancestral prestige, omen reading, and ritual order.

writingSystem.zh: 行政记录使用爱琴海书写传统中的早期记号体系，成熟的线形文字B将在稍后广泛出现；书写主要服务于宫殿管理而非大众文学。
writingSystem.en: Administrative recording used early notation within the Aegean writing tradition, with mature Linear B becoming widespread somewhat later; writing was primarily for palace management rather than public literature.

culturalAchievements.zh: 兴建巨石城防和宫殿建筑，发展竖井墓与精美金器，形成高水平壁画、陶器、兵器与珠宝工艺，并积极吸收米诺斯艺术风格后转化为大陆希腊宫廷文化。
culturalAchievements.en: They built massive fortifications and palace architecture, developed shaft graves and fine goldwork, produced accomplished frescoes, pottery, weapons, and jewelry, and actively absorbed Minoan artistic influences into a mainland Greek court culture.

languageFamily.zh: 早期希腊语，属印欧语系希腊语支
languageFamily.en: Early Greek, belonging to the Hellenic branch of the Indo-European language family

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per year
kilograms of grain equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、牧业和宫殿再分配为基础，辅以青铜冶炼、纺织、陶器和海上贸易。对财富的集中控制主要体现在宫殿仓储和贵族墓葬中，而非统一货币市场。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, pastoralism, and palace redistribution, supplemented by bronze working, textiles, pottery, and maritime trade. Concentrated wealth was expressed chiefly through palace storage and elite burials rather than a unified monetary market.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄与葡萄生产、牧羊养牛、毛纺织、陶器、青铜兵器和工具制造、造船及奢侈品工艺
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olive and grape production, sheep and cattle raising, wool textiles, pottery, bronze weapons and tool making, shipbuilding, and luxury craftsmanship

tradeGoods.zh: 出口陶器、橄榄油、酒、纺织品、香油、金属制品和手工奢侈品；进口铜、锡、象牙、金、银、琥珀、染料和部分东方高价值物品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included pottery, olive oil, wine, textiles, perfumed oil, metal goods, and crafted luxuries; imports included copper, tin, ivory, gold, silver, amber, dyes, and selected eastern prestige goods.

name.zh: 无统一铸币，主要以实物与金属衡量交换
name.en: No unified coinage; exchange was reckoned mainly in goods and weighed metal

unitName.en: Weighed units of copper, silver, and commodities

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要表现为土地使用权、谷仓存粮、羊群、陶器和工具；贵族与宫殿则掌握青铜器、战车、金饰、奴隶和大宗仓储物资。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households held wealth mainly in land use rights, stored grain, flocks, pottery, and tools; nobles and palaces controlled bronze goods, chariots, gold ornaments, slaves, and large storeroom reserves.

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per person per year
kilograms of grain equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 宫殿再分配经济与地方家庭生产并存；国家并非完全控制所有生产，但王宫主导高价值原料、武器、纺织和远程贸易。
economicSystem.en: A palatial redistributive economy coexisted with household production; the state did not control all production, but the palace dominated high-value raw materials, weapons, textiles, and long-distance trade.

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per year
kilograms of grain equivalent per year
kilograms of grain equivalent per year

source.zh: 金属、手工业与宫殿作坊产出
source.en: Metal, craft, and palace workshop output

source.zh: 贸易关口、附属地贡礼与掠夺收益
source.en: Trade tolls, dependent tribute, and raiding gains

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent in stored reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是铸币金库，而是由粮仓、油库、酒库、羊毛库存、青铜原料和少量贵金属、奢侈品组成的宫殿储备体系。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not chiefly a coin reserve but a palace storage system of granaries, oil stores, wine stores, wool stock, bronze raw materials, and smaller holdings of precious metals and luxury goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持王宫仓储充足、保障贵族和战士供给、支持建造工程与远程贸易，并通过再分配巩固政治忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on keeping palace storerooms full, supplying elites and warriors, supporting construction and long-distance trade, and using redistribution to secure political loyalty.

name.en: Elite infantry and spear-bearing foot troops

description.zh: 军队主力，由贵族随从与地方征召步兵组成，装备长矛、剑、盾和部分甲胄，用于近战与守城。
description.en: The main force, composed of noble retainers and locally levied infantry, equipped with spears, swords, shields, and some armor for close combat and fortress defense.

description.zh: 由宫殿控制的精锐机动兵种，用于指挥、冲击、射击平台和贵族威望展示，数量有限但政治意义重大。
description.en: A palace-controlled elite mobile arm used for command, shock action, missile platform functions, and aristocratic display; limited in number but politically significant.

description.zh: 包括投石手、标枪兵和弓手，多来自地方社区，适合山地作战、袭扰和支援主力步兵。
description.en: Including slingers, javelin men, and archers, often drawn from local communities, suitable for rough terrain, raiding, and support of the main infantry.

name.en: Naval armed crews and landing parties

description.zh: 依托桨帆船的武装水手与短期登陆兵，用于护航、海上掠袭、岛屿控制和跨海贸易保护。
description.en: Armed sailors and temporary landing troops using oared sailing vessels for escort, maritime raiding, island control, and protection of overseas trade.

commanderInChief.zh: 各王国瓦纳克斯亲掌最高军权，并由拉瓦格塔斯等军事贵族辅佐
commanderInChief.en: The wanax of each kingdom held supreme command, assisted by military nobles such as the lawagetas

technology.zh: 采用青铜长剑、矛、匕首、复合弓、重盾、早期青铜甲具、战车和筑城技术；海军以木质桨帆船为主，具备区域性投送能力。
technology.en: Military technology included bronze swords, spears, daggers, composite bows, heavy shields, early bronze armor elements, chariots, and fortification engineering; naval forces used wooden oared sailing vessels with regional transport capability.

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自爱琴海内部王国竞争、与米诺斯势力的海上博弈、对特洛伊及西安纳托利亚航路的争夺、地方叛服不稳，以及来自海盗与跨海袭扰的风险。
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry among Aegean kingdoms, maritime competition with Minoan power, struggles over Troy and western Anatolian routes, unstable local loyalties, and the risk of piracy and overseas raids.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在伯罗奔尼撒、阿尔戈利斯、麦西尼亚、阿提卡、维奥蒂亚等大陆希腊地区，集中于宫殿中心周边平原、山麓谷地与沿海聚落。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed mainly across mainland Greek regions such as the Peloponnese, Argolid, Messenia, Attica, and Boeotia, concentrated around palace centers, plains, foothill valleys, and coastal settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、贵族战士和高级祭司；中层为地方首领、自由农民、商人和技术工匠；下层为依附劳工、债役者、奴隶与战俘。社会高度重视血缘、战功与墓葬荣誉。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of royal houses, noble warriors, and high religious officials; the middle included local leaders, free farmers, merchants, and skilled artisans; the lower comprised dependent laborers, bonded workers, slaves, and captives. Society strongly valued kinship, martial prestige, and funerary honor.

allies.zh: 与部分爱琴海岛屿社群、若干米诺斯贸易伙伴及个别西爱琴海和东地中海港口保持互惠关系，但联盟多为临时性、贸易性或婚姻性联系。
allies.en: They maintained reciprocal ties with some Aegean island communities, certain Minoan trade partners, and select western Aegean and eastern Mediterranean ports, though alliances were often temporary, commercial, or marriage-based.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括爱琴海内其他竞争性宫殿王国、特洛伊及相关海峡贸易势力，以及在海上贸易中与其争利的强权。
enemies.en: Principal rivals included other competing palatial kingdoms within the Aegean, Troy and associated straits powers, and stronger states contesting maritime trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以争夺航路、获取金属原料、扩大威望和控制岛屿节点为主，常结合贸易、婚姻、赠礼、雇佣武力和有限海上掠袭。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling routes, securing metal supplies, expanding prestige, and holding island nodes, using a mix of trade, marriage, gift exchange, hired force, and limited maritime raiding.

era.en: Early phase of the Late Bronze Age

keyInnovations.zh: 宫殿仓储管理、早期行政书写、青铜兵器专业化、战车使用、巨石城防建筑、毛纺织组织化生产、远距离海运网络整合
keyInnovations.en: Palatial storage management, early administrative writing, specialized bronze weapon production, chariot use, cyclopean-style fortification building, organized wool textile production, and integration into long-distance maritime exchange networks

strengths.zh: 迈锡尼诸王国拥有强烈的军事贵族文化、快速扩张的宫殿组织、高水平手工业、通往东地中海的海上网络和对高价值资源的敏锐追求，因此在1600 BCE前后正成为爱琴海的新兴强权。
strengths.en: The Mycenaean kingdoms possessed a strong martial aristocratic culture, rapidly expanding palatial organization, high-level craftsmanship, maritime connections to the eastern Mediterranean, and an aggressive drive for prestige resources, making them a rising Aegean power around 1600 BCE.

weaknesses.zh: 政治上并未统一为单一国家，各宫殿彼此竞争；经济高度依赖精英控制和外来铜锡供应；社会等级尖锐，若贸易受阻、歉收或王权失稳，系统容易脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Politically they were not united as a single state, and the palaces competed with one another; the economy depended heavily on elite control and imported copper and tin; social hierarchy was sharp, and the system could prove fragile if trade failed, harvests declined, or royal authority weakened.

outlook.zh: 短期前景非常乐观。随着对米诺斯模式的吸收和大陆武力优势的增强，迈锡尼文明很可能在随后数世纪主导希腊本土并深入参与东地中海贸易与战争体系。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was very favorable. As they absorbed Minoan models and strengthened mainland military power, the Mycenaean civilization was likely to dominate mainland Greece and become more deeply involved in the trade and warfare systems of the eastern Mediterranean over the following centuries.

description.zh: 希腊本土的宫殿王国迅速扩张，成为爱琴海世界新兴强权。
description.en: Palatial kingdoms on mainland Greece were rapidly expanding and becoming the rising powers of the Aegean.

name.zh: 西地中海晚期岛屿社群
地中海岛屿青铜时代文化群

governmentForm.zh: 分散的岛屿酋邦与部落联盟，按岛屿、海湾和港湾聚落分别治理
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of island chiefdoms and tribal alliances, governed separately by island, bay, and harbor communities

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、氏族和海港聚落为核心，贵族家族、战士、航海者、工匠、农牧民和依附者构成层级，但总体上仍属小规模首领社会
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kin groups, clans, and harbor settlements, with ranked noble families, warriors, seafarers, artisans, farmers, pastoralists, and dependents, though still fundamentally small-scale chiefly societies

rulingClass.zh: 掌握土地、船只、仓储和祭祀权威的地方首领家族
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly families controlling land, boats, storage, and ritual authority

succession.zh: 多为世袭继承，通常在首领家族内部由长子、兄弟或经贵族与长老认可的亲属继位
succession.en: Usually hereditary, typically passing within leading families to a son, brother, or another kinsman approved by nobles and elders

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构；各岛屿或大型聚落由酋长主持，辅以氏族长老会议、战士追随者和祭祀人物处理分配、纠纷、防务与对外贸易
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; each island or major settlement was led by a chief, assisted by councils of elders, warrior retainers, and ritual figures who handled allocation, disputes, defense, and external exchange

function.zh: 管理谷物、橄榄油、酒、牲畜、金属与贡品储藏，并在宴飨和危机时重新分配
function.en: Managed stores of grain, olive oil, wine, livestock, metals, and tribute, redistributing them during feasts and times of need

function.zh: 组织船只、渔业、沿海运输、外来商人接待与海上联络
function.en: Organized boats, fishing, coastal transport, reception of foreign merchants, and maritime communications

function.zh: 维护地方圣地、主持献祭、祖先崇拜与季节性仪式，增强首领合法性
function.en: Maintained local sanctuaries, oversaw offerings, ancestor veneration, and seasonal rites, reinforcing chiefly legitimacy

function.zh: 负责海岸警戒、寨墙维护、战士召集与应对海盗或邻岛冲突
function.en: Handled coastal watch, upkeep of fortified sites, warrior musters, and responses to raiding or inter-island conflict

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村社、山顶寨与港湾聚落为单位，事务由首领、家族长老和专职仓储管理者共同处理
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages, hilltop forts, and harbor settlements, with affairs managed jointly by chiefs, lineage elders, and specialized storehouse keepers

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，以赔偿、调解、血亲仲裁和公开宣誓为核心；严重冲突可能导致放逐、报复性袭击或家族和解
legalSystem.en: Law was mainly customary, centered on compensation, mediation, kin arbitration, and public oaths; serious conflicts could lead to exile, retaliatory raids, or negotiated clan settlement

taxationSystem.zh: 无标准化税制，首领通过贡纳、劳役、宴飨义务、战时征发和贸易抽成获取资源
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized tax system; chiefs obtained resources through tribute, labor obligations, feast duties, wartime levies, and shares of trade

religion.zh: 以自然神灵、生育与海洋崇拜、祖先祭祀和地方圣所仪式为主，部分区域受米诺斯宗教象征影响，如峰顶圣所、洞穴祭仪和女神崇拜元素
religion.en: Religion focused on nature spirits, fertility and sea cults, ancestor worship, and rituals at local sanctuaries, with some regions influenced by Minoan religious symbolism such as peak sanctuaries, cave rites, and goddess motifs

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成抽象哲学传统，世界观以祖先权威、航海经验、季节循环、神意征兆和互惠义务为核心
philosophy.en: No abstract philosophical tradition had formed; worldview centered on ancestral authority, seafaring experience, seasonal cycles, divine signs, and reciprocal obligation

writingSystem.zh: 多数岛屿社群无本地成体系文字；与米诺斯和近东贸易接触地区可能见到输入性记号、印章符号或少量爱琴文字影响，但未普及行政书写
writingSystem.en: Most island communities lacked a native fully developed writing system; areas in contact with Minoan and Near Eastern trade may have seen imported marks, seal symbols, or limited Aegean script influence, but not widespread administrative literacy

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出精良航海技术、石砌聚落、防御性山顶寨、陶器装饰传统、金属器铸造与跨海交换网络；部分岛屿形成兼具本地特色与爱琴风格的礼仪器物
culturalAchievements.en: They developed skilled seafaring, stone-built settlements, defensive hilltop sites, distinctive ceramic traditions, metalworking, and inter-island exchange networks; some islands produced prestige goods blending local traits with Aegean styles

languageFamily.zh: 语言多样，可能包括前印欧岛屿语言、早期印欧方言及与爱琴邻区接触形成的混合语言环境
languageFamily.en: Linguistic situation was diverse, likely including pre-Indo-European island languages, early Indo-European dialects, and mixed contact environments shaped by neighboring Aegean cultures

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，以自给农业、牧业、渔业和短中程海运交换为主。部分岛屿凭借铜、黑曜石、陶器、染料或中转港角色取得超出人口规模的财富。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small in scale, based mainly on subsistence farming, herding, fishing, and short- to medium-range maritime exchange. Some islands gained wealth disproportionate to their population through copper, obsidian, pottery, dyes, or roles as transit harbors

mainIndustries.zh: 大麦和小麦种植、葡萄与橄榄栽培、绵羊山羊放牧、渔业、陶器制作、青铜与铜器加工、石器与纺织品生产、航运与转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Barley and wheat cultivation, grape and olive growing, sheep and goat herding, fishing, pottery production, bronze and copper working, stone and textile production, shipping, and entrepôt trade

tradeGoods.zh: 铜锭、少量锡与青铜器、黑曜石、陶器、纺织品、羊毛、橄榄油、葡萄酒、盐渍鱼、贝紫染料、木材、蜂蜜与香料性物产
tradeGoods.en: Copper ingots, small amounts of tin and bronze goods, obsidian, pottery, textiles, wool, olive oil, wine, salted fish, purple shellfish dye, timber, honey, and aromatic products

name.zh: 无铸币，采用实物与金属衡量交换
name.en: No coinage; exchange by goods and weighed metal

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、羊群、船只、青铜工具、储粮罐和首饰上，贵族家族拥有明显更大的仓储与礼仪财富
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in land use rights, flocks, boats, bronze tools, storage jars, and ornaments, while elite families held much larger stores and prestige wealth

unit.en: kg grain equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、氏族再分配和港湾交换相结合的酋邦经济，市场因素有限但海贸对精英财富十分重要
economicSystem.en: A chiefly economy combining household production, clan redistribution, and harbor exchange, with limited market mechanisms but maritime trade highly important for elite wealth

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国库，财富分散存于各主要首领仓库、神庙性储藏点、贵族宅邸和可移动金属器物中
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; wealth was dispersed among major chiefly storehouses, sanctuary deposits, elite residences, and portable metal goods

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持再分配、宴飨政治、港湾联系与防御工事，通常避免过度榨取以免部落离散或附庸改投他岛
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized redistribution, feast politics, harbor connections, and defensive works, usually avoiding overextraction lest communities disperse or dependents shift allegiance to other islands

description.zh: 由首领直属战士、护卫和少量精英矛兵组成，平时承担护卫、巡逻和征收任务
description.en: Composed of the chief's direct warriors, guards, and a small number of elite spearmen, serving in protection, patrol, and collection duties in peacetime

description.zh: 战时由各村社成年男子集结，装备以矛、短剑、投石索、木盾和少量青铜武器为主
description.en: Raised in wartime from adult men of village communities, equipped mainly with spears, daggers, slings, wooden shields, and limited bronze weapons

description.zh: 由熟练船员与战士组成，用于侦察、护航、反海盗和小规模跨海袭击
description.en: Made up of skilled sailors and fighters, used for scouting, escort, anti-piracy, and small-scale seaborne raids

commanderInChief.zh: 各岛首领分别统率，无统一最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: Each island chief commanded separately; there was no unified supreme commander

technology.zh: 军事技术属于中等青铜时代水平，使用青铜短剑、矛头、皮革或木盾、简易头盔、投石索和轻型桨帆船；防御依赖山顶寨、石墙和瞭望点
technology.en: Military technology was at a middling Bronze Age level, using bronze daggers, spearheads, leather or wooden shields, simple helmets, slings, and light oared boats; defense relied on hilltop forts, stone walls, and watchpoints

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近岛屿争斗、海盗袭击、外来强权对港口控制、贸易航道竞争以及局部歉收引发的掠夺
threats.en: Main threats came from rival neighboring islands, pirate attacks, outside powers seeking harbor control, competition over trade routes, and raiding triggered by local crop failures

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住在爱琴海与中部地中海若干岛屿、近岸小岛和天然港湾聚落中，单个政治单位通常规模较小
populationDescription.en: The population was scattered across various Aegean and central Mediterranean islands, offshore islets, and natural harbor settlements, with individual political units generally small in scale

name.zh: 无统一城市，主要为港湾聚落群
name.en: No unified cities, only major harbor settlement clusters

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族与贵族、祭祀人物、战士与船长、工匠、自由农牧民、渔民、依附者与俘获奴役人口
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families and nobles, ritual specialists, warriors and captains, artisans, free farmers and herders, fishers, dependents, and some captive servile people

allies.zh: 多无固定正式盟友，通常与邻近岛屿、米诺斯商站或意大利半岛沿岸社群维持临时通婚、互市与互助关系
allies.en: They generally had no fixed formal allies, instead maintaining temporary marriage, trade, and mutual aid ties with nearby islands, Minoan trading posts, or coastal communities of the Italian peninsula

enemies.zh: 敌对对象并不恒定，主要是竞争港口与航道的邻岛酋邦、海盗团体，以及偶发介入的更强爱琴势力
enemies.en: Enemies were not permanent, chiefly neighboring island chiefdoms competing for harbors and routes, pirate groups, and occasionally stronger Aegean powers intervening in local affairs

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存性平衡为主，通过馈赠、婚盟、引航、转口贸易和有限武力维持自治，尽量在米诺斯、迈锡尼及更广泛东地中海势力之间保持灵活
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily a matter of survival and balance, using gifts, marriage alliances, piloting, entrepôt trade, and limited force to preserve autonomy, while remaining flexible between Minoan, Mycenaean, and wider eastern Mediterranean powers

keyInnovations.zh: 远程海上航行、青铜铸造与修补、储藏陶器、石砌防御建筑、橄榄油与葡萄酒加工、纺织与染色技术
keyInnovations.en: Open-water seafaring, bronze casting and repair, storage pottery, stone defensive architecture, olive oil and wine processing, and textile and dyeing techniques

strengths.zh: 熟悉海域航线，社区弹性强，善于跨海交换与资源补缺；分散结构使其不易被一次性征服，且能吸收米诺斯和近东技术文化影响
strengths.en: They knew maritime routes well, had resilient communities, and were adept at overseas exchange and resource balancing; their dispersed structure made them hard to conquer in a single blow and let them absorb Minoan and Near Eastern influences

weaknesses.zh: 政治碎片化严重，资源基础狭窄，对外部贸易和气候波动敏感，缺乏统一军政机构，也较难长期抵御大型国家或宫殿经济体系的压力
weaknesses.en: They were highly fragmented politically, had a narrow resource base, were sensitive to trade disruption and climate fluctuation, lacked unified military and administrative institutions, and found it difficult to resist the pressure of larger states or palace economies over time

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后总体稳定，但未来可能进一步被米诺斯与迈锡尼贸易体系吸纳；部分岛屿会因港湾优势而繁荣，另一些则可能沦为附庸、海盗据点或迁徙中转地
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE they were broadly stable, but in the future they were likely to be drawn further into Minoan and Mycenaean trade systems; some islands would prosper through harbor advantages, while others might become dependents, pirate bases, or migration waystations

description.zh: 爱琴与中地中海诸岛分布着小型海洋社群和酋邦。
description.en: The Aegean and central Mediterranean islands contained small maritime communities and chiefdoms.

name.zh: 意大利青铜时代诸文化
特拉马雷—亚平宁文化圈

governmentForm.zh: 由设防聚落、氏族联盟与山地酋邦构成的分散部落体系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order composed of fortified settlements, clan alliances, and upland chiefdoms

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、氏族和地方共同体为核心，上层为战士贵族与祭祀首领，中层为自由农牧民与工匠，下层包括依附者、债务从属者与家内仆役。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kin groups, clans, and local communities, with a warrior aristocracy and ritual leaders at the top, free farmers, herders, and craftspeople in the middle, and dependents, debt bondsmen, and household servants at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 地方武士首领、家族长老与掌握祭仪权威的精英家族
rulingClass.en: Local warrior chiefs, lineage elders, and elite families holding ritual authority

succession.zh: 通常在显赫家族内部继承，但须获得长老、战士追随者与相关亲族集团认可；部分地区可能出现竞争性推举。
succession.en: Usually hereditary within prominent families, but requiring acceptance by elders, warrior followers, and associated kin groups; in some areas succession could be competitive or elective.

structure.zh: 无统一国家官僚机构。政治组织以村寨议事、首领家族统治和跨聚落联盟为主；在特拉马雷平原聚落中，防御工事、仓储与土地分配显示出较强的集体管理能力，而亚平宁山地社会则更依赖季节性集会与部落纽带。
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy. Political organization rested on village councils, chiefly households, and inter-settlement alliances; in the Terramare plain settlements, fortifications, storage, and land allocation suggest stronger collective management, while Apennine upland societies relied more on seasonal assemblies and tribal ties.

function.zh: 组织壕沟、木栅和出入口防守，调度战士与值守人员
function.en: Organized ditches, palisades, and gate defense, and coordinated warriors and guards

function.zh: 管理谷物、牲畜、青铜原料及酋长再分配物资
function.en: Managed grain, livestock, bronze raw materials, and goods redistributed by chiefs

function.zh: 主持祭仪、宴飨、葬俗与季节性集会，维持共同体合法性
function.en: Oversaw rituals, feasts, funerary customs, and seasonal gatherings, maintaining communal legitimacy

function.zh: 协调转场放牧、山地通道和跨区域交换联络
function.en: Coordinated transhumance, upland routes, and interregional exchange contacts

function.zh: 监管青铜铸造、工具武器生产与原料分配
function.en: Supervised bronze casting, tool and weapon production, and raw material allocation

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理由聚落首领、家族长老、战士首领和祭祀人物共同承担。大型设防聚落可能设有更明确的仓储与劳役协调者，但整体仍属于人格化统治而非正式官职体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out jointly by settlement chiefs, lineage elders, war leaders, and ritual figures. Larger fortified settlements may have had clearer coordinators for storage and labor, but the system remained one of personal authority rather than formal office.

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法，法律秩序主要基于习惯法、血亲义务、赔偿、复仇调停和首领仲裁。对牲畜、土地使用、婚姻关系和流血冲突的处理多依赖氏族协商。
legalSystem.en: There was no written law; legal order rested mainly on customary law, kin obligations, compensation, feud settlement, and chiefly arbitration. Matters involving livestock, land use, marriage ties, and bloodshed were usually handled through clan negotiation.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制。酋长通过贡赋、宴飨供奉、劳役征发、战利品分配与关键资源控制获取收入，大型聚落可能按户或按家族征集谷物、牲畜、木材与金属。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system. Chiefs obtained revenue through tribute, feast contributions, labor levies, war booty distribution, and control of key resources; larger settlements likely collected grain, livestock, timber, and metal by household or lineage.

religion.zh: 宗教以祖先崇拜、自然神圣观、山林水域祭祀和家庭祭仪为核心。火、太阳、风暴、生育与牲畜繁衍可能具有重要象征意义，葬俗和宴飨是维系共同体与精英权威的重要仪式。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, sacred views of nature, cults of mountains, woods, and waters, and household ritual. Fire, the sun, storms, fertility, and animal reproduction likely carried major symbolic meaning, while funerary rites and feasting were important for community cohesion and elite authority.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成文哲学传统，价值观建立在勇武、亲族忠诚、荣誉交换、土地与牧道控制以及祖先合法性之上。
philosophy.en: No written philosophical tradition existed; values were grounded in martial prowess, kin loyalty, honor exchange, control of land and pasture routes, and ancestral legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统，信息传递依赖口述传统、记忆性谱系、仪式符号与器物风格。
writingSystem.en: There was no indigenous writing system; information was transmitted through oral tradition, memorized genealogies, ritual symbols, and artifact styles.

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括特拉马雷设防聚落规划、壕沟与木结构工程、青铜器铸造、骨角器制作、跨山地与平原的牧农互补经济，以及反映身份和区域联系的陶器风格体系。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included Terramare fortified settlement planning, ditch and timber engineering, bronze casting, bone and antler working, a mixed agro-pastoral economy linking uplands and plains, and ceramic style systems reflecting identity and regional interaction.

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，可能由多种前印欧与早期印欧方言并存组成，区域差异显著。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain, likely comprising multiple pre-Indo-European and early Indo-European dialects with strong regional variation.

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和牧业为主，辅以区域性青铜生产和长距离交换。整体生产能力高于纯游动部落社会，但远未形成城市国家式的税收和市场整合。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence farming and herding, supplemented by regional bronze production and long-distance exchange. Productive capacity was higher than that of fully mobile tribal societies, but far from the fiscal and market integration of city-states.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、养牛养羊养猪、青铜铸造、木工、陶器制作、纺织、骨角器加工、盐与林木资源利用
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, cattle-sheep-pig husbandry, bronze casting, woodworking, pottery production, textile work, bone and antler craft, and use of salt and forest resources

tradeGoods.zh: 铜、锡、青铜器、琥珀、盐、牲畜、毛织品、皮革、陶器、木材、骨角制品
tradeGoods.en: Copper, tin, bronze goods, amber, salt, livestock, wool textiles, leather, pottery, timber, and bone-antler products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、牲畜数量、谷仓储备、青铜武器工具、纺织品和宴飨能力。精英家庭拥有更多武器、饰品和可动员追随者。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through land-use rights, livestock numbers, grain stores, bronze weapons and tools, textiles, and the ability to host feasts. Elite households possessed more weapons, ornaments, and retainers they could mobilize.

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、酋长再分配、馈赠交换和区域互补网络为核心的部落经济；市场交换存在但处于次要地位。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on household production, chiefly redistribution, gift exchange, and regional complementary networks; market exchange existed but remained secondary.

source.en: Labor levies and public works obligations

source.zh: 交换通道与资源控制收益
source.en: Returns from exchange routes and resource control
Ritual gifts and chiefly household assets

category.en: Support for craftspeople and metal production

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”并非集中金库，而是分散在各大聚落与酋长家族中的谷仓、牲畜群、青铜锭、工具武器和可再分配奢侈品储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized vault but a distributed reserve of granaries, herds, bronze ingots, tools, weapons, and redistributable prestige goods held in major settlements and chiefly households.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策的核心不是预算平衡，而是通过储粮、宴飨再分配、修筑工事和奖赏追随者来维持忠诚与应对歉收风险。
fiscalPolicy.en: The core of fiscal policy was not budget balance but the maintenance of loyalty and mitigation of bad harvests through grain storage, feasting redistribution, fortification works, and rewards to followers.

description.zh: 以长矛、短剑、斧和木盾为主的核心战力，多由自由成年男子和首领追随者组成。
description.en: The core fighting force, armed mainly with spears, short swords, axes, and wooden shields, composed largely of free adult males and chiefly retainers.

description.zh: 常驻或半常驻于大型聚落，负责壕沟、木栅、出入口和仓储区的防卫。
description.en: Permanent or semi-permanent forces in larger settlements, responsible for ditches, palisades, gates, and storage areas.

name.en: Upland Raiding and Pastoral War Bands

description.zh: 熟悉山地通道与季节迁徙路线，擅长伏击、掠袭和小规模部落冲突。
description.en: Familiar with mountain passes and seasonal movement routes, skilled in ambush, raiding, and small-scale tribal conflict.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地酋长在联盟行动中推举战时首领
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; alliance campaigns appointed temporary war leaders from among local chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术处于中期青铜时代水平，使用青铜矛头、剑、匕首、斧和护具较少的轻装战法；防御依赖壕沟、木栅、土台和设防聚落布局。
technology.en: Military technology was at a Middle Bronze Age level, employing bronze spearheads, swords, daggers, axes, and lightly equipped fighting styles with limited armor; defense relied on ditches, palisades, earthen works, and fortified settlement layouts.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落竞争、牧道与耕地争夺、资源控制冲突以及亚得里亚海和第勒尼安沿岸交换网络中的掠袭风险，而非大型帝国入侵。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival neighboring tribes, competition over pasture routes and farmland, conflicts over resource control, and raiding risks within Adriatic and Tyrrhenian exchange networks, rather than invasion by large empires.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布较为分散，集中于波河流域特拉马雷聚落、亚平宁山区牧农共同体以及中南部较小型村寨。人口增长受农业扩展、森林开垦与聚落防御能力推动，但仍受疾病、歉收和局部冲突限制。
populationDescription.en: Population was widely dispersed, concentrated in Terramare settlements of the Po Valley, agro-pastoral communities of the Apennines, and smaller villages in central and southern Italy. Growth was supported by agricultural expansion, forest clearance, and stronger settlement defenses, but remained limited by disease, harvest failure, and local conflict.

name.zh: 波河中游大型设防聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括酋长与精英家族、战士追随者、祭祀人物、自由农牧民、工匠、依附者以及少量家庭奴役人口；各地区分层程度不一，整体仍以亲族地位和资源控制为核心。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included chiefs and elite families, warrior retainers, ritual specialists, free farmers and herders, craftspeople, dependents, and a small household servile population; degrees of stratification varied by region, but kin status and resource control remained central.

allies.zh: 无固定国家盟友，但与地中海岛屿青铜文化、迈锡尼世界商旅网络、伊比利亚青铜时代社群及中欧青铜文化存在间接交换联系；联盟通常局限于近邻部落和区域聚落集团。
allies.en: There were no fixed state allies, but indirect exchange links existed with Mediterranean island Bronze cultures, Mycenaean trading networks, Iberian Bronze Age communities, and Central European Bronze cultures; alliances were usually limited to neighboring tribes and regional settlement groups.

enemies.zh: 无长期统一外敌，主要对手是邻近竞争性酋邦、争夺牧场和通道的山地群体，以及偶发海岸掠袭者。
enemies.en: There was no single long-term external enemy; the main opponents were rival neighboring chiefdoms, upland groups contesting pasture and route access, and occasional coastal raiders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持交换通道、婚姻联姻、互赠礼物、报复性袭扰和临时军事结盟为主，目标是巩固地方首领声望而非扩张领土国家。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining exchange routes, arranging marriage alliances, exchanging gifts, conducting retaliatory raids, and forming temporary military coalitions, aiming to strengthen local chiefly prestige rather than build a territorial state.

keyInnovations.zh: 木构设防聚落、壕沟与排水工程、青铜铸造技术、车轮运输的有限使用、纺织与陶器专业化、跨海与跨山地交换网络整合。
keyInnovations.en: Timber-built fortified settlements, ditch and drainage engineering, bronze casting technology, limited use of wheeled transport, specialization in textile and pottery production, and integration into maritime and trans-montane exchange networks.

strengths.zh: 优势在于多样生态环境下的牧农互补、设防聚落提供的安全性、灵活的部落联盟、活跃的区域交换以及逐渐增强的青铜工艺。
strengths.en: Strengths included agro-pastoral complementarity across diverse environments, security provided by fortified settlements, flexible tribal alliances, active regional exchange, and increasingly capable bronze craftsmanship.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治高度分散、缺乏文字与统一法制、财政和军力难以集中、对外部金属原料依赖较大，并容易因局部竞争而陷入持续冲突。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included extreme political fragmentation, the absence of writing and unified law, difficulty concentrating finance and military power, significant dependence on external metal supplies, and vulnerability to prolonged local conflict.

outlook.zh: 1600年前后总体稳定，但未来发展将取决于金属交换、聚落集中化和区域精英竞争。若设防聚落继续扩大并加强再分配功能，部分地区可能向更复杂的原始政体演化；若资源压力上升，则可能出现迁徙、聚落放弃和权力重组。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE the sphere was broadly stable, but its future depended on metal exchange, settlement concentration, and regional elite competition. If fortified settlements continued to grow and strengthen redistributive functions, some areas could evolve toward more complex proto-political structures; if resource pressure rose, migration, settlement abandonment, and power reorganization could follow.

description.zh: 意大利半岛北中南分布着多个防御聚落和山地酋邦，尚未形成国家体系。
description.en: Northern, central, and southern Italy contained fortified settlements and upland chiefdoms, not yet a state system.

name.zh: 阿尔加尔及伊比利亚青铜诸邦
name.en: El Argar and Iberian Bronze Polities

name.zh: 伊比利亚青铜时代文明

capital.zh: 以埃尔阿尔加尔等设防中心为核心
capital.en: Fortified centers such as El Argar

governmentForm.zh: 分层酋邦联盟，由若干区域性设防聚落及其附属村落构成
governmentForm.en: A hierarchical chiefdom network composed of regional fortified centers and their dependent villages

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层明显，顶层为军事与祭祀兼具的精英家族，其下为自由农牧民、工匠与依附者，底层可能包括受支配劳役人口
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with military-ritual elite families at the top, followed by free farmers, herders, and artisans, and at the bottom dependent laboring populations

rulingClass.zh: 控制山顶聚落、仓储、金属生产与武器分配的贵族家族
rulingClass.en: Noble lineages controlling hilltop settlements, storage, metal production, and weapon distribution

succession.zh: 以家族世袭为主，结合成年亲属间竞争与地方长老认可；不同聚落之间无统一王位继承制度
succession.en: Succession was mainly hereditary within ruling families, tempered by competition among adult kin and recognition by local elders; there was no single kingdom-wide succession system

structure.zh: 政治组织以区域酋长中心为核心，周边小型聚落向其输送粮食、劳役与金属资源；重要决策由酋长、家族成员、武士随从和长老共同参与
structure.en: Political organization centered on regional chiefdom strongholds, with surrounding smaller settlements supplying food, labor, and metal resources; major decisions involved the chieftain, kin group members, warrior retainers, and elders

function.zh: 征集谷物、畜产与织物，管理聚落仓库并分配口粮
function.en: Collected grain, livestock products, and textiles, managed communal storage, and distributed rations

function.zh: 监督铜、砷铜和青铜冶炼，控制武器、工具和饰品生产
function.en: Oversaw copper, arsenical copper, and bronze smelting and controlled production of weapons, tools, and ornaments

function.zh: 组织武装随从、守卫设防聚落、动员邻近村社战斗人员
function.en: Organized armed retainers, guarded fortified settlements, and mobilized fighters from nearby communities

function.zh: 安排耕作、放牧、修筑梯地、蓄水设施与公共劳动
function.en: Coordinated farming, herding, terrace construction, water management, and communal labor

function.zh: 主持埋葬、祖先纪念、婚姻联盟仪式与统治合法性象征活动
function.en: Conducted burials, ancestor commemorations, alliance marriages, and symbolic rituals legitimizing rule

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理依附于设防中心，基层由村落首领、家族长与酋长代理人负责征粮、征工和治安
localAdmin.en: Local administration was subordinate to fortified centers, with village heads, clan elders, and agents of the chieftain handling collection, labor obligations, and order

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典，规范主要依据习惯法、家族仲裁、酋长裁决和报复—赔偿机制；盗窃、违抗贡赋和暴力冲突会受到严厉处置
legalSystem.en: There was no written law code; norms rested on customary law, kin arbitration, chieftainly judgment, and retaliation-compensation mechanisms; theft, refusal of tribute, and violent disputes were treated harshly

taxationSystem.zh: 贡赋以实物和劳役为主，包括谷物、大麦、豆类、羊毛、牲畜、矿石、成品器物及季节性公共劳动
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of tribute in kind and labor, including cereals, barley, pulses, wool, livestock, ore, finished goods, and seasonal corvée labor

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、家庭与聚落祭祀、自然力量信仰和埋葬仪式为核心；精英墓葬体现社会等级与宗教象征
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, household and community rites, reverence for natural forces, and funerary ritual; elite burials expressed social rank and religious symbolism

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成抽象哲学传统，价值观强调血缘、勇武、荣誉、土地控制、祖先记忆与互惠义务
philosophy.en: No abstract philosophical tradition existed; values emphasized kinship, martial prowess, honor, control of land, ancestral memory, and reciprocal obligations

writingSystem.zh: 无本土书写系统，信息通过口述传统、记忆、象征器物和礼仪实践传承
writingSystem.en: There was no native writing system; information was transmitted through oral tradition, memory, symbolic objects, and ritual practice

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出高地设防聚落、精细金属器生产、标准化陶器、纺织技术、复杂墓葬制度与高度分层的社会组织，尤以阿尔加尔文化最具代表性
culturalAchievements.en: It developed fortified upland settlements, skilled metalworking, standardized pottery, textile production, complex burial customs, and highly stratified social organization, especially in the El Argar cultural sphere

languageFamily.zh: 前印欧与早期印欧成分并存，具体语言不可确证，可能属于伊比利亚半岛史前多语言环境
languageFamily.en: Likely a mix of pre-Indo-European and perhaps early Indo-European elements; the exact languages are unattested and belong to the prehistoric multilingual environment of Iberia

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和牧业为基础，辅以区域性矿冶和精英控制下的再分配。总体生产能力高于纯村落社会，但远低于同时代两河或埃及国家。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on subsistence farming and pastoralism, supplemented by regional mining, metallurgy, and elite-controlled redistribution. Productive capacity was above that of simple village societies but far below that of contemporary Mesopotamian or Egyptian states.

mainIndustries.zh: 旱作农业、羊群和山羊饲养、猪牛饲养、铜矿与银矿开采、冶金、陶器、纺织、骨角器制作与建筑
mainIndustries.en: Dry farming, sheep and goat herding, pig and cattle raising, copper and silver mining, metallurgy, pottery, textile production, bone and antler working, and construction

tradeGoods.zh: 铜锭、金属武器与工具、银饰、陶器、纺织品、羊毛、盐、牲畜、皮革以及少量琥珀、象牙等外来珍品
tradeGoods.en: Copper ingots, metal weapons and tools, silver ornaments, pottery, textiles, wool, salt, livestock, leather, and small quantities of prestige imports such as amber and ivory

name.zh: 无铸币，实物与金属重量交换
name.en: No coinage; barter and metal by weight

unitName.en: Weight silver and goods in kind

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为粮仓容量、牲畜数量、纺织工具和陶器；精英财富则集中于武器、金属器、贵重饰品和控制劳力的能力
householdWealth.en: For ordinary households, wealth lay mainly in stored grain, livestock, textile tools, and pottery; elite wealth was concentrated in weapons, metal objects, prestige ornaments, and control over labor

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产为基础、酋长再分配为上层整合机制的部落—酋邦经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal-chiefdom economy based on household production and integrated at the upper level through chieftainly redistribution

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Levies on mining and metal production
Corvée labor and public works equivalent

source.zh: 长途交换与精英馈赠再分配
source.en: Long-distance exchange and elite gift redistribution

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中铸币库藏，而是分散于多个设防中心的粮仓、牲群、金属锭、成品武器和贵重器物储备
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized coin reserve but a dispersed stock of grain stores, herds, metal ingots, finished weapons, and prestige goods held in multiple fortified centers

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏重实物征收和再分配，在稳定年份维持少量盈余以备歉收、修筑防御和巩固精英忠诚
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized collection in kind and redistribution, maintaining small surpluses in stable years for poor harvests, defensive works, and elite loyalty

description.zh: 由精英核心及其随从组成，装备较好的矛、短剑、匕首、木盾和少量护具，负责守卫中心聚落和执行突袭
description.en: Composed of the elite core and their retainers, equipped with relatively good spears, short swords, daggers, wooden shields, and limited armor, guarding major centers and carrying out raids

description.zh: 战时从附属村落征集，主要使用矛、投枪、石制或金属刀具、棍棒与简易盾牌
description.en: Raised from dependent villages in wartime, mainly using spears, javelins, stone or metal blades, clubs, and simple shields

description.zh: 熟悉山地和道路网络，平时护卫牧群与矿区，战时承担侦察、警戒和追击任务
description.en: Familiar with highland terrain and route networks, they guarded herds and mining zones in peacetime and served as scouts, sentries, and pursuers in war

commanderInChief.zh: 各区域酋长亲自统军，无统一最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: Regional chieftains commanded in person; there was no single overall commander

technology.zh: 军事技术以步战为主，使用铜与早期青铜武器、投射武器、设防山顶聚落、石墙和高地防御体系；无成熟骑兵与战车传统
technology.en: Military technology was primarily infantry-based, using copper and early bronze weapons, missile arms, fortified hilltop settlements, stone walls, and elevated defensive systems; there was no developed cavalry or chariot tradition

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自伊比利亚半岛内部的邻近酋邦竞争、资源争夺、矿区控制冲突和局部迁徙压力，而非远距帝国入侵
threats.en: The main threats came from rival chiefdoms within Iberia, competition over resources, struggles for control of mining zones, and localized migration pressures rather than invasion by distant empires

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于东南部和内陆若干山地与半干旱地区，以中小型村落、山顶设防中心及其附属农业社区为主，整体密度不高但局部中心化明显
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across southeastern and interior upland and semi-arid zones, concentrated in small and medium villages, hilltop fortified centers, and their associated farming communities; overall density was modest but local centralization was pronounced

name.zh: 卡斯特利翁阿尔托式设防聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为统治精英、武士与随从、专业工匠、自由农牧民、依附劳作者以及可能存在的低地位受控制人口
socialClasses.en: Society can be broadly divided into ruling elites, warriors and retainers, specialist artisans, free farmers and herders, dependent laborers, and possibly lower-status controlled populations

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟邦；主要通过婚姻联盟、交换网络和临时军事协作与伊比利亚其他部族群体维持关系
allies.en: There were no permanent unified allies; relations with other Iberian groups were maintained mainly through marriage alliances, exchange networks, and temporary military cooperation

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为邻近竞争性酋邦、争夺矿产与耕地的地方群体，以及可能进入边缘地区的流动部落
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring rival chiefdoms, local groups contesting mines and farmland, and mobile tribes entering frontier zones

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以区域均势、控制资源节点、维护通婚网络和争取珍贵外来物资为核心，尚未形成远距离国家式外交体系
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on regional balance, control of resource nodes, maintenance of marriage networks, and access to prestige imports; it had not developed into a long-distance state diplomatic system

keyInnovations.zh: 砷铜与早期青铜冶炼、设防高地聚落规划、标准化储藏与再分配设施、改进纺织工具、专业化陶器生产与精英武器制造
keyInnovations.en: Arsenical copper and early bronze metallurgy, planned fortified upland settlements, standardized storage and redistribution facilities, improved textile tools, specialized pottery production, and elite weapon manufacture

strengths.zh: 社会组织层级分明，设防聚落防御力较强，能够集中控制矿产、粮食与劳役；金属生产和精英再分配机制提升了区域统合能力
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in clear social hierarchy, strong fortified settlements, and the ability to concentrate control over minerals, food, and labor; metal production and elite redistribution improved regional integration

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一国家结构和文字行政，政治整合依赖地方家族；农业受气候波动制约明显，内部冲突和资源竞争容易削弱稳定
weaknesses.en: It lacked a unified state structure and written administration, with political cohesion dependent on local ruling families; agriculture was highly vulnerable to climatic fluctuation, and internal conflict and resource competition could undermine stability

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后总体稳定，但其稳定建立在区域精英控制与脆弱生态平衡之上；若出现长期干旱、贸易中断或精英权力斗争，社会网络可能迅速分裂重组
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE it was broadly stable, but that stability rested on regional elite control and a fragile ecological balance; prolonged drought, trade disruption, or elite power struggles could quickly fragment and reorganize the social network

description.zh: 伊比利亚半岛东南和内陆存在层级分明的青铜时代酋邦社会。
description.en: Southeastern and interior Iberia contained hierarchical Bronze Age chiefdom societies.

name.zh: 不列颠与威塞克斯文化诸酋邦
name.en: British Bronze Chiefdoms of the Wessex Sphere

governmentForm.zh: 由多个区域性酋邦构成的部落首领制联盟网络，权力建立在亲族、礼物交换、战士追随者与对金属资源和长途贸易的控制之上。
governmentForm.en: A tribal chiefdom network of multiple regional polities, with authority based on kinship, gift exchange, warrior followings, and control of metal resources and long-distance trade.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层明显但仍以部落和亲族为基础，上层为埋葬精英与酋长家族，中层为自由农牧民、工匠与战士，下层包括依附者、仆役及少量奴隶或战争俘虏。
socialStructure.en: Society was clearly stratified but still grounded in tribe and kinship, with burial elites and chiefly lineages at the top, free farmers, herders, craftsmen, and warriors in the middle, and dependents, servants, and a small number of slaves or war captives below.

rulingClass.zh: 区域酋长、其亲族集团、持有武器和礼器的战士贵族，以及控制铜、锡、黄金和琥珀交换的贸易精英。
rulingClass.en: Regional chiefs, their kin groups, warrior aristocrats possessing weapons and prestige goods, and exchange elites controlling copper, tin, gold, and amber traffic.

succession.zh: 通常为家族内继承，多偏向父系或同宗支系继承，但实际继位常取决于成年资格、武力、婚姻联盟与追随者支持。
succession.en: Succession was usually within chiefly families, often favoring patrilineal or same-lineage inheritance, but in practice depended on adult status, military strength, marriage alliances, and support from followers.

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家官僚机构。各地酋邦由酋长家庭、长老会议、战士随从、祭仪主持者和管理牲畜、仓储与工艺的家内人员共同治理；跨区域事务依靠婚盟、赠礼、集会与季节性仪式协调。
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy. Each chiefdom was governed by the chief's household, councils of elders, warrior retainers, ritual specialists, and household managers overseeing livestock, storage, and craft activity; interregional affairs were coordinated through marriage alliances, gift exchange, assemblies, and seasonal ceremonies.

function.zh: 管理粮食储备、牲畜、宴飨、礼物分配、来客接待及劳役组织，是最接近“行政核心”的单位。
function.en: Managed food stores, livestock, feasting, gift distribution, guest reception, and labor organization, functioning as the closest equivalent to an administrative core.

function.zh: 负责护卫酋长、组织突袭与报复、守护高地据点和重要通道，并在争端中展示武力。
function.en: Protected chiefs, organized raids and reprisals, guarded hilltop sites and key routes, and displayed force in disputes.

name.zh: 祭仪与祖先崇拜主持者

function.zh: 主持葬礼、祭献、季节仪式和合法化酋长权威的宗教活动，维护墓冢与圣地传统。
function.en: Conducted funerary rites, offerings, seasonal ceremonies, and religious acts legitimizing chiefly authority, while maintaining barrows and sacred sites.

function.zh: 监管青铜铸造、原料金属分配、礼器流通以及与爱尔兰、布列塔尼和欧洲大陆的交换联系。
function.en: Oversaw bronze casting, allocation of raw metal, circulation of prestige objects, and exchange links with Ireland, Brittany, and continental Europe.

name.en: Council of Elders and Dispute Mediation

function.zh: 协调土地、婚姻、血仇、牲畜损失和边界争端，以习惯法和赔偿维持秩序。
function.en: Handled disputes over land, marriage, blood-feud, livestock losses, and boundaries, maintaining order through customary law and compensation.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落、谷地和小型酋邦为单位。酋长通过亲族首领、村社长老和追随者间接施政，缺乏固定书面行政区划。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages, valleys, and small chiefdoms. Chiefs governed indirectly via kin leaders, village elders, and retainers, without fixed written territorial divisions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是口头习惯法，强调赔偿、和解、血亲责任、誓言和公开仲裁；严重冲突则可能导致复仇、放逐或武力裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of oral customary practice, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, kin liability, oath-taking, and public arbitration; severe conflict could lead to feud, exile, or armed settlement.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一定额税制。酋长收入主要来自贡纳、宴飨供给、季节性劳役、牲畜上缴、战利品分配和对金属交换的控制。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform fixed tax system. Chiefly income came mainly from tribute, feast contributions, seasonal labor, livestock renders, shares of booty, and control over metal exchange.

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然神圣观和地方性祭仪为核心，重视太阳、河流、泉水、丘陵和墓冢等神圣空间，常以器物埋藏、湿地献祭和隆重葬仪表达信仰。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, sacred views of nature, and local ritual practice, with special importance attached to the sun, rivers, springs, hills, and burial mounds; belief was often expressed through hoards, wetland offerings, and elaborate funerary rites.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学传统，世界观建立在祖先记忆、荣誉、互惠、血缘义务与人与土地、季节循环相连的宗教观念之上。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition; worldview rested on ancestral memory, honor, reciprocity, kin obligations, and religious ideas linking people to land and seasonal cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 无本地书写系统，知识、谱系、仪式和法律记忆通过口头传承、歌谣、纪念物和葬仪传统保存。
writingSystem.en: There was no native writing system; knowledge, genealogy, ritual, and legal memory were preserved through oral transmission, song, monuments, and funerary tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 突出成就包括圆冢与墓地景观建设、精美青铜武器和首饰制作、黄金饰物工艺、长距离金属贸易网络，以及与斯通亨奇后期仪式景观相关的纪念性传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included the construction of round barrows and cemetery landscapes, fine bronze weapons and ornaments, goldworking, long-distance metal trade networks, and monumental traditions associated with later ritual landscapes such as Stonehenge.

languageFamily.zh: 语言不明，可能属于前凯尔特的印欧方言与更早本地语言并存；无文字记载，无法精确分类。
languageFamily.en: The language is uncertain, possibly involving pre-Celtic Indo-European dialects alongside older local languages; without writing, precise classification is impossible.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业和畜牧业为基础，辅以青铜冶铸、黄金加工和地区间交换。整体生产水平不高，但在锡、金和礼器流通方面具有超出其人口规模的区域重要性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on farming and pastoralism, supplemented by bronze metallurgy, goldworking, and regional exchange. Overall productivity was modest, but the society held importance beyond its population size through tin, gold, and prestige-goods circulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、青铜铸造、采矿与淘金、陶器制作、纺织、木工、皮革加工和礼器生产。
mainIndustries.en: Cereal agriculture, animal husbandry, bronze casting, mining and gold washing, pottery, textile production, woodworking, leatherworking, and prestige-object manufacture.

tradeGoods.zh: 锡、铜制品、青铜武器与工具、黄金饰物、琥珀、兽皮、羊毛、盐、陶器及少量异域奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Tin, copper goods, bronze weapons and tools, gold ornaments, amber, hides, wool, salt, pottery, and small amounts of exotic luxury items.

unitName.zh: 以牲畜、金属与礼物计值
unitName.en: Value reckoned in livestock, metal, and gift goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、牛羊数量、储粮、金属工具、武器和饰物；精英则通过冢墓随葬品和宴飨能力展示财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to land, numbers of cattle and sheep, stored grain, metal tools, weapons, and ornaments; elites displayed wealth through grave goods and their ability to sponsor feasts.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、自给农业、酋长再分配、互惠交换和礼物经济为主，长途贸易主要集中于金属原料和精英消费品。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was dominated by household production, subsistence farming, chiefly redistribution, reciprocal exchange, and a gift economy, with long-distance trade focused chiefly on metal raw materials and elite goods.

source.en: Profits from control of metal exchange

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国库，财富分散储存在各酋长家庭、牲畜群、金属锭、礼器、黄金饰物和储粮中；部分财富以可埋藏器物形式保存。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single state treasury; wealth was dispersed among chiefly households in herds, metal ingots, prestige goods, gold ornaments, and grain stores, with some wealth preserved in hoarded objects.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践以维系酋长威望为核心，通过再分配、宴飨、奖赏追随者和资助仪式巩固统治，而非追求制度化征税。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on sustaining chiefly prestige through redistribution, feasting, rewarding followers, and sponsoring rituals, rather than through institutionalized taxation.

description.zh: 由酋长直接维持的核心武装，装备较好的青铜剑、匕首、矛和盾牌，负责护卫、突袭和威慑。
description.en: The core armed force maintained directly by chiefs, equipped with better bronze swords, daggers, spears, and shields for protection, raiding, and intimidation.

description.zh: 由自由农牧民战时动员组成，主要使用木矛、石或青铜尖头武器、斧和盾，作战持续能力有限。
description.en: Raised in wartime from free farmers and herders, using mainly wooden spears, stone- or bronze-tipped weapons, axes, and shields, with limited sustained combat capability.

description.zh: 受欧洲大陆影响出现的少量车乘与马匹精英，更多具有身份和机动象征意义，实际战场作用有限且地区分布不均。
description.en: A small elite of horse and chariot users influenced by continental Europe, serving more as symbols of status and mobility than as decisive battlefield forces, and unevenly distributed by region.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地酋长分别统兵
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; each chief led his own forces

technology.zh: 军用技术处于中等青铜时代水平，使用青铜剑、矛、斧、匕首、木盾、皮革防护和少量马车；无攻城体系，也缺乏职业化军队。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a middling Bronze Age level, using bronze swords, spears, axes, daggers, wooden shields, leather protection, and a limited number of chariots; there was no siege system and no professional army in the state sense.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦之间的袭击、牲畜掠夺、边界争夺和贸易路线竞争；外来大规模入侵在此时期并不突出，更多是海峡与海路上的文化和人口流动。
threats.en: The main threats came from raids between neighboring chiefdoms, cattle rustling, boundary conflicts, and competition over trade routes; large-scale foreign invasion was not a major issue in this period, though movement across sea routes and the Channel did occur.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住于英格兰、威尔士、苏格兰南部和部分爱尔兰海沿岸联系区，以小型农庄、村落和季节性牧场网络为主，区域密度差异很大。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across England, Wales, southern Scotland, and connected Irish Sea zones, living mainly in small farmsteads, villages, and seasonal pasture networks, with major regional differences in density.

name.zh: 无真正城市；韦塞克斯仪式核心区
name.en: No true cities; Wessex ritual core zone

name.zh: 无真正城市；巨石阵—埃夫伯里地区
无真正城市；康沃尔锡矿区聚落群
name.en: No true cities; Cornish tin-mining settlement clusters

socialClasses.zh: 社会大致分为酋长与贵族家族、战士与仪式精英、自由农牧民与工匠、依附者和仆役，以及少量奴隶或俘虏。
socialClasses.en: Society was broadly divided into chiefs and noble families, warrior and ritual elites, free farmers, herders and craftsmen, dependents and servants, and a small number of slaves or captives.

allies.zh: 不存在统一外交盟友，但许多不列颠酋邦与爱尔兰、布列塔尼、低地国家和大西洋沿岸欧洲青铜时代群体保持婚姻、贸易和礼物交换关系。
allies.en: There were no unified diplomatic allies, but many British chiefdoms maintained marriage, trade, and gift-exchange relations with Ireland, Brittany, the Low Countries, and Bronze Age communities along the Atlantic façade.

enemies.zh: 没有固定的整体性外敌，敌对关系主要存在于相邻酋邦之间，围绕牧场、矿产、荣誉和交换通道展开。
enemies.en: There were no permanent civilizational enemies; hostility existed mainly between neighboring chiefdoms over pasture, mineral resources, honor, and exchange routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以获取金属、维持海上交换网络、缔结婚盟和展示精英声望为核心，外交更多体现为礼物往来而非正式条约。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on securing metals, maintaining maritime exchange networks, arranging marriage alliances, and displaying elite prestige; diplomacy took the form of gift exchange rather than formal treaties.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括成熟的青铜冶铸、分段模铸工艺、黄金薄片与饰物制作、轮式交通和马匹使用的有限扩展、改良木工、织造与羊毛加工，以及更系统的墓冢与地景营造。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature bronze metallurgy, sectional-mold casting, gold sheet and ornament production, limited expansion of wheeled transport and horse use, improved woodworking, weaving and wool processing, and more systematic construction of barrows and ritual landscapes.

strengths.zh: 优势在于灵活的区域网络、丰富的锡资源、活跃的海上交换、强烈的酋长威望政治和高水平礼器工艺，使其在西欧青铜贸易中具有重要地位。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in flexible regional networks, rich tin resources, active seaborne exchange, strong chiefly prestige politics, and high-quality prestige crafts, giving it an important role in western European bronze trade.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治高度分裂、缺乏统一行政和书写、军事动员能力有限、经济剩余较低，且对气候波动、歉收和贸易中断较为敏感。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, lack of unified administration and writing, limited military mobilization, low economic surplus, and sensitivity to climatic fluctuation, bad harvests, and disruption of trade.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后，该文明总体稳定，并将继续通过金属贸易、葬仪文化和区域酋邦竞争发展；但未来变化将更多来自内部权力重组和跨海峡文化联系，而非形成统一国家。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE this civilization was broadly stable and likely to continue developing through metal trade, funerary culture, and rivalry among regional chiefdoms; future change would come more from internal reorganization and cross-Channel contacts than from the emergence of a unified state.

description.zh: 不列颠群岛分布着依赖金属贸易、墓葬精英与区域联盟的青铜时代酋邦。
description.en: The British Isles were divided among Bronze Age chiefdoms tied to metal trade, burial elites, and regional alliances.

name.zh: 北欧早期青铜时代文化

governmentForm.zh: 由区域性酋邦组成的分散首领制网络，以宗族、战士随从和长距离交换关系维系权威。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of regional chiefdoms, in which authority was sustained through kinship, warrior retinues, and long-distance exchange ties.

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈分层结构，上层为控制土地、船只、金属器与祭祀活动的酋长家族，其下为自由农牧民、工匠与武士追随者，底层包括依附者、债务仆役和少量奴隶。
socialStructure.en: Society was stratified, with chiefly families controlling land, boats, metal goods, and ritual activity at the top, followed by free farming and pastoral households, artisans, and warrior retainers, with dependents, debt-servants, and a limited number of slaves at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、战士贵族与主持仪式的宗教领袖共同构成统治阶层。
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly lineages, warrior elites, and ritual leaders together formed the ruling class.

succession.zh: 以家族继承为主，通常在显赫宗族内部传承，但需获得亲族、战士追随者与地方集会的认可。
succession.en: Succession was primarily hereditary within prominent lineages, but required acceptance by kin groups, warrior followers, and local assemblies.

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构，政治权力分布于日德兰半岛、丹麦群岛、斯堪的纳维亚南部与波罗的海沿岸的多个酋邦。大型墓冢、仪式中心、会盟地点与港湾构成权力节点。
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; political power was distributed among multiple chiefdoms in Jutland, the Danish islands, southern Scandinavia, and the Baltic littoral. Large barrows, ritual centers, assembly places, and harbors served as nodes of authority.

function.zh: 征集谷物、牲畜、毛皮、琥珀与手工制品，集中储藏后用于宴饮、祭祀、赠礼和对外交换。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, hides, amber, and craft goods, storing them for feasting, ritual, gift exchange, and external trade.

function.zh: 组织海上航线、维护船只、接待外来商旅，并协调琥珀输出与铜锡输入。
function.en: Organized sea routes, maintained boats, received foreign traders, and coordinated amber exports with copper and tin imports.

function.zh: 主持墓葬、献祭、太阳崇拜仪式与季节性集会，借宗教活动巩固酋长合法性。
function.en: Oversaw burials, offerings, solar rituals, and seasonal gatherings, using religion to reinforce chiefly legitimacy.

function.zh: 维持酋长卫队、组织劫掠或护航行动，并在部族冲突中集结兵员。
function.en: Maintained chiefly guards, organized raiding or convoy protection, and mobilized fighters during intergroup conflict.

function.zh: 监管青铜铸造、武器与饰物生产，以及高地位物品的再分配。
function.en: Supervised bronze casting, the production of weapons and ornaments, and the redistribution of high-status goods.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村落长者、宗族首领和区域酋长。行政并非文官化体制，而是以亲属网络、口头协商和集会裁决为基础。
localAdmin.en: Local administration depended on village elders, clan heads, and regional chiefs. Governance was not bureaucratic but based on kinship networks, oral negotiation, and assembly judgment.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以习惯法、赔偿原则、血亲调解和集体仲裁为核心。对杀伤、盗窃、婚姻纠纷和财产争议多采用赔偿、誓言或报复性制裁。
legalSystem.en: The legal system centered on customary law, compensation principles, kin mediation, and collective arbitration. Injury, theft, marriage disputes, and property conflicts were commonly addressed through compensation, oaths, or retaliatory sanctions.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一定额税制，酋长通过贡赋、馈赠义务、宴饮征收、战利品分配和对贸易节点的控制获取资源。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform fixed tax system; chiefs obtained resources through tribute, gift obligations, feast levies, distribution of war booty, and control over trade nodes.

religion.zh: 宗教以太阳崇拜、祖先祭祀、自然神圣观和武器、船只、金属器的献祭为核心。沼泽地和水域常被视为通向神灵的祭祀空间。
religion.en: Religion centered on sun worship, ancestor veneration, sacred views of nature, and the offering of weapons, boats, and metal goods. Bogs and waters were often regarded as ritual spaces connecting to divine powers.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成文哲学体系，但世界观强调祖先血统、荣誉、交换义务、战士声望以及太阳循环与季节秩序。
philosophy.en: No formal written philosophy existed, but the worldview emphasized ancestral lineage, honor, exchange obligations, warrior prestige, and the solar and seasonal order.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，信息主要通过口传传统、象征图像、器物装饰和仪式记忆传递。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, symbolic imagery, object decoration, and ritual memory.

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就包括精美青铜剑、斧、剃刀与号角铸造，巨型墓冢建设，岩画传统，以及展现太阳车、船与战士意象的复杂象征体系。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included fine casting of bronze swords, axes, razors, and lur horns, the construction of monumental barrows, rock art traditions, and a complex symbolic system featuring solar carts, ships, and warriors.

languageFamily.zh: 可能属于早期印欧语支前阶段或相关方言连续体，但具体分类仍不确定。
languageFamily.en: It likely belonged to an early Indo-European branch or a related dialect continuum, though its exact classification remains uncertain.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以混合农业、畜牧、渔猎、手工业和长距离交换为基础。按人均产出衡量属中低水平，但因控制琥珀与海上通道而在跨区域贸易中占有重要位置。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on mixed farming, herding, fishing, hunting, craft production, and long-distance exchange. Output per person was modest, but control of amber and maritime routes gave the culture an important place in interregional trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、渔业、木工、造船、纺织、皮革加工、琥珀采集与加工、青铜铸造。
mainIndustries.en: Cereal cultivation, livestock herding, fishing, woodworking, boatbuilding, textile production, leatherworking, amber collection and working, and bronze casting.

tradeGoods.zh: 主要输出琥珀、毛皮、皮革、羊毛制品、可能的奴隶、干鱼和地方工艺品；主要输入铜、锡、青铜制成品、玻璃珠及南方奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Main exports included amber, furs, hides, woolen goods, possible slaves, dried fish, and local craft items; main imports included copper, tin, finished bronze goods, glass beads, and southern prestige items.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、船只份额和家庭工具；精英财富则体现在青铜武器、饰物、大墓冢和远方珍品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was reflected in land use rights, livestock, grain stores, shares in boats, and household tools; elite wealth appeared in bronze weapons, ornaments, large barrows, and exotic prestige goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度属于以家庭生产为基础、由酋长再分配和礼物交换调节的区域贸易经济，市场因素存在但未制度化。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a regional trade economy based on household production and regulated by chiefly redistribution and gift exchange, with market-like behavior present but not institutionalized.

source.zh: 手工业与金属作坊贡纳

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非统一国家金库，而是分散在各大酋长中心的储藏财富，包括金属锭、成品青铜器、琥珀、牲畜、谷物和礼品库存。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not a unified state reserve but wealth dispersed among major chiefly centers, including metal ingots, finished bronze goods, amber, livestock, grain, and gift stockpiles.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持酋长威望为目标，重视再分配、宴饮、祭祀与战士赏赐；盈余通常用于储备稀有金属或资助新的交换远航。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at maintaining chiefly prestige, emphasizing redistribution, feasting, ritual, and rewards for warriors; surpluses were typically used to store rare metals or finance new exchange voyages.

description.zh: 由酋长长期供养的核心武装，装备较好的青铜剑、斧、矛和盾，承担护卫、威慑和突袭任务。
description.en: The core armed force maintained by chiefs, equipped with relatively good bronze swords, axes, spears, and shields, serving in guard, intimidation, and raiding roles.

description.zh: 由自由农民和青年男子在冲突时集结，武器多样，训练不一，适用于防御、集体械斗和短程作战。
description.en: Raised from free farmers and young men during conflict, they carried varied weapons and had uneven training, suitable for defense, collective fighting, and short-range operations.

description.zh: 依托海船行动的战斗人员，负责沿岸巡航、护送贸易航程、劫掠对手和控制海峡要道。
description.en: Fighting crews operating from boats, responsible for coastal patrol, convoy escort, raiding rivals, and controlling strategic straits.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各区域大酋长分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; major regional chiefs commanded separately

technology.zh: 军事技术以青铜剑、矛、斧、木盾、皮甲和海上机动能力为特征；尚无成熟骑兵体系，战车影响有限且多属威望象征。
technology.en: Military technology was characterized by bronze swords, spears, axes, wooden shields, leather protection, and maritime mobility; there was no mature cavalry system, and chariot influence was limited and mostly symbolic of prestige.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦间的竞争、对海上通道与琥珀来源的争夺、波罗的海沿岸冲突以及偶发性劫掠与报复战。
threats.en: Major threats came from rivalry among neighboring chiefdoms, competition over sea routes and amber sources, conflicts along the Baltic littoral, and episodic raiding and revenge warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在日德兰半岛、丹麦群岛、斯堪的纳维亚南部和部分波罗的海沿岸地区，以农村聚落为主，密度较低但沿海和优质农耕地带较集中。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in Jutland, the Danish islands, southern Scandinavia, and parts of the Baltic littoral, mostly in rural settlements with low overall density but greater concentration along coasts and good farming zones.

name.zh: 无真正城市，主要为区域中心聚落与港湾
name.en: No true cities, only regional central settlements and harbors

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括酋长与贵族家族、战士与随从、专业工匠、自由农牧民、依附者及少量奴隶。社会差异在墓葬规模、青铜器拥有量和远方奢侈品分配上表现明显。
socialClasses.en: Major social classes included chiefly and noble families, warriors and retainers, specialist artisans, free farmers and herders, dependents, and a limited number of slaves. Social differences were clearly visible in burial size, possession of bronze goods, and access to exotic prestige items.

allies.zh: 无固定国家级盟友，但与中欧青铜时代文化、不列颠青铜时代社群以及波罗的海沿岸诸集团保持互利交换与阶段性合作关系。
allies.en: There were no fixed state-level allies, but mutually beneficial exchange and periodic cooperation existed with Central European Bronze Age cultures, British Bronze Age communities, and groups along the Baltic littoral.

enemies.zh: 无长期统一敌国，主要对手是争夺贸易节点、航道和资源的邻近酋邦与沿岸竞争集团。
enemies.en: There was no single long-term enemy state; the main rivals were neighboring chiefdoms and coastal competitor groups contesting trade nodes, routes, and resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持交换网络、保障海上通道、通过馈赠建立声望联盟以及避免破坏琥珀与金属流通的全面战争为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining exchange networks, securing maritime routes, building prestige alliances through gift exchange, and avoiding full-scale wars that would disrupt amber and metal circulation.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括成熟的青铜铸造、失蜡与分铸工艺的区域应用、长船建造、羊毛纺织、木工加工、墓冢工程和复杂装饰纹样制作。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included developed bronze casting, regional use of lost-wax and sectional casting techniques, longboat construction, wool textile production, woodworking, barrow engineering, and complex decorative patterning.

strengths.zh: 优势在于控制优质琥珀资源、拥有熟练航海与沿海联络能力、精英网络稳固，并能将外来青铜与本地威望体系结合。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in control of high-quality amber resources, skilled seafaring and coastal communication, strong elite networks, and the ability to integrate imported bronze into local prestige systems.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一国家结构、对外来铜锡供应依赖较大、人口分散、军事动员受季节和地方忠诚限制。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the lack of a unified state structure, heavy dependence on imported copper and tin, dispersed population, and military mobilization limited by seasonality and local loyalties.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后，该文化总体稳定，若外部金属供应和海上通道持续畅通，精英酋邦体系将进一步繁荣，并推动更复杂的社会分层与象征文化发展。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, this culture was broadly stable; if external metal supplies and maritime routes remained open, the chiefly system would likely prosper further and drive more complex social stratification and symbolic development.

description.zh: 斯堪的纳维亚与波罗的海沿岸通过琥珀和青铜交换联系成广域网络。
description.en: Scandinavia and the Baltic littoral were connected by broad exchange networks in amber and bronze.

name.zh: 辛塔什塔－安德罗诺沃文化圈

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族和部落联盟为基础的酋邦体制，战时可形成临时性的跨部落军事联盟。
governmentForm.en: A clan- and tribe-based chiefdom system, with temporary supratribal military coalitions formed in time of war.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以父系氏族、家族牧群单位和战车武士集团为核心，兼有金属工匠、祭司性人物、自由牧民与依附者。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on patrilineal clans, family herding units, and chariot-warrior groups, alongside metalworkers, ritual specialists, free pastoralists, and dependents.

rulingClass.zh: 拥有大型畜群、战车装备和祭祀威望的部落首领家族与武士贵族。
rulingClass.en: Tribal leading families and warrior aristocrats who controlled large herds, chariot equipment, and ritual prestige.

succession.zh: 以家族世袭为主，但需获得部落长老、战士集团与亲族网络的认可；强势首领常通过婚姻结盟与战功巩固继承。
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within leading families, but succession required acceptance by tribal elders, warrior groups, and kin networks; strong leaders often secured succession through marriage alliances and military prestige.

structure.zh: 不存在高度官僚化国家机构，政治运作依靠部落议事、长老协商、战时统帅与祭祀权威；在大型聚落和 fortified settlements 周边可见较稳定的首领中心。
structure.en: There was no highly bureaucratic state apparatus; politics operated through tribal councils, elder consultation, wartime commanders, and ritual authority, with relatively stable chiefly centers around large fortified settlements.

function.zh: 协调部落间关系、仲裁纠纷、确认首领权威并决定迁徙、战争与大型祭祀。
function.en: Coordinated intertribal relations, arbitrated disputes, affirmed chiefly authority, and decided on migrations, warfare, and major rituals.

function.zh: 组织季节性转场、分配放牧区域、保护水源并统计畜群义务。
function.en: Organized seasonal transhumance, allocated grazing zones, protected water sources, and tracked herd obligations.

function.zh: 在战争中征集战士、维护战车和兵器、组织突袭与防御。
function.en: Mobilized warriors in wartime, maintained chariots and weapons, and organized raids and defense.

function.zh: 主持火祭、葬礼、誓约仪式和与太阳、火、祖先相关的宗教活动。
function.en: Oversaw fire rituals, funerary rites, oath ceremonies, and religious practices tied to the sun, fire, and ancestors.

function.zh: 管理铜矿、锡源交换、铸造作坊和武器生产分配。
function.en: Managed copper sources, exchange for tin, foundry workshops, and the distribution of weapon production.

localAdmin.zh: 基层管理由氏族长、家族长和聚落首领承担，各冬营地、夏牧场和设防聚落拥有相对自治权。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by clan heads, household elders, and settlement chiefs; winter camps, summer pastures, and fortified settlements retained substantial autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、血亲义务、赔偿原则和公开誓约为基础，重视牲畜赔偿、婚姻安排、复仇调解与战利品分配。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary norms, kin obligations, compensation principles, and public oaths, emphasizing livestock compensation, marriage arrangements, revenge settlement, and division of spoils.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，首领通过贡纳牲畜、战利品分成、婚姻馈赠、祭祀献纳和对工匠产出的控制取得资源。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; chiefs drew resources through livestock tribute, shares of war booty, marriage gifts, ritual offerings, and control over craft output.

religion.zh: 宗教以祖先崇拜、火与太阳崇拜、天空神祇、誓约仪式及与战车、马匹相关的祭祀为核心，墓葬与随葬品反映强烈的来世观念与身份等级。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, fire and solar cults, sky deities, oath rituals, and ceremonies linked to chariots and horses; burials and grave goods reflect strong beliefs in the afterlife and social rank.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成文哲学体系，但其价值观强调勇武、亲族忠诚、慷慨馈赠、契约誓言、祭祀纯洁与人与牲畜及自然周期的和谐。
philosophy.en: No formal written philosophy existed, but core values emphasized martial prowess, kin loyalty, generous gift exchange, binding oaths, ritual purity, and harmony between people, livestock, and seasonal cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统，信息主要通过口述传统、仪式记忆、谱系传承和物质符号传播。
writingSystem.en: There was no indigenous writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, ritual memory, genealogy, and material symbols.

culturalAchievements.zh: 其代表性成就在于成熟的轻型战车传统、马匹驾驭技术、高水平青铜冶铸、设防聚落规划、复杂墓葬仪式以及对后世印欧草原文化的深远影响。
culturalAchievements.en: Its hallmark achievements included a mature light-chariot tradition, advanced horse management, high-level bronze metallurgy, planned fortified settlements, complex funerary rites, and profound influence on later Indo-European steppe cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 早期印欧语系，通常与印伊语支前身相关。
languageFamily.en: Early Indo-European, commonly associated with the pre-Indo-Iranian branch.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧业为主，辅以青铜冶炼、骨角器制作、少量农业和远距离交换。财富主要体现在牛、马、羊及金属武器、车具和首饰上，而非城市市场中的货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was primarily pastoral, supplemented by bronze metallurgy, bone and horn craftwork, limited farming, and long-distance exchange. Wealth was stored mainly in cattle, horses, sheep, and in metal weapons, vehicle fittings, and ornaments rather than in monetized urban markets.

mainIndustries.zh: 马牛羊畜牧、青铜冶炼与铸造、采矿、战车制造、皮革加工、骨角器制作、织物与毡制品生产。
mainIndustries.en: Horse, cattle, and sheep herding; bronze smelting and casting; mining; chariot manufacture; leatherworking; bone and antler craft; and textile and felt production.

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、战车技术、青铜器、铜锭、兽皮、羊毛、牲畜、骨角器、装饰品，以及换入的锡、珍贵石材和异域金属制品。
tradeGoods.en: Horses, chariot technology, bronze goods, copper ingots, hides, wool, livestock, bone and antler products, ornaments, and imported tin, semi-precious stones, and exotic metal goods.

unitName.en: head of livestock / weight of metal

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要由牲畜群、车具、帐居器物和亲族支持网络构成；精英家庭则拥有战车、更多马匹、青铜武器与显赫墓葬资源。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of herds, vehicle gear, household equipment, and kin support networks; elite households held chariots, more horses, bronze weapons, and resources for prestigious burials.

economicSystem.zh: 以牧群私有、亲族再分配、首领馈赠经济和跨草原交换网络并存的混合型部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribal economy combining private herd ownership, kin-based redistribution, chiefly gift exchange, and trans-steppe trade networks.

category.en: Support for specialists and resource reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中储存于单一都城，而是分散在首领家族的畜群、金属贮藏、祭祀器物和盟友馈赠网络之中。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not concentrated in a single capital, but dispersed across chiefly herds, metal hoards, ritual objects, and alliance gift networks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在于维持武士追随者、保障迁徙与战争动员、通过馈赠维系忠诚，并把稀缺金属资源投入高威望用途。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal priorities focused on maintaining warrior followings, supporting migration and war mobilization, preserving loyalty through gifts, and channeling scarce metal resources into high-prestige uses.

description.zh: 由精英战士组成，使用双轮轻型战车、复合战术配合长矛、弓箭和近战兵器，是该文化圈最具声望的军事力量。
description.en: Composed of elite fighters using light two-wheeled chariots with combined tactics employing spears, bows, and close-combat weapons; this was the most prestigious military arm of the sphere.

description.zh: 主要承担侦察、追踪、警戒和跨草原联络，虽尚非成熟重骑兵，但在机动性上极具优势。
description.en: Responsible mainly for reconnaissance, tracking, screening, and steppe communications; not yet a mature heavy cavalry force, but highly mobile.

description.zh: 由自由牧民动员而成，装备矛、斧、弓和短刃，适合防守营地、伏击和支援战车行动。
description.en: Mobilized from free pastoralists and armed with spears, axes, bows, and short blades, they were suited for camp defense, ambushes, and support of chariot operations.

description.zh: 驻守设防聚落、金属工坊和关键渡口，负责木土防御设施和资源点的保护。
description.en: Stationed in fortified settlements, metallurgical sites, and key crossings, they protected timber-earth defenses and strategic resource locations.

commanderInChief.zh: 战时推举的大酋长或联盟统帅
commanderInChief.en: A wartime elected great chieftain or coalition commander

technology.zh: 军事技术核心包括成熟的辐条双轮战车、马匹驯控、青铜矛头与斧、复合木骨角结构武器部件、皮革护具以及设防环壕聚落。
technology.en: Core military technology included mature spoked two-wheeled chariots, horse control techniques, bronze spearheads and axes, composite wood-bone-antler weapon components, leather protection, and fortified ring-ditch settlements.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自草原内部部落竞争、牧场与水源争夺、向南接触绿洲与农耕社会时的冲突，以及迁徙过程中对补给和牲畜安全的压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal steppe competition, struggles over pasture and water, conflict with oasis and farming societies to the south, and pressures on supply and herd security during migrations.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布广泛而分散，集中于乌拉尔以东至西西伯利亚和哈萨克草原北部的河谷、冬营地、设防聚落与季节性牧场之间。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely dispersed, concentrated between the eastern Urals, western Siberia, and the northern Kazakh steppe across river valleys, winter camps, fortified settlements, and seasonal grazing zones.

socialClasses.zh: 社会大体可分为部落首领与战车贵族、祭祀与仪式人物、金属工匠与专门手工业者、自由牧民家庭以及依附者和战俘。
socialClasses.en: Society can be broadly divided into tribal chiefs and chariot aristocrats, ritual specialists, metalworkers and craft specialists, free pastoral householders, and dependents or captives.

allies.zh: 无固定国家盟友，但与部分安德罗诺沃相关部落、南西伯利亚草原群体及中亚北缘交换网络保持松散合作关系。
allies.en: There were no fixed state allies, but loose cooperative ties existed with related Andronovo tribes, southern Siberian steppe groups, and exchange networks along the northern margins of Central Asia.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象并不固定，主要是竞争牧场的草原部落，以及在向南扩张和贸易接触中发生冲突的边缘绿洲与农耕群体。
enemies.en: Enemies were not fixed, consisting mainly of rival steppe tribes competing for pasture and marginal oasis or farming communities encountered in southern expansion and trade contact.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以迁徙、联姻、馈赠、掠袭和技术传播并行为特征；其影响力更多依靠机动战争力、马匹与战车技术以及跨区域交换而非领土官僚统治。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was characterized by migration, marriage alliances, gift exchange, raiding, and technology transfer; influence rested more on mobility, horse and chariot expertise, and interregional exchange than on territorial bureaucratic rule.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键创新包括辐条轻型战车、系统化马匹驾驭、先进青铜冶铸、矿冶组织化生产、设防环形聚落设计以及跨草原远距离交流体系。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included the spoked light chariot, systematic horse management, advanced bronze metallurgy, organized mining and metal production, fortified circular settlement planning, and long-distance trans-steppe communication networks.

strengths.zh: 优势在于高度机动的牧战结合经济、卓越的战车与马匹技术、强大的武士文化、灵活的部落联盟形式以及对金属与军事创新的快速扩散能力。
