strengths.en: Strengths included a highly mobile pastoral-warrior economy, exceptional chariot and horse technology, a powerful martial culture, flexible tribal coalition structures, and the rapid diffusion of metallurgical and military innovations.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一国家机构和稳定税基，政治凝聚力受亲族关系限制，人口分散，易受气候波动、牧场退化和部落内斗影响。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included the absence of unified state institutions and a stable tax base, political cohesion limited by kinship ties, dispersed population, and vulnerability to climate fluctuation, pasture degradation, and intertribal conflict.

outlook.zh: 到公元前1600年前后，该文化圈正处于兴盛与扩张阶段，其战车、马匹和印伊语相关传统极可能继续向中亚、伊朗高原与南亚传播，对后续欧亚大陆文明格局产生深远影响。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, the sphere was in a phase of vigor and expansion; its chariotry, horse practices, and Indo-Iranian-associated traditions were likely to continue spreading into Central Asia, the Iranian plateau, and South Asia, exerting lasting influence on the later Eurasian civilizational landscape.

description.zh: 欧亚草原中部的牧战部族广泛传播战车、马匹与金属技术。
description.en: Pastoral-warrior tribes of the central Eurasian steppe were spreading chariotry, horse use, and metallurgical technologies.

name.zh: 巴克特里亚—马尔吉亚纳绿洲诸邦
巴克特里亚—马尔吉亚纳文明

governmentForm.zh: 以绿洲城邦为基础的寡头君主制，各城由地方贵族家族统治，宗教与宫廷权力紧密结合
governmentForm.en: An oasis city-state oligarchic monarchy, with each center ruled by local noble families and with religious and palatial authority closely intertwined

socialStructure.zh: 社会由领主家族、祭司集团、武装侍从、工匠、商人、农民与牧民构成，并吸纳周边流动部落人口
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of lordly families, priestly groups, armed retainers, artisans, merchants, farmers, and pastoralists, while also absorbing mobile tribal populations from surrounding regions

rulingClass.zh: 各绿洲要塞与礼仪中心中的地方贵族、祭司首领及其家族网络
rulingClass.en: Local nobles, priestly leaders, and their family networks based in oasis fortresses and ceremonial centers

succession.zh: 以家族世袭为主，但常受宗族协商、婚姻联盟与地方武装支持影响
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within ruling families, but often shaped by clan negotiation, marriage alliances, and support from local armed retainers

structure.zh: 文明并无统一帝国官僚体系，而是由若干绿洲中心组成的松散网络；各城拥有宫殿、仓储、宗教建筑与防御工事，政治权力围绕领主府邸和祭祀机构运作
structure.en: The civilization lacked a unified imperial bureaucracy and instead formed a loose network of oasis centers; each city had palatial compounds, storage facilities, religious buildings, and fortifications, with power operating around the lordly residence and cult institutions

function.zh: 管理粮食、油料、纺织品和贵重物资的征收、储存与再分配
function.en: Managed the collection, storage, and redistribution of grain, oils, textiles, and valuable goods

function.zh: 监督渠道、分水、田地维护与农时组织
function.en: Oversaw canals, water allocation, field maintenance, and seasonal agricultural coordination

function.zh: 主持礼仪、管理圣火与供品，并维护宗教建筑群
function.en: Conducted rituals, managed sacred fires and offerings, and maintained religious complexes

function.zh: 组织金属、石器、陶器、印章和纺织品生产
function.en: Organized production of metal goods, stone tools, pottery, seals, and textiles

function.zh: 监管商队往来、交换品登记和通道安全
function.en: Supervised caravan movement, registration of exchange goods, and route security

function.zh: 负责城墙、城门、守卫队与应急动员
function.en: Handled walls, gates, guard units, and emergency mobilization

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以单个绿洲聚落及其周边耕地为基本单位，由领主亲族、祭司、仓储管理员和村社首领共同管理
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on each oasis settlement and its surrounding fields, jointly managed by the lord’s kin, priests, storage administrators, and village headmen

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、宗教禁忌和领主裁断为主，重视财产、灌溉分配、婚姻关系和商旅纠纷的调解
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on custom, religious taboo, and the lord’s judgment, with emphasis on property, irrigation allocation, marriage relations, and mediation of trade disputes

taxationSystem.zh: 税收多以实物和劳役形式征收，包括谷物、牲畜、纺织品、手工业成品以及渠道维护和筑城徭役
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was levied mostly in kind and labor, including grain, livestock, textiles, craft goods, and corvée for canal maintenance and construction

religion.zh: 以火祭、水源崇拜、祖先与地方神灵信仰为核心，礼仪活动集中于大型圣所、祭坛和仪式庭院
religion.en: Religion centered on fire rituals, water-source veneration, and the worship of ancestors and local deities, with ceremonial life focused on major sanctuaries, altars, and ritual courtyards

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学传统，世界观主要体现为礼仪秩序、天地循环、洁净观念与王权—神权结合的宇宙观
philosophy.en: No fully systematic philosophical tradition had emerged; the worldview was expressed mainly through ritual order, cyclical cosmology, ideas of purity, and a sacred linkage between authority and the divine

writingSystem.zh: 未见成熟本地文字系统，行政与交换多依靠印章、标记、器物符号和口述记忆
writingSystem.en: There is no evidence for a mature native writing system; administration and exchange relied largely on seals, marks, object symbols, and oral memory

culturalAchievements.zh: 代表成就包括规整化城址、复杂防御工事、礼仪建筑、精美印章、金属工艺、石器与骨器制作，以及将绿洲农业与长距离商路结合的社会组织能力
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included planned settlements, complex fortifications, ceremonial architecture, finely made seals, metalworking, stone and bone craftsmanship, and the social organization needed to combine oasis agriculture with long-distance exchange routes

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，可能包含多种绿洲定居群体语言，并与印度—伊朗语前期接触圈相互影响
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain, likely involving multiple oasis sedentary languages influenced by contact with early Indo-Iranian speech communities

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉农业、畜牧业、手工业与商队中转为基础。至前1600年总体仍具区域重要性，但许多核心城市已较早期鼎盛阶段缩小，产出与贸易活力下滑
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on irrigated agriculture, pastoralism, craft production, and caravan transit. By 1600 BCE it still retained regional importance, but many core cities had contracted from their earlier peak and both output and trade dynamism were declining

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、牧羊与养牛、青铜与铜器制作、陶器、珠饰加工、纺织、印章制作和商队贸易服务
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated farming, sheep and cattle herding, bronze and copper working, pottery, bead production, textiles, seal carving, and caravan trade services

tradeGoods.zh: 输出或转运谷物、羊毛纺织品、牲畜、青铜器、半宝石、绿松石、青金石转口品与手工器物；输入锡、铜、木材、奢侈品和部分远距原料
tradeGoods.en: It exported or transshipped grain, wool textiles, livestock, bronze goods, semi-precious stones, turquoise, lapis lazuli transit goods, and crafted objects; it imported tin, copper, timber, luxury items, and selected long-distance raw materials

unitName.zh: 舍客勒式重量单位与谷物计量
unitName.en: Shekel-like weight units and grain measures

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为房舍、粮食储备、牲畜、工具和纺织品；贵族家庭则控制仓储、祭祀资源、武装侍从和奢侈品网络
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted chiefly of dwellings, grain reserves, livestock, tools, and textiles; elite families controlled storage, cult resources, armed retainers, and networks of prestige goods

economicSystem.zh: 以宫殿—圣所再分配经济与家庭生产并存，辅以绿洲之间和跨区域商路上的礼物交换与商队贸易
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining palace-sanctuary redistribution with household production, supplemented by gift exchange and caravan trade between oases and across wider regions

treasuryDescription.zh: 财富储备主要并非金属钱币，而是仓储粮食、牲畜、纺织物、器物和少量贵金属及宝石。由于衰落与区域重组，财政缓冲能力正在减弱
treasuryDescription.en: Stored wealth consisted primarily not of coined money but of grain reserves, livestock, textiles, manufactured goods, and small amounts of precious metal and gemstones. Because of decline and regional reorganization, fiscal resilience was weakening

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持灌溉系统、守城与宗教中心，同时通过再分配稳定附属劳动力；在衰退期更依赖实物征收而非扩张性建设
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining irrigation systems, defensive centers, and cult institutions while using redistribution to stabilize dependent labor; in this period of decline it relied more on in-kind collection than on expansionary building

description.zh: 驻守城墙、城门和宫殿区的步兵，以矛、短斧、弓和盾为主要装备
description.en: Infantry stationed on walls, gates, and palace precincts, equipped mainly with spears, short axes, bows, and shields

description.zh: 由贵族家族直接供养的精锐护卫，用于内部控制、护送商队和危机时快速集结
description.en: Elite guards maintained directly by noble families, used for internal control, caravan escort, and rapid concentration in crisis

description.zh: 来自周边耕作与牧放社区的临时征召力量，负责外围巡逻、运补和战时增援
description.en: Temporarily mobilized men from surrounding farming and herding communities, responsible for outer patrols, logistics, and wartime reinforcement

description.zh: 数量有限，主要用于联络、侦察和追击，反映与草原及伊朗高原接触后的军事适应
description.en: Limited in number and used mainly for communication, scouting, and pursuit, reflecting military adaptation through contact with steppe and Iranian plateau groups

commanderInChief.zh: 各绿洲领主兼任最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: Each oasis lord served as his own commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 使用青铜矛头、斧、匕首、弓箭、泥砖与夯土防御工事，并具备有限车乘能力；军事实力偏向守备而非远征
technology.en: Military technology included bronze spearheads, axes, daggers, bows, mudbrick and rammed-earth fortifications, and limited chariot capability; military strength was oriented toward defense rather than conquest

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自绿洲间竞争、商路衰退、草原与边缘牧民压力，以及来自南方和东方新兴印欧—伊朗语流动群体的渗透
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry among oases, declining trade routes, pressure from steppe and peripheral pastoral groups, and infiltration by emerging Indo-Iranian mobile populations from the south and east

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在穆尔加布河三角洲、阿姆河相关绿洲及若干灌溉定居点。与早期高峰相比，部分中心人口已经下降并向较小聚落分散
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly in the Murghab delta, oases linked to the Oxus system, and various irrigated settlements. Compared with the earlier peak, some major centers had already lost population and dispersed into smaller communities

socialClasses.zh: 上层为领主家族、祭司与武装侍从；中层为工匠、商旅组织者与富裕农牧户；下层为普通农民、牧民、劳役人口及依附社群
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included lordly families, priests, and armed retainers; the middle strata included artisans, caravan organizers, and prosperous agro-pastoral households; lower strata included ordinary farmers, herders, laborers, and dependent communities

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟友，更多依赖与伊朗高原早期高地文化、后城市化印度河地区和部分商路社群的临时合作
allies.en: There were no stable unified allies; the civilization depended more on temporary cooperation with early highland cultures of the Iranian plateau, post-urban Indus regions, and selected trade-route communities

enemies.zh: 并无单一宿敌，但与周边草原牧团、竞争性绿洲中心及新进入的流动战士集团关系紧张
enemies.en: It had no single archenemy, but relations were tense with neighboring steppe pastoral groups, rival oasis centers, and newly arriving mobile warrior groups

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持商道、交换婚姻、宗教威望传播和边缘缓冲为主，倾向防御和吸纳而非大规模扩张
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining trade corridors, arranging marriage exchange, extending ritual prestige, and preserving frontier buffers, favoring defense and absorption over large-scale expansion

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括绿洲灌溉系统、规范化泥砖建筑、防御性城墙、大型仓储设施、青铜铸造、石珠和印章工艺，以及复杂礼仪建筑布局
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included oasis irrigation systems, standardized mudbrick architecture, defensive walls, large storage facilities, bronze casting, stone bead and seal craftsmanship, and complex ceremonial architectural planning

strengths.zh: 优势在于成熟的绿洲农业、坚固设防、精细手工业、跨区域商路节点地位以及强烈的礼仪整合能力
strengths.en: Its strengths were mature oasis agriculture, strong fortifications, refined craft production, a nodal position on long-distance exchange routes, and a strong capacity for ritual integration

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分散、缺乏统一文字官僚、对灌溉与商路高度依赖，以及在外来流动人口压力下的脆弱性
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included political fragmentation, the absence of a unified literate bureaucracy, heavy dependence on irrigation and trade routes, and vulnerability under pressure from incoming mobile populations

outlook.zh: 到前1600年前后，该文明整体已进入衰退与重组阶段。其核心城市网络将继续缩小，但许多宗教观念、工艺传统和聚落模式可能会被后续的伊朗高原与中亚人群吸收并延续
outlook.en: By around 1600 BCE the civilization had clearly entered a phase of decline and reorganization. Its core urban network would continue to contract, but many of its religious ideas, craft traditions, and settlement patterns were likely to be absorbed and carried on by later populations of the Iranian plateau and Central Asia

description.zh: 中亚绿洲城市文明仍有余续，但整体正由早期高峰走向衰落重组。
description.en: The oasis urban civilization of Central Asia still endured but was moving from its earlier peak into decline and reorganization.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个世袭酋邦组成的部落贵族统治体系，权力分散于莱茵河、多瑙河与阿尔卑斯周边诸首领家族
governmentForm.en: A tribal aristocratic system of multiple hereditary chiefdoms, with power dispersed among leading families across the Rhine, Danube, and Alpine zones

socialStructure.zh: 社会以贵族武士、随从战士、自由农牧民、工匠与附属人口构成；墓冢精英通过宴饮、馈赠和青铜器控制地区网络
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of noble warriors, retainer fighters, free agro-pastoral households, craft specialists, and dependent populations; tumulus elites controlled regional networks through feasting, gift exchange, and bronze prestige goods

rulingClass.zh: 掌握土地、武装与金属贸易通道的墓冢贵族和酋长家族
rulingClass.en: Tumulus nobles and chieftainly families controlling land, armed followings, and routes of metal exchange

succession.zh: 通常为父系世袭，但需获得宗族长者、武士随从与姻亲联盟承认；在部分地区可由同族支系竞争继承
succession.en: Usually patrilineal hereditary succession, but legitimacy required recognition from clan elders, warrior retainers, and marriage allies; in some regions succession could be contested among collateral branches

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家官僚制，而是由地区酋长、宗族长者、祭祀人物和战士首领共同治理。重要事务通过酋长居所、山堡与季节性集会决定。
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; governance was shared by regional chiefs, clan elders, ritual specialists, and war leaders. Major matters were decided from chiefly residences, hillforts, and seasonal assemblies.

function.zh: 管理粮食储备、宴饮分配、工匠供养、贡礼收藏与首领庄园劳役
function.en: Managed food stores, feast distribution, support for artisans, prestige-gift holdings, and labor obligations on chiefly estates

function.zh: 召集武装随从、护卫贸易通道、组织袭击与防御、维持首领威望
function.en: Mobilized armed retainers, guarded trade routes, organized raids and defense, and maintained chiefly prestige

function.zh: 主持葬仪、祭献、誓约仪式与季节性宗教活动，维护酋长合法性
function.en: Oversaw burials, offerings, oath rituals, and seasonal religious observances, sustaining chiefly legitimacy

function.zh: 协调铜、锡、金、盐和成品青铜器的采集、运输、再分配与长途交换
function.en: Coordinated the procurement, transport, redistribution, and long-distance exchange of copper, tin, gold, salt, and finished bronze goods

function.zh: 联络村落、安排耕地与放牧地使用、组织公共防御工事与季节迁徙
function.en: Linked villages, arranged use of arable and pasture land, and organized communal defenses and seasonal movements

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托亲族网络与聚落首领。大型村落、山堡和墓冢中心各有其从属村社，酋长通过馈赠、婚姻与武力维系支配关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on kinship networks and settlement leaders. Large villages, hillforts, and tumulus centers each had dependent communities, while chiefs maintained authority through gift exchange, marriage ties, and force.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，以血亲赔偿、誓言、仲裁和报复平衡为核心。财产、婚姻、牲畜、伤害与杀人纠纷通常由长者和酋长裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, centered on kin compensation, oath-taking, arbitration, and balances of retaliation. Disputes over property, marriage, livestock, injury, and homicide were usually judged by elders and chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，酋长主要通过贡礼、劳役、牲畜、谷物、金属原料和宴饮义务获取资源；战时还可征发武装与运输服务。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; chiefs drew resources mainly through tribute, labor dues, livestock, grain, metal raw materials, and feast obligations; in wartime they could also levy armed service and transport.

religion.zh: 宗教以祖灵崇拜、自然神圣性、太阳与天象象征、武器献祭和墓葬礼仪为中心。河流、沼泽、山地与古树可能被视为神圣地点。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, sacred nature, solar and celestial symbolism, weapon offerings, and funerary ritual. Rivers, wetlands, mountains, and ancient trees were likely regarded as sacred places.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学文本，社会观念强调血缘忠诚、勇武声望、馈赠互惠、祖先延续与首领慷慨。
philosophy.en: There was no formal textual philosophy, but social values emphasized kin loyalty, martial prestige, reciprocal gift exchange, ancestral continuity, and chiefly generosity.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土书写系统，信息主要通过口述传统、记忆谱系、象征性器物与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no native writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, memorized genealogies, symbolic objects, and ritual practice.

culturalAchievements.zh: 代表性成就包括墓冢葬制、精制青铜武器与饰物、金片与琥珀装饰、木构建筑、车辆技术扩散以及跨区域礼物交换网络。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included tumulus burial traditions, finely made bronze weapons and ornaments, gold foil and amber decoration, timber architecture, the spread of wheeled transport, and supra-regional prestige-gift exchange networks.

languageFamily.zh: 可能以早期印欧语诸方言为主，具体分化尚难确定，并夹杂地方前印欧基底成分
languageFamily.en: Likely dominated by early Indo-European dialects, though precise divisions remain uncertain, with local pre-Indo-European substrate elements also present

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、牧业、采矿、青铜冶铸和礼物交换为基础。其生产剩余有限，但金属制品、盐、琥珀和牲畜提高了地区精英财富集中度。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on farming, herding, mining, bronze metallurgy, and gift exchange. Surplus production was limited, but metal goods, salt, amber, and livestock increased the concentration of wealth among regional elites.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牛羊猪饲养、铜矿与盐矿开采、青铜铸造、木工、陶器、纺织、琥珀与金饰加工
mainIndustries.en: Cereal cultivation, cattle-sheep-pig husbandry, copper and salt extraction, bronze casting, woodworking, pottery, textiles, and amber and gold ornament production

tradeGoods.zh: 铜、锡、青铜剑斧饰物、盐、琥珀、黄金、皮革、毛织物、牲畜、木材与陶器
tradeGoods.en: Copper, tin, bronze swords, axes and ornaments, salt, amber, gold, hides, wool textiles, livestock, timber, and pottery

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富体现为土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓储备、木屋、工具和亲族关系；贵族则集中拥有武器、马具、金属器、宴饮器具和进口珍品。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth consisted of land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, houses, tools, and kin ties; noble wealth was concentrated in weapons, horse gear, metal objects, feasting equipment, and imported prestige items.

economicSystem.zh: 经济属于以家庭生产为基础、由酋长再分配和礼物交换强化的部落青铜时代混合体系；远距离贸易多服务于精英地位竞争。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a tribal Bronze Age mixed system based on household production and reinforced by chiefly redistribution and gift exchange; long-distance trade served elite status competition to a large extent.

source.en: Levies on metal and salt production

source.zh: 贸易过路礼与馈赠收入
source.en: Transit dues and gift income from trade

category.en: Feasting, gift exchange, and political alliances

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中于单一首都，而是分散存放于酋长仓储、牲畜群、青铜锭、盐块、武器库和贵重饰物中。财富多为实物，并依靠人际关系维持。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not concentrated in a single capital, but dispersed across chiefly storehouses, herds, bronze ingots, salt blocks, weapon stocks, and prestige ornaments. Wealth was mostly held in kind and maintained through personal relations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在积累可重新分配的实物资源，以支持战士、宴饮和联盟维系；稳定时期偏向保守储备，危机时则扩大征发。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on accumulating redistributable goods in kind to support warriors, feasts, and alliances; in stable times it favored cautious storage, while crises led to broader levies.

description.zh: 由酋长直属战士、贵族青年和长期随从组成，装备较好的青铜剑、斧、矛与盾牌，是核心机动力量。
description.en: Composed of directly attached warriors, noble youths, and long-term retainers, equipped with relatively better bronze swords, axes, spears, and shields; they formed the core mobile force.

description.zh: 由自由农牧民在战时征召，主要使用长矛、木盾、石制或青铜工具改装武器，适合防御、伏击与短期集结。
description.en: Raised from free agro-pastoral households in wartime, they mainly used spears, wooden shields, and adapted stone or bronze tools as weapons, suitable for defense, ambush, and short-term muster.

description.zh: 数量有限，主要包括骑乘信使、侦察者、驾车贵族与运输队；战车在中欧更偏向威望和机动指挥用途，而非大规模冲击兵种。
description.en: Limited in number, these included mounted messengers, scouts, chariot nobles, and transport teams; in Central Europe chariots were used more for prestige and mobile command than as mass shock troops.

commanderInChief.zh: 联盟酋长或最强地区首领
commanderInChief.en: Alliance chieftain or the strongest regional chief

technology.zh: 军事技术处于中期青铜时代水平，使用青铜剑、矛头、斧、匕首、木盾、皮甲和少量马车；防御依托山堡、木栅和壕沟。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a Middle Bronze Age level, using bronze swords, spearheads, axes, daggers, wooden shields, leather armor, and limited horse-drawn vehicles; defense relied on hillforts, palisades, and ditches.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦竞争、贸易路线争夺、牲畜掠夺、内部继承冲突以及阿尔卑斯与多瑙走廊上的区域性战争。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival neighboring chiefdoms, struggles over trade routes, cattle raiding, internal succession conflicts, and regional warfare along the Alpine and Danube corridors.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住于河谷村落、湖岸聚落、丘陵农牧社区和设防高地，整体密度不高，但沿莱茵、多瑙及阿尔卑斯通道较为集中。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across river-valley villages, lakeside settlements, upland agro-pastoral communities, and fortified heights; overall density was low, but it was more concentrated along the Rhine, Danube, and Alpine corridors.

name.zh: 乌内蒂采地区大聚落群
阿尔卑斯北麓盐矿聚落
波希米亚墓冢中心聚落

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括墓冢贵族、酋长亲族、武士随从、自由农牧民、专业工匠、贸易中介者以及附属劳役人口。地位差异主要体现于墓葬规模、武器、马具和贵重金属拥有量。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included tumulus nobles, chiefly kin, warrior retainers, free agro-pastoralists, specialist artisans, trade intermediaries, and dependent laboring groups. Status differences were expressed chiefly in burial scale, weapons, horse gear, and possession of precious metals.

allies.zh: 无固定国家级盟友，主要通过婚姻联盟、礼物交换和临时军事协定与意大利北部、伊比利亚、英国及北欧青铜文化圈保持关系。
allies.en: There were no fixed state-level allies; relations were maintained mainly through marriage alliances, gift exchange, and temporary military agreements with northern Italy, Iberia, Britain, and the Nordic Bronze cultural sphere.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象通常不是遥远文明，而是相邻竞争酋邦、争夺矿源与通道的部落集团，以及可能从东部草原边缘渗入的武装群体。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually not distant civilizations but neighboring rival chiefdoms, tribal groups competing for mines and transit corridors, and possibly armed groups filtering in from the eastern steppe margins.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制交换网络、确保铜锡来源、维持婚姻关系和避免长期消耗战为主；外交高度人格化，依赖酋长信誉与馈赠能力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling exchange networks, securing copper and tin sources, maintaining marriage ties, and avoiding prolonged attritional warfare; diplomacy was highly personalized and depended on chiefly reputation and generosity.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括高质量青铜合金铸造、双片模和失蜡法的局部应用、轮式运输、马具改进、木栅山堡工程、盐矿开采与长距离金属交换组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included high-quality bronze alloy casting, local use of two-piece molds and lost-wax methods, wheeled transport, improved horse gear, palisaded hillfort engineering, salt extraction, and organized long-distance metal exchange.

strengths.zh: 其优势在于金属资源与工艺、跨区域贸易通道、灵活的酋邦联盟、强烈的武士文化以及能通过宴饮与馈赠迅速整合追随者。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in metal resources and craftsmanship, cross-regional trade corridors, flexible chiefdom alliances, a strong warrior culture, and the ability to rapidly integrate followers through feasting and gift exchange.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点包括政治高度分裂、缺乏书写与统一行政、对精英声望经济依赖过重、粮食生产波动大，以及继承争端容易引发局部崩解。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included extreme political fragmentation, lack of writing and unified administration, heavy dependence on elite prestige economics, volatile food production, and succession disputes that could easily trigger local breakdowns.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后总体稳定，若金属与盐贸易持续、主要贵族家族维持联盟，此文明将继续发展为更复杂的区域青铜文化；但若交换网络中断或精英竞争升级，则可能迅速分裂重组。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE it was broadly stable; if metal and salt trade continued and major noble families preserved alliances, this civilization would likely develop into more complex regional Bronze Age cultures. Yet if exchange networks were disrupted or elite competition intensified, it could fragment and recombine rapidly.

description.zh: 莱茵、多瑙与阿尔卑斯周边存在多个以冢墓贵族和金属制造为核心的酋邦。
description.en: The Rhine, Danube, and Alpine zones hosted multiple chiefdoms centered on tumulus elites and metal production.

governmentForm.zh: 以王室家族和地区总督为基础的青铜时代世袭君主制，兼具低地都城与高地地方势力的复合统治结构。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Bronze Age monarchy based on the royal family and regional governors, with a composite structure balancing the lowland capital and highland local powers.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为王室与贵族、神庙祭司、军事与行政人员、城市工匠与商人、乡村农民、牧民以及奴婢与战俘等层次；低地农业城镇与高地部族社群并存。
socialStructure.en: Society was divided among the royal house and nobility, temple priests, military and administrative personnel, urban craftsmen and merchants, rural farmers, pastoralists, and slaves or war captives; lowland agricultural towns coexisted with highland tribal communities.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由苏萨王室、与王室联姻的贵族家族、重要神庙祭司以及掌控地方征税和征兵的地区长官构成。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal house of Susa, noble families tied by marriage to the dynasty, major temple priests, and regional officials who controlled taxation and recruitment.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室内部世袭，但苏卡尔马赫时期存在复杂的家族共治与旁系继承传统，王弟、外甥或其他近亲可能继位。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal house, but the Sukkalmah period featured complex family co-rule and collateral succession, allowing brothers, nephews, or other close kin to inherit.

structure.zh: 中央政权以苏萨宫廷为核心，王室通过大摄政、地方总督、神庙管理者和书记官网络治理苏西亚那平原与周边高地地区；部分边远地带保留较强地方自治。
structure.en: The central state was centered on the court at Susa, where the royal house governed Susiana and surrounding highlands through the Grand Regent, provincial governors, temple administrators, and networks of scribes; some peripheral zones retained strong local autonomy.

function.zh: 负责土地、贡赋、劳役、外交文书、司法记录以及楔形文字行政档案的保存。
function.en: Handled land, tribute, labor, diplomatic correspondence, judicial records, and the preservation of cuneiform administrative archives.

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金属与手工业贡品，并管理宫廷与神庙仓储和再分配。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and craft tribute, and managed palace and temple storage and redistribution.

function.zh: 协调主要神庙祭祀、祭司供给、圣地财产和宗教节庆，尤其是苏萨与安善地区的重要神庙。
function.en: Coordinated major temple rituals, priestly provisions, sacred estates, and religious festivals, especially in Susa and Anshan-related cult centers.

function.zh: 监督地方长官、高地附属首领、道路节点和边境治安，处理低地与高地间的政治协调。
function.en: Supervised provincial officials, highland subordinate chiefs, road stations, and frontier security, managing coordination between lowlands and highlands.

function.zh: 负责征兵、武器储备、战车牲畜调配、守军补给与战时动员。
function.en: Managed troop levies, weapon reserves, chariot animals, garrison supply, and wartime mobilization.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依靠苏萨周边核心官僚、城镇长官、神庙地产管理者以及受王廷承认的高地部族首领。王权在核心平原地带较强，在扎格罗斯边缘地区则更多通过盟约、婚姻和贡赋关系维持。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on core bureaucrats around Susa, town officials, temple estate managers, and highland tribal chiefs recognized by the court. Royal authority was strongest in the central lowlands, while Zagros fringe areas were governed more through alliances, marriage, and tribute ties.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王室裁断、地方习惯法、契约文书和神庙仲裁为基础，受美索不达米亚书写法律文化影响，但在婚姻、财产和家族继承上保留明显埃兰特色。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on royal judgment, local customary law, contractual documents, and temple arbitration, influenced by Mesopotamian written legal culture but retaining distinct Elamite features in marriage, property, and family inheritance.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自谷物、牲畜、金属、纺织品与劳役形式的贡赋，而非统一货币税；附属地区和高地首领常以季节性进贡和军役义务履行臣属关系。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from tribute in grain, livestock, metals, textiles, and labor service rather than a uniform monetary tax; subordinate districts and highland chiefs often fulfilled dependence through seasonal deliveries and military obligations.

religion.zh: 埃兰宗教为多神信仰，崇拜因舒什纳克、纳皮里沙、基里里沙等神祇。王权与神庙密切结合，祭祀、供献、神谕与祖先纪念共同维系国家合法性。
religion.en: Elamite religion was polytheistic, venerating deities such as Inshushinak, Napirisha, and Kiririsha. Kingship was closely tied to temples, and ritual, offering, divination, and ancestral commemoration together upheld state legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 尚无后世意义上的系统哲学，思想核心在于神意、王权正当性、家族秩序、契约义务与对圣地和祖先的敬重。
philosophy.en: There was no systematic philosophy in the later sense; core thought centered on divine will, royal legitimacy, family order, contractual obligation, and reverence for sacred places and ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与外交主要使用阿卡德语楔形文字，同时存在用于本地语言记录的埃兰语书写传统；苏萨是文书活动的重要中心。
writingSystem.en: Administration and diplomacy primarily used Akkadian cuneiform, alongside Elamite writing traditions for local language use; Susa was a major center of scribal activity.

culturalAchievements.zh: 埃兰在王室印章、金属器、神庙建筑、彩釉与石质器物、跨高地与低地的艺术融合方面成就显著，并形成兼具伊朗高地与美索不达米亚特征的宫廷文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Elam achieved notable distinction in royal seals, metalwork, temple architecture, glazed and stone objects, and artistic fusion across highland and lowland traditions, producing a court culture combining Iranian plateau and Mesopotamian features.

languageFamily.zh: 埃兰语，通常被视为孤立语言；外交和部分行政领域广泛接触闪米特语阿卡德语。
languageFamily.en: Elamite, generally regarded as a language isolate; Semitic Akkadian was also widely encountered in diplomacy and some administrative contexts.

unit.en: kg barley equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 埃兰经济属中等规模的青铜时代宫廷—神庙—乡村复合经济。苏西亚那平原的灌溉农业提供基础，高地牧业与金属资源补充财富，对外贸易使其在两河与伊朗高地之间占据重要中介地位。
gdpDescription.en: The Elamite economy was a medium-scale Bronze Age palace-temple-rural composite economy. Irrigated agriculture in Susiana provided the base, while highland pastoralism and metal resources added wealth; foreign trade gave Elam an important intermediary role between Mesopotamia and the Iranian plateau.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、畜牧、铜与青铜加工、陶器、纺织、木工、石器与奢侈工艺品制造、转运贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, herding, copper and bronze working, pottery, textiles, carpentry, stone and luxury crafts, and transit trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口或转运谷物、羊毛纺织品、牲畜、沥青、陶器、金属制品与高地物产；输入锡、优质木材、半宝石、部分奢侈品以及来自海湾与高地的稀有原料。
tradeGoods.en: Exports or re-exports included grain, wool textiles, livestock, bitumen, pottery, metal goods, and highland products; imports included tin, quality timber, semi-precious stones, some luxury goods, and rare raw materials from the Gulf and uplands.

name.zh: 称量银与实物贡赋体系
name.en: Weighed silver and commodity tribute system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、农具和家族劳动力；城市富户则持有仓储、金属器、纺织品与贸易关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed through land-use rights, livestock, grain reserves, tools, and family labor; urban wealthier households also held storage stocks, metal goods, textiles, and trade connections.

unit.en: kg barley equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济为宫廷、神庙与家户并存的再分配体系，辅以地方市场交换和跨区域商旅网络；财富动员高度依赖贡赋、劳役和仓储管理。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a redistributive system combining palace, temple, and household sectors, supplemented by local market exchange and interregional merchant networks; wealth mobilization relied heavily on tribute, labor obligations, and storage administration.

source.zh: 王室地产与手工业作坊

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由谷仓储备、牲畜群、青铜与铜库存、称量银以及神庙与王室库房中的贵重器物共同构成。其形态以实物储备为主，而非统一铸币现金。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of grain reserves, herd stocks, bronze and copper inventories, weighed silver, and valuable objects held in royal and temple storehouses. It was primarily material stock rather than a unified coin cash reserve.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，以稳定征收、扩大仓储冗余、维护军备和通过馈赠稳固附属关系为主；在歉收或战争时可提高劳役和临时征发。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing steady collection, maintaining storage surpluses, supporting arms, and stabilizing subordinate ties through gifts; labor dues and emergency levies could be increased in poor harvests or war.

description.zh: 由矛兵、持盾兵和轻装投射兵组成，是守城、列阵和远征的核心力量。
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, and light missile troops, serving as the core force for garrisoning, line combat, and expeditions.

description.zh: 来自扎格罗斯边缘与附属部族，适合山地行军、袭扰和边境作战，但纪律与装备不如核心军队统一。
description.en: Raised from Zagros fringe areas and subordinate tribes, suited for mountain movement, raiding, and frontier warfare, though less uniform in discipline and equipment than the core army.

description.zh: 数量不大但地位显赫，用于王室护卫、冲击和指挥中枢，反映青铜时代精英战争形态。
description.en: Small in number but prestigious, used for royal protection, shock action, and command functions, reflecting elite Bronze Age warfare.

description.zh: 驻守苏萨、主要仓储点、渡口和边境通道，兼负治安与护送任务。
description.en: Stationed at Susa, major storage points, river crossings, and frontier routes, also handling policing and escort duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 大摄政兼国王
commanderInChief.en: Grand Regent and King

technology.zh: 军队使用青铜矛、斧、短剑、弓箭、皮革与木质盾牌、简易头盔以及驴拉或早期马拉战车；攻城能力有限，仍以掠袭、围困和地区机动为主。
technology.en: The military used bronze spears, axes, short swords, bows, leather and wooden shields, simple helmets, and donkey-drawn or early horse-drawn chariots; siege capabilities were limited, with warfare focused on raiding, blockade, and regional mobility.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自巴比伦及两河东缘势力竞争、北方与西北方向的胡里安—米坦尼扩张影响、扎格罗斯山地部族的离心倾向，以及与海湾和高地贸易路线有关的冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry with Babylon and powers on Mesopotamia’s eastern edge, the influence of Hurrian-Mitanni expansion to the north and northwest, centrifugal tendencies among Zagros highland groups, and conflicts tied to Gulf and upland trade routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在苏西亚那平原和苏萨周边灌溉农业区，其余分布于高地山麓聚落、牧业地带和若干区域中心。人口规模足以支撑稳定王国，但尚不足形成类似埃及或巴比伦的超大官僚国家。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the irrigated agricultural zone of Susiana and around Susa, with the rest spread across highland foothill settlements, pastoral zones, and a few regional centers. This scale was sufficient for a stable kingdom, though not enough to sustain a super-sized bureaucratic state like Egypt or Babylon.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族位居顶层，其次为祭司、书记官、军官和地方长官；中层包括商人、工匠和自由农；底层为佃作者、劳役人群、牧民从属者及奴婢。高地部族社会在这一框架外保留较多宗族结构。
socialClasses.en: The royal house and nobility stood at the top, followed by priests, scribes, officers, and provincial administrators; middle groups included merchants, craftsmen, and free farmers; lower groups consisted of tenant cultivators, laborers, dependent pastoralists, and slaves. Highland tribal society retained stronger clan structures outside this framework.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的长期正式盟友，但可与部分伊朗高地首领、海湾贸易伙伴及反巴比伦势力形成暂时合作关系。
allies.en: There were no stable long-term formal allies in the modern sense, but temporary cooperation could be formed with some Iranian highland chiefs, Gulf trade partners, and anti-Babylonian forces.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是巴比伦及其在两河东部的利益网络；同时需警惕北方亚述旧势力残余、米坦尼影响和边地山民叛离。
enemies.en: The principal rival was Babylon and its interest network in eastern Mesopotamia; Elam also had to watch residual Old Assyrian influence, Mitanni expansion, and defections among frontier highlanders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制两河东向通道、维持高地附属关系、争夺边境城市影响力并保障海湾与内陆贸易路线为核心，兼用婚姻、贡赋、武力与外交馈赠。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling eastward routes from Mesopotamia, maintaining highland dependencies, contesting influence over frontier cities, and securing Gulf and inland trade lines, using marriage, tribute, force, and diplomatic gifts.

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的灌溉农业、楔形文字行政、青铜武器与工具、印章与档案制度、仓储再分配体系、早期战车运用以及跨区域贸易组织能力。
keyInnovations.en: Mature irrigation agriculture, cuneiform administration, bronze weapons and tools, seal and archive systems, warehouse redistribution, early chariot use, and the organizational capacity for interregional trade.

strengths.zh: 埃兰的优势在于苏萨的战略位置、连接两河与伊朗高地的中介贸易地位、兼具农业与高地资源的复合经济，以及灵活吸纳地方首领的统治方式。
strengths.en: Elam’s strengths lay in Susa’s strategic position, its intermediary trade role linking Mesopotamia and the Iranian plateau, a composite economy combining agriculture with highland resources, and a flexible mode of rule that incorporated local chiefs.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是政治结构较为松散、地方和高地势力不易完全整合、对宫廷与神庙再分配体系依赖较深，且面对巴比伦等低地强国时资源总量处于相对劣势。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a relatively loose political structure, difficulty in fully integrating provincial and highland powers, heavy dependence on palace-temple redistribution, and a comparative resource disadvantage against stronger lowland states such as Babylon.

outlook.zh: 公元前1600年前后的埃兰总体稳定，若能维持苏萨对低地农业区和高地通道的控制，仍可在地区政治中保持重要地位；但一旦王室继承失序或巴比伦、北方强权加压，其统一性将迅速受到挑战。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE Elam was broadly stable, and if Susa could retain control over the lowland agricultural zone and upland routes, it would remain an important regional power; however, any disorder in succession or pressure from Babylon and northern powers could quickly undermine its cohesion.

description.zh: 伊朗西南部古国，长期与美索不达米亚诸强互动并竞争。
description.en: An ancient kingdom in southwestern Iran interacting and competing with Mesopotamian powers.

name.zh: 伊朗高原早期高地文化
name.en: Early Highland Cultures of the Iranian Plateau

governmentForm.zh: 松散的部族联盟与山地小酋邦并存，权力分散于氏族首领、长老会议与战时盟主之间。
governmentForm.en: A loose landscape of tribal confederations and small highland chiefdoms, with authority dispersed among clan leaders, councils of elders, and temporary war leaders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与村社为核心，上层为酋长家族、武士随从与祭司，中层为自由牧民、农民与工匠，下层包括依附民、债役者与战俘。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, extended families, and village communities. The upper strata consisted of chiefly lineages, warrior retainers, and ritual specialists; the middle strata of free herders, farmers, and artisans; and the lower strata included dependents, debt servants, and war captives.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、氏族长老、战士贵族与掌管祭祀的宗教首领共同构成统治层。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was formed by local chiefly families, clan elders, warrior nobles, and religious leaders who oversaw ritual life.

succession.zh: 通常为父系家族内继承，但需获得长老与武士支持；强势部落中常由最有威望的成年男性出任盟主。
succession.en: Succession was usually patrilineal within chiefly families, but required acceptance by elders and warrior followers; in stronger tribes, the most prestigious adult male often emerged as confederate leader.

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家中枢，政治结构由众多山地聚落、谷地酋邦与季节性部族联盟构成。日常事务由村社长老处理，跨部事务由酋长会盟决定。
structure.en: There was no unified state center. The political structure consisted of many mountain settlements, valley chiefdoms, and seasonal tribal confederations. Daily affairs were handled by village elders, while intertribal matters were decided in assemblies of chiefs.

function.zh: 协调部族联盟、裁决争端、分配牧场与组织对外战争或议和。
function.en: Coordinated tribal alliances, judged disputes, allocated pasturelands, and organized warfare or peace with outsiders.

function.zh: 处理婚姻、继承、赔偿、血仇调停与地方习惯法执行。
function.en: Handled marriage, inheritance, compensation, blood-feud mediation, and the enforcement of local customary law.

function.zh: 征集谷物、牲畜、羊毛、金属与劳役，管理山谷仓储与祭祀分配。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, wool, metals, and labor dues, and managed valley storage facilities and ritual redistribution.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、占卜、祖先崇拜、盟誓仪式与季节性庆典。
function.en: Conducted rituals, divination, ancestor cults, oath ceremonies, and seasonal festivals.

function.zh: 在战时动员部众、传达酋长命令、护卫通道与看守山隘。
function.en: Mobilized tribesmen in wartime, carried chiefs' commands, guarded routes, and watched mountain passes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村落、谷地聚落与牧季营地为单位，由氏族长、村社长老和酋长代理人共同治理；其权力更依赖人身关系与习惯，而非固定文书制度。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages, valley settlements, and seasonal encampments, jointly governed by clan heads, village elders, and chiefly agents; authority depended more on personal ties and custom than on fixed documentation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以部族习惯法为主，重视赔偿、誓约、婚姻联盟与复仇调解；严重案件由酋长或长老会裁决，常以牲畜、金属或劳役作为赔付。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary tribal law, emphasizing compensation, oath-taking, marriage alliances, and feud settlement. Serious cases were judged by chiefs or councils of elders, often with payment in livestock, metal, or labor.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，主要通过贡赋、战利品分配、牲畜上缴、谷物贡献、矿产控制与集体劳役维系酋长权威。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform taxation system; chiefly authority was sustained through tribute, distribution of war booty, livestock dues, grain contributions, control of mineral resources, and collective labor obligations.

religion.zh: 宗教以自然神灵、山岳崇拜、祖先祭祀、火与太阳象征、泉水与风暴力量敬畏为核心，不同部族间信仰有较大差异，并吸收两河与埃兰边缘地区的宗教影响。
religion.en: Religion centered on nature spirits, mountain worship, ancestor rites, symbolic reverence for fire and the sun, and awe of springs and storm powers. Beliefs varied widely between tribes and also absorbed influences from Mesopotamia and the Elamite borderlands.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成文哲学体系，价值观强调氏族忠诚、祖先传统、勇武、盟誓信誉、牧场共享规则与对自然周期的顺应。
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical system existed; core values stressed clan loyalty, ancestral tradition, martial honor, trust in oaths, shared rules over pasture use, and adaptation to natural cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 多数高地社群尚未普遍使用文字，行政与记忆主要依赖口述传统、记忆者与象征性标记；靠近埃兰和两河贸易区的精英可能接触原始记账或外来文字体系。
writingSystem.en: Most highland communities did not widely use writing. Administration and memory relied chiefly on oral tradition, specialists of recollection, and symbolic marks; elites near Elamite and Mesopotamian trade zones may have encountered primitive accounting methods or foreign scripts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 高地石砌聚落、防御性山丘据点、青铜器与武器制作、织毯与羊毛纺织、陶器区域风格、驯养畜群管理以及跨山地季节性迁徙组织是其重要成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included stone-built upland settlements, defensive hilltop sites, bronze tool and weapon production, carpet and wool textile traditions, regional ceramic styles, effective herd management, and the organization of seasonal transhumant movement across mountain zones.

languageFamily.zh: 语言构成高度多样，可能包括前伊朗高原本地语言群，并与胡里安语、埃兰语及早期印欧语支边缘接触；整体上缺乏统一语言。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic landscape was highly diverse, likely including indigenous pre-Iranian Plateau languages, with contact zones involving Hurrian, Elamite, and early Indo-European speech communities; there was no single unified language.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农牧业为主，市场交换有限但并非缺失。财富主要体现在牲畜、谷物、羊毛、金属工具与家族劳动力上，而非统一货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was primarily based on subsistence agro-pastoralism, with limited but real exchange networks. Wealth was expressed mainly in livestock, grain, wool, metal tools, and family labor rather than in accumulation of a uniform currency.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、旱作农业、山地采矿、铜与锡贸易中转、青铜冶铸、陶器、羊毛纺织、皮革加工与驮运贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included herding, dry farming, upland mining, copper and tin transit trade, bronze metallurgy, pottery, wool textiles, leatherworking, and pack-animal transport trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 羊毛、牲畜、皮革、乳制品、谷物、木材、石材、半宝石、铜矿、成品青铜器及从东西方转运的稀有金属与奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included wool, livestock, hides, dairy products, grain, timber, stone, semi-precious stones, copper ore, finished bronze goods, and rare metals and prestige goods moving between east and west.

name.zh: 以物易物与金属重量交换

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要由羊群、山羊、少量牛马、谷仓储备、织物、金属工具与亲族关系网络构成；富裕酋长家庭则拥有更多牧场控制权、战利品与外来奢侈器物。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly composed of sheep flocks, goats, small numbers of cattle and equids, grain stores, textiles, metal tools, and kinship networks; richer chiefly households also controlled more pasture, war booty, and imported prestige goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、氏族共有资源、酋长再分配与边境贸易相结合的山地部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A mountain tribal economy combining household production, clan-held resources, chiefly redistribution, and frontier trade.

category.zh: 酋长再分配与盟友馈赠
category.en: Chiefly redistribution and gifts to allies

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，财富分散储存在各酋长家族的牲群、谷仓、金属贮藏和祭祀宝物中；少量贵金属主要用于馈赠、婚盟和跨区域贸易。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; wealth was dispersed across chiefly households in the form of herds, grain stores, metal stockpiles, and ritual valuables. Small amounts of precious metal were mainly used for gifts, marriage alliances, and long-distance exchange.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政侧重维持酋长威望与联盟稳定，通过定期再分配、危机储备和战时临时征集来平衡各部利益。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on sustaining chiefly prestige and alliance stability through periodic redistribution, emergency reserves, and temporary wartime collections that balanced the interests of different tribes.

description.zh: 由自由部民临时征集，使用长矛、短剑、木盾、投石索和弓箭，适合山地伏击与近程冲突。
description.en: Temporarily levied from free tribesmen, armed with spears, short blades, wooden shields, slings, and bows, well suited for mountain ambushes and close local fighting.

description.zh: 依托地形作战，负责骚扰、守隘与远程打击，在高地冲突中极具效率。
description.en: Fought from advantageous terrain and specialized in harassment, pass defense, and ranged attack, making them highly effective in upland warfare.

description.zh: 为酋长直属精锐，装备较好的青铜武器、皮甲或简易鳞片护具，承担护卫与突击任务。
description.en: The chiefs' direct elite force, equipped with better bronze weapons, leather armor, or simple scale protection, serving as guards and shock troops.

description.zh: 主要使用驴、骡或早期马匹进行侦察、传令与快速袭扰，并非成熟重型骑兵。
description.en: Used donkeys, mules, or early horses chiefly for scouting, messaging, and rapid raiding rather than as developed heavy cavalry.

commanderInChief.zh: 战时盟主酋长
commanderInChief.en: Wartime confederate chieftain

technology.zh: 军备处于青铜时代山地水平，拥有青铜矛头、斧、匕首、复合弓雏形或强化弓、皮革护具与石木防御工事，但缺乏大规模攻城技术和标准化军械体系。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a Bronze Age upland level, including bronze spearheads, axes, daggers, strengthened bows or proto-composite forms, leather protection, and stone-and-timber defenses, but lacking large-scale siege techniques and standardized arsenals.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间争牧场冲突、来自两河与埃兰边缘势力的征伐或掠夺、东北草原群体流动，以及贸易通道控制权竞争。
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal struggles over pasture, raids or expeditions from powers on the Mesopotamian and Elamite margins, movements of northeastern steppe groups, and competition over control of trade routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于扎格罗斯山地、山间谷地与伊朗高原内陆绿洲边缘，多数为小型村落居民与季节性迁徙牧民，人口密度低且区域差异显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across the Zagros highlands, intermontane valleys, and the margins of interior Iranian plateau oases. Most people lived in small villages or as seasonally mobile pastoralists, with low population density and strong regional variation.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致分为酋长与贵族氏族、祭司与仪式专家、自由战士、牧民与农民、工匠与商旅、依附民及少量奴役人口。
socialClasses.en: Social classes can be broadly divided into chiefly and noble lineages, priests and ritual specialists, free warriors, herders and farmers, artisans and traders, dependents, and a small enslaved population.

allies.zh: 无固定统一盟友，不同部族分别与埃兰边境势力、卡西特高地群体、札格罗斯邻近部落及部分东向商路伙伴保持临时联盟。
allies.en: There were no permanent unified allies; different tribes maintained temporary alliances with Elamite frontier powers, Kassite highland groups, neighboring Zagros tribes, and some eastern trade-route partners.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系同样不统一，常见对手包括争夺山隘与牧场的邻近部族，以及试图控制高地通道的巴比伦、亚述或其他低地势力。
enemies.en: Enemy relations were likewise not unified; common adversaries included neighboring tribes competing over passes and pasture, as well as lowland powers such as Babylonia, Assyria, or others seeking to control upland routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以务实生存为原则，重视婚姻结盟、互市、护送商队、共同防御和在强邻之间维持缓冲地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was guided by pragmatic survival, emphasizing marriage alliances, exchange, caravan protection, mutual defense, and maintenance of a buffer position between stronger neighbors.

keyInnovations.zh: 山地梯田与旱作适应技术、季节性转场放牧体系、铜矿开采与青铜铸造、羊毛纺织、石基泥砖建筑、山隘防御设施以及驮运网络组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mountain terrace and dry-farming adaptations, seasonal transhumant herding systems, copper extraction and bronze casting, wool textile production, stone-founded mudbrick architecture, pass-defense works, and organized pack-transport networks.

strengths.zh: 地形防御优势明显，部族社会韧性强，农牧结合适应多样生态，掌握山地矿产与东西交通节点，便于在大国夹缝中生存。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in defensive terrain, resilient tribal organization, mixed agro-pastoral adaptation to varied ecologies, access to upland mineral resources, and control of transit corridors between major civilizations.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂严重，缺乏统一征税、文字行政与常备军体系，内部血仇与部族竞争频繁，面对组织化低地王国时难以长期集中力量。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included severe political fragmentation, the absence of unified taxation, written administration, and a large standing army, as well as frequent feuds and tribal rivalry that made sustained central mobilization difficult against organized lowland kingdoms.

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后总体保持稳定，但未来将持续受到卡西特、埃兰、两河诸国及东部草原和高原迁徙群体影响；若贸易与军事压力上升，部分高地酋邦可能走向更强的联盟化与早期国家化。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE the region was broadly stable, but it would continue to be shaped by Kassite, Elamite, Mesopotamian, and eastern steppe or plateau movements. If trade and military pressures increased, some highland chiefdoms could evolve toward stronger confederations and early state formation.

description.zh: 札格罗斯与伊朗内陆山地散布着众多部族和小型山地政体。
description.en: The Zagros and interior Iranian highlands contained numerous tribes and small upland polities.

governmentForm.zh: 以港口宫廷为核心的世袭君主制贸易政体
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy centered on a port-palace trade polity

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与宫廷贵族、神庙祭司、书记与仓储管理者、远程商人、工匠、渔民与农民、依附劳工构成。岛屿聚落与沿海据点之间通过贸易、贡纳与宗教权威相连接。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal household and court elites, temple priests, scribes and storehouse managers, long-distance merchants, artisans, fishers and farmers, and dependent laborers. Island settlements and coastal outposts were linked through trade, tribute, and religious authority.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、港口贵族、大神庙祭司及控制仓储与海运的商人精英
rulingClass.en: The royal family, port aristocracy, leading temple priests, and merchant elites controlling storage and shipping

succession.zh: 大体为父系世袭，王位通常在统治家系内部传承，并受宫廷贵族与宗教集团支持确认
succession.en: Broadly patrilineal hereditary succession, with the throne normally passing within the ruling line and being affirmed by court elites and religious authorities

structure.zh: 政权规模不大但组织化程度较高，以王宫、港口仓库与神庙为行政核心。统治者直接控制首都周边，并通过地方首领、港务监督和税吏管理岛上村落、淡水泉区、棕榈园及沿海转运点。
structure.en: The state was modest in scale but relatively organized, with the palace, port storehouses, and temples as administrative centers. The ruler directly controlled the capital zone and governed island villages, freshwater spring areas, palm groves, and coastal transshipment points through local chiefs, harbor supervisors, and tax collectors.

function.zh: 管理王室财产、宫廷侍从、接待使节及总协调政务
function.en: Managed royal property, court attendants, diplomatic receptions, and overall state coordination

function.zh: 监督船只停泊、装卸、航道安全、商旅登记与转运安排
function.en: Supervised docking, loading and unloading, route security, merchant registration, and transshipment arrangements

function.zh: 征收实物税、保管铜锭、粮食、椰枣、羊毛及进口奢侈品
function.en: Collected taxes in kind and stored copper ingots, grain, dates, wool, and imported luxury goods

function.zh: 管理祭祀、神庙地产、宗教节庆与与王权相关的仪式合法性
function.en: Managed rituals, temple lands, religious festivals, and the ritual legitimacy associated with kingship

function.zh: 维护泉水、井渠、棕榈园与小规模灌溉资源，保障岛上生计基础
function.en: Maintained springs, wells, channels, palm groves, and small-scale irrigation resources sustaining island life

function.zh: 负责记录收支、封泥印记、货单、外交往来及贸易契约
function.en: Handled accounts, sealings, cargo lists, diplomatic correspondence, and trade agreements

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖村社首领、港口监督、泉区管理者与王室代理人。王权对首都及主要港口控制较强，对外围海岸和贸易节点则更多通过盟约、贡纳与商业利益维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village headmen, harbor overseers, spring managers, and royal agents. Royal control was strongest in the capital and major ports, while outlying coasts and trade nodes were held more through agreements, tribute, and commercial interests.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以习惯法、王令、商贸惯例及宣誓作证为基础。海运纠纷、货损、债务、仓储责任与人身赔偿可能由宫廷或神庙主持裁断，并以印章和见证人确认。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, royal decrees, mercantile practice, and oath-taking. Maritime disputes, cargo loss, debt, warehouse liability, and personal compensation were likely judged by palace or temple authorities and confirmed by seals and witnesses.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖港口关税、过境税、停泊费、实物贡纳和王室对贸易利润的抽成。税收多以铜、粮食、椰枣、牲畜、贝货与进口高值物资形式征收。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue depended chiefly on harbor customs, transit dues, anchorage fees, in-kind tribute, and royal shares of trade profits. Taxes were often collected in copper, grain, dates, livestock, shell valuables, and imported high-value goods.

religion.zh: 宗教为多神崇拜，兼具本地海湾神祇与两河传统影响。迪尔蒙在美索不达米亚观念中常与纯净、泉水与神圣之地相关，本地祭祀强调泉水、丰产、航海平安与祖先纪念。
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, combining local Gulf deities with Mesopotamian influence. In Mesopotamian thought, Dilmun was often associated with purity, fresh water, and sacred space; local worship emphasized springs, fertility, safe navigation, and ancestral commemoration.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学传统，世界观主要体现为王权神授、贸易诚信、家族荣誉、祭祀秩序与海陆交换带来的实用知识。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition; worldview was expressed through divine kingship, mercantile trust, family honor, ritual order, and practical knowledge derived from maritime and overland exchange.

writingSystem.zh: 行政记录主要借用楔形文字传统，并辅以本地印章体系。与两河世界往来时可能使用阿卡德语书写文书，地方管理中则高度依赖口述、标记与封泥。
writingSystem.en: Administrative recording mainly drew upon the cuneiform tradition alongside local sealing systems. Written documents in contact with Mesopotamia may have used Akkadian, while local administration relied heavily on oral communication, markings, and sealings.

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就包括成熟的海湾中转贸易网络、标准化仓储与封印管理、石制印章和器物制作、广泛的墓冢传统、海运航线知识，以及在岛屿有限环境中发展出的泉水—棕榈园经济。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included a mature Gulf transshipment network, standardized storage and sealing practices, stone seal and vessel production, extensive burial mound traditions, maritime route knowledge, and a spring-and-palm-grove economy adapted to a limited island environment.

languageFamily.zh: 本地语言未被确切记录，可能属于阿拉伯东部古代闪米特或混合海湾语言环境；官方对外书写多受阿卡德语影响
languageFamily.en: The local spoken language is not securely attested and may have belonged to an eastern Arabian Semitic or mixed Gulf linguistic environment; official external writing was strongly influenced by Akkadian

gdpDescription.zh: 该经济体规模不大，但因控制海湾贸易枢纽而拥有高于单纯农业社会的交换密度。财富集中于港口、仓储、税收与中介贸易，整体产出来自农业、渔业、手工业和转口贸易的结合。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small in absolute size but had an exchange density higher than that of a purely agrarian society because it controlled a Gulf trade hub. Wealth concentrated in ports, storage, taxation, and brokerage trade, while total output combined agriculture, fishing, craft production, and re-export commerce.

mainIndustries.zh: 海运与转口贸易、铜贸易与再分配、椰枣种植、渔业、制陶、石器与印章制作、船只维护、羊毛与纺织中转
mainIndustries.en: Seaborne and re-export trade, copper trade and redistribution, date cultivation, fishing, pottery, stone vessel and seal production, ship maintenance, and wool and textile transshipment

tradeGoods.zh: 主要贸易品包括阿曼铜、两河毛织物与谷物、海湾珍珠与贝壳、椰枣、沥青、木材、象牙、半宝石、可能来自印度河地区的珠饰与工艺品，以及少量贵金属与奢侈器物。
tradeGoods.en: Major trade goods included Omani copper, Mesopotamian wool textiles and grain, Gulf pearls and shells, dates, bitumen, timber, ivory, semi-precious stones, beadwork and crafted goods possibly from the Indus sphere, and limited quantities of precious metals and luxury objects.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富多体现在棕榈树、羊只、渔具、储粮、陶器与房屋；富裕家庭和商人则拥有印章、铜锭、船只股份、进口奢侈品及更大的仓储能力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in palm trees, sheep, fishing gear, stored grain, pottery, and houses; wealthy families and merchants held seals, copper ingots, shares in ships, imported luxuries, and larger storage capacity.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室与神庙监管的港口再分配经济为核心，辅以商人经营、家族网络和契约贸易；农业与渔业提供基础生计，国际贸易提供财富增量。
economicSystem.en: The economy centered on a port redistribution system supervised by the palace and temples, supported by merchant enterprise, kinship networks, and contract trade; agriculture and fishing provided subsistence, while international commerce generated surplus wealth.

category.zh: 港口、仓库与淡水设施维护
category.en: Maintenance of harbors, warehouses, and freshwater works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以银、铜锭、粮食储备、椰枣、羊毛织物和高价值进口品混合构成。真正可立即动用的贵金属储备有限，但仓储物资与贸易信用构成了政权财力的重要部分。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of a mix of silver, copper ingots, grain reserves, dates, wool textiles, and high-value imports. Immediately liquid precious-metal reserves were limited, but stored commodities and trade credit formed an important part of state fiscal strength.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持港口畅通、保障商路安全、稳定贡纳和积累应急储备。对贸易活动通常采取鼓励而非过度盘剥的策略，以保持迪尔蒙作为中转港的吸引力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized keeping ports functional, securing trade routes, stabilizing tribute flows, and building emergency reserves. Trade was generally encouraged rather than heavily burdened so as to preserve Dilmun’s attractiveness as a transshipment hub.

description.zh: 驻守王宫、仓库和主要宗教设施的精锐守卫，负责君主安全与首都秩序
description.en: Elite guards stationed at the palace, warehouses, and major cult sites, responsible for royal security and order in the capital

description.zh: 装备矛、盾和短兵器，负责港区巡逻、仓储守备、商路节点防御和登船检查
description.en: Equipped with spears, shields, and short weapons, they patrolled port districts, guarded storehouses, defended trade nodes, and inspected ships

description.zh: 由小型船只和熟悉海湾航道的水手组成，用于近海巡逻、护送商船与反制海盗袭扰
description.en: Composed of small craft and sailors familiar with Gulf routes, used for coastal patrol, merchant escort, and countering pirate raids

description.zh: 由村社和沿海据点临时征发，用于危机时期守卫泉水、棕榈园和外围聚落
description.en: Temporarily mobilized from villages and coastal outposts to guard springs, palm groves, and outer settlements in times of danger

commanderInChief.zh: 迪尔蒙之王
commanderInChief.en: King of Dilmun

technology.zh: 军事技术属青铜时代海湾水平，以青铜矛头、斧、匕首、木盾和弓箭为主，防御工事以城墙、塔楼、港口防守点和仓库警备为核心，缺乏大规模战车军传统。
technology.en: Military technology reflected Bronze Age Gulf conditions, relying on bronze spearheads, axes, daggers, wooden shields, and bows. Defensive works centered on walls, towers, harbor strongpoints, and warehouse security, with no major chariot tradition.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括海盗与海上劫掠、阿曼和阿拉伯沿岸竞争据点、贸易线路中断、两河强权对海湾贸易的间接压力，以及淡水资源遭袭导致的生计危机。
threats.en: Main threats included piracy and maritime raiding, rival Omani and Arabian coastal centers, disruption of trade routes, indirect pressure from Mesopotamian great powers over Gulf commerce, and subsistence crises caused by attacks on freshwater resources.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于巴林主岛及邻近海岸据点，密度受淡水泉和可耕地限制。人口结构中港口居民、农园经营者与渔民比例较高，并有相当数量外来商旅和季节性航海人员。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly on Bahrain Island and nearby coastal stations, with density limited by freshwater springs and cultivable land. The demographic profile featured many port dwellers, orchard cultivators, and fishers, along with a notable number of foreign merchants and seasonal seafarers.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致分为王室与贵族、祭司与书记、商人船主、工匠、农民与渔民、依附劳工及外来仆役。不同阶层之间流动有限，但成功商人可积累显著财富并进入政治核心。
socialClasses.en: Social classes were broadly divided into the royal family and nobility, priests and scribes, merchants and shipowners, artisans, farmers and fishers, dependent laborers, and foreign servants. Mobility between strata was limited, but successful merchants could accumulate substantial wealth and enter the political core.

allies.zh: 迪尔蒙通常不以正式军事同盟见长，而是通过与巴比伦尼亚城邦、卡西特势力、阿曼铜产区和海湾沿岸首领建立互惠商贸关系来维持安全。
allies.en: Dilmun was not known for formal military alliances; instead it maintained security through reciprocal commercial relations with Babylonia, Kassite powers, Omani copper-producing regions, and Gulf coastal chiefs.

enemies.zh: 并无长期固定的头号宿敌，但常受海盗集团、敌对海岸部族、争夺铜源与转口利润的海湾竞争者威胁；若两河政治紧张升级，也可能遭受经济胁迫。
enemies.en: It had no single permanent arch-enemy, but faced threats from pirate groups, hostile coastal tribes, and Gulf rivals competing for copper sources and re-export profits; intensified Mesopotamian political tensions could also bring economic coercion.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以贸易中立、航道安全、向多方开放港口和避免卷入大国陆上战争为核心。迪尔蒙通过馈赠、婚姻联系、使节往来与提供中转服务来维系区域影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on commercial neutrality, route security, open ports for multiple partners, and avoiding entanglement in great-power land wars. Dilmun maintained regional influence through gifts, marriage ties, diplomatic exchanges, and the provision of transshipment services.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括海湾航海与季风时序知识、铜锭流通与称量制度、封泥印章管理、仓储与再分配体系、泉水利用与园圃灌溉、船只修造，以及适应长距离贸易的标准化包装与记账实践。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included Gulf navigation and seasonal sailing knowledge, copper-ingot circulation and weighing systems, sealing administration, warehousing and redistribution, spring use and orchard irrigation, boat construction and repair, and standardized packing and accounting practices suited to long-distance trade.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于地理位置优越，能够连接美索不达米亚、阿曼与更远的印度洋世界；其港口、仓储和商人网络使小人口社会也能积累可观财富与外交空间。
strengths.en: Its greatest strength was its strategic location linking Mesopotamia, Oman, and the wider Indian Ocean world; its ports, warehouses, and merchant networks allowed a small population to accumulate notable wealth and diplomatic room for maneuver.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于人口和军力有限、农业基础狭窄、对外部贸易依赖极高，且淡水、航道安全和区域政治稳定任何一项受损都可能迅速冲击国家运作。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in limited population and military power, a narrow agricultural base, and heavy dependence on external trade; damage to freshwater access, route security, or regional political stability could quickly disrupt the state.

outlook.zh: 公元前1600年前后，迪尔蒙总体保持稳定，只要海湾贸易持续、铜供应不断、且不被两河或阿拉伯沿岸强力政权直接吞并，其作为区域中转中心的地位仍可延续；但长期前景高度依赖贸易路线变动。
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE, Dilmun was broadly stable. So long as Gulf trade continued, copper supplies remained available, and it was not directly absorbed by stronger Mesopotamian or Arabian coastal powers, its position as a regional entrepôt could endure; its long-term outlook, however, depended heavily on shifting trade routes.

description.zh: 波斯湾岛屿与海岸贸易中心，联通美索不达米亚、阿曼与印度河世界。
description.en: A Gulf island and coastal trade center linking Mesopotamia, Oman, and the Indus world.

name.zh: 阿拉伯青铜时代部落文化

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族为基础的部落联盟与酋邦网络，权力分散，由部落长老、战首与祭仪领袖共同协商
governmentForm.en: A kin-based network of tribes and small chiefdoms with decentralized authority, governed through consultation among tribal elders, war leaders, and ritual authorities

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与附属群体为核心，分为酋长家族、自由部落成员、盟附者、工匠与少量奴仆；定居绿洲居民与游牧群体并存
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on clans, extended families, and dependent groups, divided among chieftainly families, free tribespeople, client groups, artisans, and a small number of slaves; oasis dwellers and mobile pastoralists coexisted

rulingClass.zh: 部落酋长家族、长老会议成员、主要战首以及掌握神圣祭仪的显贵家族
rulingClass.en: Chieftainly lineages, members of councils of elders, leading war chiefs, and prominent families controlling sacred ritual functions

succession.zh: 多为家族内部继承，但通常须经长老与重要支系认可；在松散联盟中，强势首领的地位依赖个人威望、财富分配与军事能力
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within leading lineages but normally required recognition by elders and major branches; in loose confederations, a strong leader's position depended on personal prestige, gift distribution, and military ability

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家官僚体系，而是由若干部落、绿洲聚落与季节性商路节点构成的分散治理网络；重大事务通过部落会议、盟约与婚姻关系协调
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; instead, governance operated through a dispersed network of tribes, oasis settlements, and seasonal trade nodes, with major matters coordinated by tribal assemblies, alliances, and marriage ties

function.zh: 裁决争端、确认继承、协调迁徙路线与部落盟约
function.en: Resolved disputes, confirmed succession, and coordinated migration routes and tribal alliances

function.zh: 安排放牧、井泉使用、季节迁徙与牲畜分配
function.en: Oversaw grazing, use of wells and springs, seasonal migration, and livestock allocation

function.zh: 组织商队、征收通行贡礼、保障贸易路线安全并处理对外交涉
function.en: Organized caravans, collected passage tribute, secured trade routes, and handled external dealings

function.zh: 主持部落祭祀、见证盟誓、保管神圣场所传统
function.en: Conducted tribal rites, witnessed oaths, and maintained traditions at sacred places

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依氏族营地、绿洲村落和季节性集市分层进行，由家族长、井泉看护者与商路首领共同管理
localAdmin.en: Local administration was conducted through clan camps, oasis villages, and seasonal markets, managed jointly by household heads, well custodians, and caravan leaders

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、血亲赔偿、誓约与调解为核心；谋杀、偷盗牲畜、破坏井泉与违背盟誓被视为重罪，通常以赔偿、逐出或报复解决
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, kin compensation, oath-taking, and mediation; murder, livestock theft, damage to wells, and oath-breaking were grave offenses, usually settled through compensation, expulsion, or feud

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一定额税制，主要以酋长贡纳、牲畜份额、商队通行费、绿洲收成分成及战争战利品分配构成
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized tax system; revenue came mainly from tribute to chieftains, shares of livestock, caravan tolls, portions of oasis harvests, and distribution of war booty

religion.zh: 以多神崇拜、祖先敬奉、圣石与圣所祭仪为主，常与井泉、山地、天象和部落守护神相关；不同地区存在显著差异
religion.en: Religion centered on polytheistic worship, ancestral veneration, and rituals at sacred stones and sanctuaries, often tied to wells, high places, celestial phenomena, and tribal patron deities; practices varied greatly by region

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学，核心观念强调血缘忠诚、誓约信用、慷慨待客、复仇义务与在严酷环境中维持群体荣誉
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophy; core values emphasized kin loyalty, trust in oaths, generosity to guests, obligations of vengeance, and preservation of group honor in a harsh environment

writingSystem.zh: 整体上以口述传统为主，绝大多数部落无日常书写行政；在西北与边缘商贸接触区可能借用邻近文明的楔形文字或原始字母性记号进行有限记录
writingSystem.en: The culture was predominantly oral, and most tribes lacked routine written administration; in the northwest and other contact zones, neighboring cuneiform systems or early alphabetic marks may have been borrowed for limited recording

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长沙漠导航、井泉与绿洲利用、长途驼前驯化阶段的驴队贸易、歌谣与口头谱系记忆、石构墓葬传统以及区域间物资转运
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included desert navigation, management of wells and oases, long-distance donkey caravan trade before full camel dominance, oral poetry and genealogical memory, stone-built funerary traditions, and regional transfer of goods

languageFamily.zh: 以古代闪米特语族的阿拉伯半岛方言连续体为主，南部、北部与东部方言差异较大，并夹杂非闪米特基层成分
languageFamily.en: The main language base was an ancient Semitic dialect continuum of the Arabian Peninsula, with considerable variation between southern, northern, and eastern speech zones, alongside non-Semitic substrate elements

unit.en: annual grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量较低但适应性强，主要依赖畜牧、绿洲农业、采集与中转贸易。财富高度分散，牲畜与关系网络比贵金属更重要。
gdpDescription.en: Overall output was low but highly adaptive, relying mainly on pastoralism, oasis farming, gathering, and transit trade. Wealth was widely dispersed, and livestock and relationship networks mattered more than precious metals.

mainIndustries.zh: 游牧畜牧业、绿洲农业、盐与沥青采集、皮革与毛织品制作、铜矿及石材小规模开采、商旅护送与转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Nomadic pastoralism, oasis agriculture, collection of salt and bitumen, leatherwork and wool textiles, small-scale copper and stone extraction, caravan escorting, and transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 羊毛、皮革、牲畜、乳制品、枣类、盐、沥青、铜料、贝壳、香料性植物与从阿曼、海湾、黎凡特和美索不达米亚转运的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Wool, leather, livestock, dairy products, dates, salt, bitumen, copper materials, shells, aromatic plants, and luxury goods transshipped from Oman, the Gulf, the Levant, and Mesopotamia

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以羊群、山羊、驴、帐幕、武器、水权与亲族盟友体现；绿洲家庭则另有小块耕地和果树
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in flocks of sheep and goats, donkeys, tents, weapons, water rights, and kin allies; oasis households also held small plots and orchards

unit.en: annual grams of silver equivalent per worker

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭和部落为基础的混合经济，结合自给性牧业、绿洲种植、礼物交换、贡纳关系和有限市场交换
economicSystem.en: A household- and tribe-based mixed economy combining subsistence pastoralism, oasis cultivation, gift exchange, tributary relations, and limited market exchange

source.zh: 商队通行费与护送收入

category.en: Maintenance of wells, camps, and trade routes

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在集中式国库，财富分散保存在酋长家族、神圣场所、牲畜群与贸易存货中；所谓“财政储备”更多是可动员的牲畜、金属与盟友义务
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized state treasury; wealth was dispersed among chieftainly families, sacred places, livestock herds, and trade stocks. What counted as a treasury was really mobilizable livestock, metal, and allied obligations

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政强调平衡馈赠与威望，避免对部落成员征收过重负担；在旱灾或冲突期则通过提高商路收费、征集牲畜和临时劫掠弥补缺口
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized balancing gift distribution and prestige while avoiding excessive burdens on tribespeople; during drought or conflict, deficits were met through higher route charges, livestock levies, and temporary raiding

description.zh: 以持矛、短剑、木盾及弓箭的部落战士为主，适合伏击、守井泉和近距离突袭
description.en: Primarily tribal warriors armed with spears, daggers, wooden shields, and bows, suited for ambushes, well defense, and close-range raids

description.zh: 依靠驴运输补给与快速移动，不是成熟骑兵，但能显著提高沙漠远程机动能力
description.en: Using donkeys for transport and rapid movement, these were not true cavalry but significantly improved long-range desert mobility

description.zh: 守卫定居点、仓储、井泉和商道，兼具警戒与作战功能
description.en: Guarded settlements, storage areas, wells, and trade paths, serving both security and combat roles

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各大部落战首在盟会中推举主导者
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; leading war chiefs of major tribes chose a dominant leader in council when needed

technology.zh: 使用青铜与石制武器并存，配有复合程度有限的弓、长矛、短剑、皮革护具和简易盾牌；擅长依赖地形、缺水环境和情报进行作战
technology.en: Weapons included both bronze and stone implements, with relatively simple bows, spears, daggers, leather protection, and basic shields; warfare relied heavily on terrain, water scarcity, and intelligence

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自争夺水源和牧场的邻近部落、海湾与西北商路上的竞争者，以及在边缘地带与两河、黎凡特或埃及势力接触时可能遭遇的间接军事压力
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes competing for water and pasture, rivals on Gulf and northwestern trade routes, and indirect military pressure where Arabian groups encountered powers from Mesopotamia, the Levant, or Egypt

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于北阿拉伯草原、内陆荒漠边缘、海岸带与若干绿洲，季节性迁徙显著；多数人从事牧业，少数集中在绿洲和贸易节点
populationDescription.en: The population was thinly spread across the North Arabian steppe, inland desert margins, coastal zones, and scattered oases, with pronounced seasonal mobility; most people engaged in pastoralism, while a minority clustered in oases and trade nodes

name.zh: 迪勒蒙相关东阿拉伯聚落
达丹地区前期绿洲聚落
name.en: Early oasis settlements of the Dadan region

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族与长老、自由牧民、绿洲农民、依附者、工匠、商旅从属人员及少量奴仆构成主要阶层，社会流动多依附于婚姻、战功和牲畜积累
socialClasses.en: The main social layers were chieftainly families and elders, free pastoralists, oasis cultivators, dependents, artisans, caravan retainers, and a small number of slaves; social mobility often depended on marriage, war achievement, and accumulation of livestock

allies.zh: 无固定全国性盟友，通常与海湾贸易社群、阿曼铜产区、部分黎凡特商人和邻近绿洲部落保持灵活合作关系
allies.en: There were no fixed nationwide allies; tribes typically maintained flexible cooperative ties with Gulf trading communities, Omani copper zones, some Levantine merchants, and neighboring oasis groups

enemies.zh: 无持续统一宿敌，但与竞争水源、商道与放牧区的邻近部落时常发生冲突；部分边缘地区也会与定居国家的边防或代理势力发生摩擦
enemies.en: There was no constant unified enemy, but frequent conflict occurred with neighboring tribes competing for water, routes, and pasture; in frontier zones, friction also arose with border forces or proxies of settled states

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以实用主义为主，通过婚盟、护商、贡礼、互不侵犯协议和选择性劫掠维护生计与地位，避免与大型定居帝国正面长期战争
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic, using marriage alliances, caravan protection, gift exchange, non-aggression agreements, and selective raiding to preserve livelihood and status, while avoiding prolonged direct war with major settled powers

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括沙漠井泉利用、驴队长途运输、青铜工具与武器的有限采用、皮革和毛织帐幕制作、绿洲灌溉经验以及跨荒漠路线知识的世代传承
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included exploitation of desert wells, long-distance donkey transport, limited adoption of bronze tools and weapons, leather and wool tent making, oasis irrigation know-how, and intergenerational transmission of trans-desert route knowledge

strengths.zh: 优势在于高度机动、熟悉荒漠环境、能连接海湾、阿曼、黎凡特与两河贸易通道，并通过灵活联盟适应政治变化
strengths.en: Strengths included high mobility, deep knowledge of desert environments, ability to connect Gulf, Omani, Levantine, and Mesopotamian trade routes, and flexible alliances that adapted to political change

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一政权与稳定税基，人口分散，易受旱灾、水源危机和部落内斗影响，军事上难与大型国家持续对抗
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included the absence of a unified state and stable tax base, dispersed population, vulnerability to drought, water crises, and intertribal feuding, and limited capacity for sustained warfare against large states

outlook.zh: 在公元前1600年前后总体稳定，若区域贸易持续活跃，部落与绿洲网络将继续繁荣；但其未来发展仍高度取决于气候波动、商路转移以及周边文明对边缘地带的渗透
outlook.en: Around 1600 BCE the culture was generally stable; if regional trade remained active, tribal and oasis networks would continue to prosper. Its future, however, depended heavily on climatic shifts, changing trade routes, and encroachment by surrounding civilizations into frontier zones

description.zh: 阿拉伯半岛大部由游牧与绿洲部族控制，形成松散贸易与迁徙网络。
description.en: Most of Arabia was controlled by nomadic and oasis tribes forming loose networks of trade and migration.

capital.zh: 巴提奈海岸与内陆绿洲中心
capital.en: Batinah coast and inland oasis centers

governmentForm.zh: 以部族酋长为核心、由亲族网络和地方议事传统维系的分散型酋邦联合体
governmentForm.en: A decentralized confederation of chiefdoms centered on tribal leaders, sustained by kinship networks and local councils

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部族、氏族和家族为基本单位，上层为掌控矿山、灌溉和海贸的酋长家族，其下为自由农民、牧民、矿工、冶金工匠、水手与商旅，底层包括债役者、战俘和依附劳工
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around tribes, clans, and extended families. At the top stood chiefly lineages controlling mines, irrigation, and maritime trade; beneath them were free farmers, herders, miners, metallurgists, sailors, and merchants; the lower strata included debt dependents, captives, and attached laborers

rulingClass.zh: 掌握铜矿、港湾和绿洲水源的部族酋长家族及其随从武士
rulingClass.en: Chiefly lineages controlling copper mines, harbors, and oasis water sources, together with their retainer warriors

succession.zh: 多为父系家族内部继承，但需获得氏族长老与主要支系认可；若局势动荡，也可由最强支系首领取代
succession.en: Succession was usually patrilineal within chiefly families, but required recognition by clan elders and major lineage branches; in times of instability, the strongest branch leader could supplant rivals

structure.zh: 不存在统一官僚国家，而是由若干沿海与内陆酋邦组成。各酋长依靠家族亲信、长老会议、港口管理者、矿区监督者和祭仪主持者处理政务，重大事务通过部族协商与交换盟约决定
structure.en: There was no unified bureaucratic state; rather, multiple coastal and inland chiefdoms operated together. Each chief relied on family retainers, councils of elders, harbor overseers, mining supervisors, and ritual specialists, while major matters were settled through tribal consultation and exchange-based alliances

function.zh: 组织铜矿开采、木炭供应、矿石运输和冶炼分配，并监督向外交换的铜锭与半成品
function.en: Organized copper extraction, charcoal supply, ore transport, and smelting allocation, and supervised ingots and semi-finished metal for external exchange

function.zh: 管理停泊点、船队组织、贸易接待、贡品交换和海上航路信息
function.en: Managed anchorages, fleet organization, trade reception, tribute exchange, and maritime route information

function.zh: 协调井泉、渠道、棕榈园与谷地耕地的用水秩序及劳役安排
function.en: Coordinated wells, channels, palm groves, and wadi farmland through water allocation and labor scheduling

function.zh: 主持地方祭祀、祖先仪式、誓盟见证和神圣物资保管
function.en: Oversaw local cult rituals, ancestor ceremonies, oath witnessing, and the custody of sacred goods

function.zh: 维持酋长亲兵、调集部族战士、守卫矿区与通道，并应对劫掠和部族冲突
function.en: Maintained chiefly retainers, mobilized tribal warriors, guarded mines and routes, and responded to raiding and tribal conflict

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村落首领、绿洲管理者、港口长和氏族长老。沿海聚落、山地矿区与内陆绿洲各自保持高度自治，仅在贸易、战争和祭仪问题上接受更大酋邦协调
localAdmin.en: Local administration depended on village headmen, oasis managers, harbor heads, and clan elders. Coastal settlements, mountain mining zones, and inland oases retained high autonomy and only accepted broader chiefly coordination in trade, warfare, and ritual matters

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，以部族传统、血亲责任、赔偿原则和酋长裁断为核心。争端多通过长老调解、牲畜与金属赔偿、婚姻安排或宣誓解决，严重案件可引发逐出或复仇
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary, centered on tribal tradition, kin liability, compensation, and chiefly judgment. Disputes were usually settled by elder mediation, livestock and metal compensation, marriage arrangements, or oaths; severe cases could lead to expulsion or feud

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在标准化税制，酋长收入主要来自矿产分成、贸易抽取、灌溉地贡赋、战利品分配和节庆馈赠。劳役、物资上缴和航海服务比定额货币税更常见
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized tax system. Chiefs drew income mainly from shares of mining output, trade exactions, agricultural tribute from irrigated land, distributions of war booty, and ceremonial gifts. Labor service, deliveries in kind, and maritime service were more common than fixed monetary taxation

religion.zh: 宗教以祖先崇拜、地方神灵、山地与泉水圣所崇拜为主，并与航海、丰产和金属冶炼相关的祭仪紧密结合
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, local deities, and the cult of mountain and spring sanctuaries, closely tied to rituals of seafaring, fertility, and metallurgy

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系统哲学传统，核心观念是血缘忠诚、互惠交换、誓言神圣性、对水源与土地的共同责任，以及酋长以慷慨与勇武维持威望
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical tradition had emerged; core ideas emphasized kin loyalty, reciprocal exchange, the sacredness of oaths, communal responsibility for water and land, and chiefly prestige maintained through generosity and valor

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本地成熟文字系统，行政与贸易记录主要依赖口头记忆、印记、器物标志和外来接触中偶见的简单记号
writingSystem.en: There was no mature local writing system; administration and trade records relied mainly on oral memory, marks, vessel symbols, and occasional simple notations in contexts of foreign contact

culturalAchievements.zh: 其重要成就在于大规模铜矿开采与冶炼、石砌墓葬传统、海湾航海网络、绿洲农业管理以及将山地、海岸与内陆经济连结为统一交换体系
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included large-scale copper mining and smelting, stone-built funerary traditions, Gulf seafaring networks, oasis agricultural management, and the integration of mountain, coastal, and inland economies into a coherent exchange system

languageFamily.zh: 前阿拉伯半岛语言群，可能属于早期闪米特化或相关的地方语言连续体
languageFamily.en: Pre-Arabian Peninsula language groups, possibly within an early Semiticizing or related local linguistic continuum

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在阿拉伯东南部属较高水平，但远低于两河或埃及等王国。其财富集中于铜矿、区域中转贸易、椰枣与谷物农业、渔业及牧业，多数产出仍用于维持地方共同体而非市场交换
gdpDescription.en: The economy was relatively advanced for southeastern Arabia, though far below the great kingdoms of Mesopotamia or Egypt. Wealth centered on copper mining, regional transit trade, date and grain agriculture, fishing, and pastoralism, while most output still sustained local communities rather than formal markets

mainIndustries.zh: 铜矿开采、铜冶炼、海上运输、椰枣与小麦大麦种植、山羊绵羊牧养、渔业、石器与陶器制作
mainIndustries.en: Copper mining, copper smelting, maritime transport, cultivation of dates and wheat and barley, goat and sheep herding, fishing, and stone and pottery production

tradeGoods.zh: 主要输出铜锭、铜器半成品、椰枣、鱼干、贝壳与石料；输入木材、奢侈陶器、谷物补给、纺织品、沥青、珠饰和少量锡
tradeGoods.en: Main exports included copper ingots, semi-finished copper products, dates, dried fish, shells, and stone; imports included timber, fine pottery, grain supplements, textiles, bitumen, beads, and small amounts of tin

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异明显。普通家庭以椰枣园、牲畜、渔具和陶器为主，富裕酋长家族则积累铜料、船只、劳力网络和外来奢侈品
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was unevenly distributed. Ordinary families possessed date groves, livestock, fishing gear, and pottery, while wealthy chiefly households accumulated copper stocks, boats, labor networks, and imported prestige goods

economicSystem.zh: 经济属于部族再分配与交换混合体系。矿产、港口和水资源由酋长与氏族控制，日常生产以家庭为单位，跨区域贸易则依靠酋长主导的馈赠、盟约和互市
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mixed tribal redistributive and exchange system. Minerals, harbors, and water resources were controlled by chiefs and clans; everyday production was household-based, while long-distance trade depended on chiefly directed gifting, alliance, and barter

source.en: Shares from copper mining and smelting

source.zh: 港口贸易抽取与通行贡赋

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中于单一都城，而分散保存在酋长家宅、圣所仓储、铜锭库存和牲畜群中。可迅速动员的财富主要是铜料、粮食、椰枣和礼品性奢侈物
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not concentrated in a single capital but dispersed among chiefly residences, shrine storerooms, copper stocks, and herds. The most readily mobilized wealth consisted of copper, grain, dates, and prestige goods

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持酋长威望和联盟稳定为目标，强调从矿业与海贸中抽取资源，再通过馈赠、祭礼和防务支出来巩固追随关系
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to preserve chiefly prestige and alliance stability, extracting resources from mining and maritime trade and then reinforcing loyalty through redistribution, ritual, and defense spending

description.zh: 由酋长直属武士、护卫和精干战士组成，负责保护酋长、港口、矿区和重要仓储
